quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 9 of CAMA 2020, the Registrar-General must be a legal practitioner qualified for at least 10 years and must have at least 8 years of corporate law experience. [4]

quizzes.scenario: XYZ Ltd intends to sue the Corporate Affairs Commission for a perceived wrong. The company's solicitor files the suit today without prior communication with the CAC. What is the status of this suit?
options: The suit is validly filed
The suit is incompetent for failure to give 30 days' pre-action notice
The suit should be filed at the State High Court
The suit requires the Attorney General's consent
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 17(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that no suit shall be commenced against the Commission until the expiration of 30 days after a written notice of intention to sue has been served. [4, 5]

quizzes.scenario: A dispute arises regarding the removal of a director in a private company. The aggrieved director wants to file an action. Which court has exclusive jurisdiction?
options: State High Court
National Industrial Court
Federal High Court
Investment and Securities Tribunal
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 251(1)(e) of the 1999 Constitution and Section 851(12) of CAMA 2020, the Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction over matters arising from the operation of CAMA. [6-8]

quizzes.scenario: A public company, ABC Plc, wants to offer its shares to the general public for the first time. Which agency is the apex regulator for this capital market operation?
options: Corporate Affairs Commission
Central Bank of Nigeria
Securities and Exchange Commission
Nigerian Exchange Limited
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 1 of the Investment and Securities Act (ISA) establishes the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) as the apex body overseeing capital market operations and the public offer of securities. [9-11]

quizzes.scenario: GlobalTech Inc., a US-based company, wants to open a branch in Nigeria to process cassava. Before they can legally begin operations as a foreign investor, they must register with which body?
options: Nigerian Export Promotion Council
Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission
National Office for Technology Acquisition and Promotion
Nigerian Immigration Service
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 20 of the NIPC Act, foreign companies intending to operate in Nigeria must register with the Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission (NIPC). [12-15]

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian company, TechHub Ltd, enters into a contract with a German firm to import a specialized software engine. Within what timeframe must they register this technology transfer contract?
options: 30 days
60 days
90 days
15 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: By Section 5(2) of the NOTAP Act, any contract for the transfer of foreign technology must be registered with the National Office for Technology Acquisition and Promotion (NOTAP) within 60 days of execution. [16-18]

quizzes.scenario: SecureGuard Ltd is a new company providing private security guards. Apart from CAMA, which specific Act must they comply with for licensing?
options: Police Act
Private Guard Companies Act
Labour Act
Criminal Code Act
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Private security firms are regulated by the Private Guard Companies Act in addition to general corporate regulations under CAMA. [19, 20]

quizzes.scenario: A pharmaceutical company, MedChem Ltd, wants to distribute a new cough syrup in Nigeria. Which agency's approval is mandatory for the safety and quality control of this product?
options: SON
FCCPC
NAFDAC
NIS
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) is responsible for regulating the manufacture, distribution, and sale of drugs and food under the NAFDAC Act. [21-23]

quizzes.scenario: PowerSource Ltd wants to spend ?750,000 on new heavy machinery for its factory. Which division must they notify regarding this capital expenditure?
options: Federal Inland Revenue Service
Industrial Inspectorate Division
Corporate Affairs Commission
Securities and Exchange Commission
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Industrial Inspectorate Act, a company planning a capital expenditure of over ?500,000 must notify the Industrial Inspectorate Division of the Federal Ministry of Trade and Investment. [24-26]

quizzes.scenario: SuperMart Ltd and GroceryHub Ltd plan to merge. Which body is the primary regulator responsible for ensuring the merger does not create a monopoly?
options: SEC
CAC
FCCPC
CBN
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Act 2018, the FCCPC is the primary regulator for reviewing and approving mergers to prevent anti-competitive practices. [6, 17, 27]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign national, Hans, is hired as an IT expert by a Nigerian firm. Which agency grants the "Expatriate Quota" required for him to work in Nigeria?
options: NIPC
NOTAP
Nigerian Immigration Service
Ministry of Foreign Affairs
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Immigration Service (NIS) manages the control of foreign nationals and grants expatriate quotas and work permits under the Immigration Act. [18, 23, 28]

quizzes.scenario: ABC Bank has a large portfolio of non-performing loans. Which corporation was established to purchase these toxic assets to stabilize the banking sector?
options: NDIC
CBN
AMCON
FIRS
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Asset Management Corporation of Nigeria (AMCON) was established to manage liquidity in banks by disposing of non-performing loans and toxic assets. [23, 29, 30]

quizzes.scenario: A company needs to stamp its Memorandum and Articles of Association during incorporation. Which agency is responsible for this?
options: CAC
Federal High Court
Federal Inland Revenue Service
Ministry of Justice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) is responsible for the collection of stamp duties and the stamping of incorporation documents. [18, 23]

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer called to the bar 2 years ago wants to incorporate a company for his client. He is not a director or shareholder of the proposed company. Can he file the documents?
options: Yes, as a legal practitioner
No, he must be accredited with the CAC first
Yes, if he has a seal
No, only the client can file
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Accreditation with the CAC is required for professionals (Legal Practitioners, Accountants, Secretaries) to provide incorporation services unless they are first shareholders or directors. [31-33]

quizzes.scenario: An accountant wants to act as a consultant for a public company's share issuance. He has 3 years of experience. Does he meet the SEC registration requirements?
options: Yes, he is an accountant
No, he needs 5 years post-call/qualification experience
No, he needs 10 years experience
Yes, if he passes the SEC test
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: SEC registration for individuals requires at least five years post-call/qualification experience and 4 years of corporate practice experience. [34, 35]

quizzes.scenario: A company's name is approved by the CAC, but it is later discovered the approval was obtained via fraudulent documents. What can the CAC do?
options: Nothing, the certificate is conclusive
Sue the company in court
Withdraw or cancel the approval of the name
Fine the directors only
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 31(5) of CAMA 2020 empowers the Commission to withdraw or cancel a name reservation if discovered it was fraudulently or improperly procured. [36-38]

quizzes.scenario: An investor in the capital market has a dispute with his stockbroker regarding the unauthorized sale of shares. Which specialized body has jurisdiction?
options: Federal High Court
Investment and Securities Tribunal
Administrative Proceedings Committee of CAC
State High Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Investment and Securities Tribunal (IST) handles capital market disputes as established under the Investment and Securities Act. [39-41]

quizzes.scenario: A telecom company, Telco Nig Ltd, wishes to renew its operating license. Which specific sector regulator handles this?
options: NCC
NBC
SON
FCCPC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) is the regulatory body for the telecommunications sector under the NCC Act. [42, 43]

quizzes.scenario: An electricity distribution company, PowerGrid Ltd, is accused of unfair pricing. Which body regulates pricing and consumer protection in the power sector?
options: NERC
NCC
FCCPC
SON
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Electricity Regulatory Commission (NERC) regulates the electricity sector, including pricing and service delivery under the Electricity Act. [21, 43]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign investor wants to ensure his investment is protected against nationalization. Which Act provides this guarantee?
options: CAMA 2020
ISA 2025
NIPC Act
Foreign Exchange Act
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 25 of the NIPC Act protects foreign investments from asset nationalization or expropriation, ensuring guaranteed compensation. [44, 45]

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian entrepreneur wants to export cocoa to Europe. Which council provides incentives and promotes export trade?
options: NIPC
NEPC
SON
NAFDAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Export Promotion Council (NEPC) is responsible for promoting and developing Nigeria's export trade and providing incentives. [46, 47]

quizzes.scenario: A company produces electric cables and needs to ensure they meet the minimum national industrial standards. Which body issues the mandatory certification?
options: NAFDAC
SON
FCCPC
NOTAP
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Standard Organization of Nigeria (SON) ensures that products manufactured in Nigeria meet specific quality standards under the SON Act. [6, 43]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company, Alpha Inc., is invited by the Federal Government to execute a specific power project in Delta State. Must they incorporate a local subsidiary?
options: Yes, Section 78 CAMA applies
No, they can apply for exemption under Section 80 CAMA
Yes, NIPC requires it
No, foreign companies are never registered
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 80(1)(a) of CAMA 2020 allows foreign companies invited by the Federal Government for specified projects to apply for exemption from local incorporation. [48, 49]

quizzes.scenario: The CAC refuses to register "CleanEnergy Ltd" because it conflicts with a trademark. The applicant disagrees. What is the applicant's first step?
options: Sue the CAC in the Supreme Court
Give notice to the Commission to apply to the court for directions
File an action in the State High Court
Ignore the CAC and start business
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 41(2) of CAMA 2020, if aggrieved by a refusal to register, the applicant gives notice to the Commission, which must then apply to the court for directions within 21 days. [50, 51]

quizzes.scenario: A company is registered with a name that is "suggestive of government patronage" without consent. Which internal CAC committee can adjudicate this dispute?
options: Audit Committee
Administrative Proceedings Committee
Remuneration Committee
Disciplinary Committee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Administrative Proceedings Committee (APC) of the CAC resolves non-criminal disputes, including those relating to prohibited or restricted names. [52-54]

quizzes.scenario: An exempted foreign company fails to deliver its "Annual Report" to the CAC for the 2024 calendar year. Which form should they have used?
options: CAC Form 19
CAC Form 5
CAC Form 22 (or 24 in some lists)
CAC Form 7
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Exempted foreign companies are required to deliver an Annual Report using the prescribed form (CAC Form 22/24) rather than the standard Annual Returns form. [55, 56]

quizzes.scenario: A company's registrar has failed to remit dividends to a shareholder. The shareholder wants to file a complaint. Which body handles such investment disputes?
options: Federal High Court
SEC Administrative Proceedings Committee
CAC Administrative Proceedings Committee
National Industrial Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The SEC Administrative Proceedings Committee (APC) is a quasi-judicial body that hears perceived contraventions of the ISA or SEC Rules by capital market operators. [40, 57]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign investor brings 1 million USD into Nigeria through an authorized dealer. What document must they obtain to facilitate future repatriation of funds?
options: Certificate of Incorporation
Certificate of Capital Importation
Expatriate Quota
NIPC Registration
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to the Foreign Exchange (Monitoring and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act, investors must import capital through authorized dealers and obtain a Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI). [58, 59]

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to verify if its competitor has registered a charge over its assets. What process should they initiate at the CAC?
options: Corporate Restructuring
Corporate Search
Winding Up
Annual Returns
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A corporate search (Manual or Electronic) at the CAC allows parties to confirm a company's details, including registered charges or encumbrances. [60, 61]

quizzes.scenario: A company in the "upstream" petroleum sector is being investigated for environmental non-compliance. Which PIA body regulates upstream operations?
options: NMDPRA
NUPRC
NNPC
DPR
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) regulates upstream petroleum operations like exploration and drilling under the PIA. [29]

quizzes.scenario: A director of the CAC Governing Board is representing the accounting profession. Who appoints this representative?
options: The President
The Minister of Trade
ICAN
The Registrar General
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Members of the CAC Governing Board, including the representative of the accounting profession, are appointed by the Minister. [62, 63]

quizzes.scenario: A dispute involving a collective investment scheme (CIS) arises between the fund manager and unit holders. Which tribunal has original jurisdiction?
options: IST
FHC
NIC
CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Investment and Securities Tribunal (IST) has jurisdiction over disputes arising from the administration and management of collective investment schemes. [57, 64]

quizzes.scenario: A legal practitioner is handling a take-over bid for a public company. Is he a "Capital Market Operator" or "Capital Market Consultant"?
options: Operator
Consultant
Both
Neither
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Professionals like legal practitioners and accountants serving the capital market are generally classified as Capital Market Consultants under SEC rules. [65, 66]

quizzes.scenario: A company secretary of a public company must be qualified. Which of the following is NOT a valid qualification?
options: Legal Practitioner
Member of ICSAN
Member of ICAN
A director with 10 years business experience
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 332 of CAMA 2020 lists Legal Practitioners, Chartered Secretaries, and Chartered Accountants as qualified for public company secretaryship. [67-69]

quizzes.scenario: How many members constitute a quorum for a meeting of the CAC Governing Board?
options: Three
Five
Seven
Ten
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 5(3) of CAMA 2020 (as noted in S.A Badmus) specifies that the quorum for a meeting of the Commission's board is five. [54]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company wishes to buy shares in a Nigerian company using convertible currency. Is this FDI or FPI?
options: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI)
Foreign Portfolio Investment (FPI)
Joint Venture
Acquisition
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Buying shares in an existing company without necessarily establishing a local business presence is classified as Foreign Portfolio Investment (FPI). [59, 70]

quizzes.scenario: A dispute arises between a foreign investor and the Federal Government regarding an enterprise. What is the first mandated step for resolution?
options: Arbitration
Litigation in Federal High Court
Amicable settlement through mutual discussion
Petition to the President
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 26 of the NIPC Act, parties to an investment dispute must first attempt to reach an amicable settlement through mutual discussion. [44, 71]

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to use the word "Holding" in its name. Whose consent is required before registration?
options: Attorney General
Registrar General of CAC
Minister of Trade
SEC Chairman
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Use of restricted words like "Holding" or "Group" requires the prior consent of the Registrar-General of the CAC. [43, 72, 73]

quizzes.scenario: An applicant for SEC registration is refused. How long does the SEC have to notify the applicant of the reasons for refusal?
options: 7 days
14 days
60 days
30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The SEC has 60 days to review an application. If refused, it must notify the applicant and allow representation within 14 days. [74]

quizzes.scenario: A small company is being incorporated. Can they have only one director?
options: No, minimum is always two
Yes, CAMA 2020 allows it
Only if it is a foreign company
Only if the director is a lawyer
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 271(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that every company shall have at least two directors, except for a small company which may have one. [5, 75]

quizzes.scenario: Which regulatory body is responsible for managing the "Combined Expatriate Residence Permit and Aliens Card" (CERPAC)?
options: NIPC
NIS
CAC
Ministry of Interior
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Immigration Service (NIS) issues the CERPAC to enable foreigners to live and work in Nigeria. [23, 76]

quizzes.scenario: A company fails to file annual returns for 10 consecutive years. What action can the CAC take?
options: Fine the shareholders
Arrest the Secretary
Strike the name of the company off the register
Seize the company's assets
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 425 of CAMA 2020, failure to file annual returns for 10 years is a ground for the Commission to strike the company off the register as defunct. [77, 78]

quizzes.scenario: A public company wants to appoint a director who is 75 years old. What is the requirement?
options: He cannot be appointed
He needs a court order
Special notice must be given to shareholders disclosing his age
Ordinary resolution without notice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 282 of CAMA 2020 requires special notice for the appointment of a director 70 years and above in a public company. [69, 79, 80]

quizzes.scenario: An aggrieved person wishes to appeal a decision of the Investment and Securities Tribunal. Where does the appeal go?
options: Federal High Court
Supreme Court
Court of Appeal
SEC APC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Appeals from the decisions of the IST lie to the Court of Appeal on points of law or mixed law and fact. [81, 82]

quizzes.scenario: A company intends to spend ?100 million on R&D. Which tax incentive allows them to get a 20% credit on this expenditure?
options: Pioneer Status
Investment Tax Credit
Double Taxation Relief
Capital Allowance
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Investment Tax Credit provides a 20% tax credit on funds spent by a company on research and development. [44]

quizzes.scenario: A newly called lawyer wants to register a business name for a client. Does he need to be accredited by the CAC?
options: Yes, all filings require accreditation
No, accreditation is only required for company incorporation under Part B
No, if he has a NBA seal
Yes, if the client is foreign
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Accreditation is specifically required for incorporating companies under Part B; anyone can register entities under Part E (Business Names) or F (Trustees). [83]

quizzes.scenario: Which body is responsible for granting "Pioneer Status" which provides a tax holiday to certain industries?
options: FIRS
NIPC
CAC
Ministry of Finance
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission (NIPC) handles applications for Pioneer Status under the Industrial Development (Income Tax Relief) Act. [84, 85]

quizzes.scenario: A company's registered office is moved from Lagos to Abuja. Which form must be filed with the CAC to notify them of this change?
options: CAC Form 1.1
CAC Form 5
CAC Form 6
CAC Form 7
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Notice of a change in a company's registered address is filed using CAC Form 6. [86, 87]

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to appoint a new auditor to fill a casual vacancy. Who has the primary power to do this in the interim?
options: The Shareholders
The Managing Director
The Board of Directors
The CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 401(6) of CAMA 2020 empowers the Board of Directors to fill a casual vacancy in the office of an auditor. [88]

quizzes.scenario: BlueWave Inc., a French company, has been invited by the Federal Government of Nigeria to execute a specific specialist bridge construction project in Delta State. The company does not wish to incorporate a local subsidiary. What is the most appropriate legal step?
options: They must incorporate as a separate entity under S.78 CAMA
They should apply to the Minister of Trade for an exemption under S.80 CAMA
They can operate using their French certificate of incorporation
They must register a Business Name with the CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 80(1)(a) of the Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA) 2020, a foreign company invited by the Federal Government to execute a specified individual project may apply to the Minister of Trade, Industry and Investment for an exemption from the mandatory requirement to incorporate a local entity [1-3].

quizzes.scenario: Hans, a German national, intends to set up a business in Nigeria solely for the production of military camouflage and tactical gear for private security firms. He approaches you for legal advice. What would you tell him?
options: He can proceed if he registers with the NIPC
He is prohibited as this falls under the Negative List
He only needs a Business Permit from the NIS
He must partner with a Nigerian citizen
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 18 and 31 of the NIPC Act and Section 126 of CAMA 2020, foreigners are prohibited from investing in enterprises listed on the "Negative List," which includes the production of military and paramilitary wears and accoutrement [4-6].

quizzes.scenario: A Chinese investment group successfully incorporates 'Dragon Steel Ltd' with the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC). They intend to begin operations immediately. What is the mandatory next step before they can legally commence business?
options: Registration with the Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission (NIPC)
Obtaining a Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI)
Applying for a Business Permit from the NIS
Registering the technology transfer agreement with NOTAP
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 20 of the NIPC Act, every enterprise in which foreign participation is involved must, after its incorporation, be registered with the NIPC before it can legally commence business in Nigeria [7-9].

quizzes.scenario: TechFlow Ltd, a Nigerian company, enters into a licensing agreement with a US software firm to use proprietary coding for 10 years. The agreement was executed on January 1st. By what date must this contract be registered with NOTAP?
options: Within 30 days of execution
Within 60 days of execution
Within 90 days of execution
Before any payment is made to the US firm
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 5(2) of the National Office for Technology Acquisition and Promotion (NOTAP) Act, any contract or agreement involving the transfer of foreign technology must be registered with NOTAP within 60 days of its execution or conclusion [10-12].

quizzes.scenario: An American investor, Sarah, brings $5 million into Nigeria through Zenith Bank to fund her new startup. She wants to ensure she can eventually repatriate her profits in US Dollars. What document must she obtain?
options: A Business Permit
A NIPC Registration Certificate
A Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI)
An Expatriate Quota
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 15 of the Foreign Exchange (Monitoring and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act, an authorized dealer (bank) through which capital is imported must issue a Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI), which guarantees the unconditional transferability and repatriation of profits and capital in a freely convertible currency [13-15].

quizzes.scenario: GlobalOil PLC, a UK-based company, intends to post six of its top British engineers to manage its new subsidiary in Port Harcourt. Who is responsible for applying for the expatriate quota?
options: The six British engineers individually
The British Embassy in Nigeria
The Nigerian subsidiary company (employer)
The Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 2 of the Immigration Act and judicial precedent in Oil Field Supply Centre v Johnson, the duty to apply for an expatriate quota lies with the company wishing to employ the foreigners, not the individual employees themselves [16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: A Canadian firm, MapleSoft, begins selling software directly in Lagos through a local office without incorporating a local entity or obtaining an exemption. They sign a contract with a local distributor. What is the legal status of this contract?
options: Valid and enforceable
Voidable at the option of MapleSoft
Void and illegal
Enforceable only if registered with NIPC later
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 78(2) of CAMA 2020, any act or transaction of a foreign company carried out in Nigeria before it is incorporated as a local entity (unless exempted) is void and a nullity [18-20].

quizzes.scenario: An Italian investor has a dispute with the Federal Government regarding his manufacturing plant. They have tried to resolve it via mutual discussion but failed. They did not specify a method in their agreement. What rules apply to resolve the conflict?
options: The Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023
The rules of the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID)
The High Court Civil Procedure Rules
The NIPC Administrative Rules
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 26 of the NIPC Act, where there is a disagreement between an investor and the Federal Government as to the method of dispute settlement to be adopted, the rules of the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID) shall apply [21-23].

quizzes.scenario: Bjorn, a Swedish national, does not want to manage a company but wants to buy 10% of the shares in a public company listed on the Nigerian Exchange. What form of investment is this?
options: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI)
Foreign Portfolio Investment (FPI)
Joint Venture Investment
Exempted Foreign Investment
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Foreign Portfolio Investment (FPI) involves the purchase of financial assets such as shares or stocks in Nigerian companies without the investor seeking active management or control [24, 25].

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian company fails to register its technical service agreement with NOTAP within the statutory period. The foreign partner is demanding payment. What is the legal effect of this non-registration?
options: The entire contract is void
The foreign partner cannot sue in Nigeria
It only prevents the transfer of fees through the official foreign exchange market
The Nigerian company's directors are criminally liable
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 7 of the NOTAP Act and Stanbic IBTC v FRCN, non-registration of a technology transfer agreement does not make it void but prevents the repatriation of funds/fees due under the contract to a foreign account through the Central Bank of Nigeria [26-28].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company seeks an exemption from incorporation under CAMA 2020. To which specific official must the written application be addressed?
options: The Registrar General of the CAC
The Minister of Trade, Industry and Investment
The Executive Secretary of the NIPC
The President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 80(2) of CAMA 2020, an application for exemption from the requirement of incorporation for a foreign company must be addressed in writing to the Minister of Trade, Industry and Investment [29, 30].

quizzes.scenario: 'Nippon Solar Inc.' is granted an exemption from registration by the Minister to work on a specific project. Within how many days must they serve a notice of this exemption on the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC)?
options: 7 days
14 days
30 days
60 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 80(7) of CAMA 2020, once a foreign company is granted an exemption, it must serve a notice of such exemption on the CAC within 30 days of the grant [31-33].

quizzes.scenario: 'London Brands Ltd' is a foreign company not registered in Nigeria. A local vendor breaches a supply contract with them. Can London Brands Ltd sue the vendor in a Nigerian court?
options: No, because they are not a juristic person in Nigeria
Yes, but only through the Attorney General
Yes, an unregistered foreign company can sue and be sued in its name
Only if they first obtain a Business Permit
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 84 of CAMA 2020 and Ritz & Co KG v Techno Ltd, the fact that a foreign company is not incorporated in Nigeria does not affect its capacity to sue or its liability to be sued in Nigeria [6, 34, 35].

quizzes.scenario: A small company is being formed by two Nigerians and one British national. The company's turnover is projected to be N20 million. Can this company qualify as a "Small Company" under CAMA 2020?
options: Yes, because the turnover is below N120 million
No, because a small company cannot have an alien as a member
Yes, provided the alien owns less than 10%
No, because it must be 100% Nigerian owned
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 394(2)(d) of CAMA 2020, one of the criteria for a company to be classified as a "Small Company" is that none of its members is an alien [36-38].

quizzes.scenario: A group of Japanese partners want to form a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) to provide consultancy services in Lagos. They are registered as an LLP in Tokyo. What must they do to operate in Nigeria?
options: They can operate directly with their Tokyo certificate
They must register a local subsidiary as a company
They must register the foreign LLP as a separate entity in Nigeria under S.788 CAMA
They are prohibited from forming LLPs in Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 788 of CAMA 2020, a foreign Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) intending to carry on business in Nigeria must be registered as a separate entity in Nigeria [39, 40].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. X, a Lebanese national, wants to start a business in Nigeria dealing in the distribution of narcotic substances for medical use. Is this allowed under the NIPC Act?
options: Yes, provided he has NAFDAC approval
No, this is on the Negative List and prohibited to all investors
Only if he partners with the Federal Government
Yes, but he cannot own more than 40%
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 18 and 31 of the NIPC Act, the production of and dealing in narcotic drugs and psychotropic substances is on the "Negative List" and is prohibited to both Nigerians and foreigners [4-6].

quizzes.scenario: A technical agreement between a Nigerian firm and a South African company is refused registration by the Director of NOTAP. What is the remedy for the aggrieved Nigerian firm?
options: File a suit at the Federal High Court
Appeal to the NOTAP Governing Council within 60 days
Apply for a judicial review at the State High Court
Re-submit the application to the CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 9 of the NOTAP Act, any party whose contract for foreign technology transfer is refused registration may appeal to the NOTAP Governing Council within 60 days of the refusal [41, 42].

quizzes.scenario: ABC Ltd has been granted an expatriate quota for a Managing Director position to be filled by a foreigner. What is the duration of this specific type of quota?
options: Valid for 2 years renewable
Valid for 5 years renewable
Permanent Until Reviewed (PUR)
Valid for 10 years
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Expatriate quotas for top management positions like Chairman or Managing Director are typically granted as "Permanent Until Reviewed" (PUR) [16, 43].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign-owned company, 'Dubai Decor Ltd', wants to legally open its offices and start hiring in Lagos. Which specific permit must they obtain from the Nigerian Immigration Service (NIS)?
options: Residence Permit
Business Permit
CERPAC
Cable Visa
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 8(1)(b) of the Immigration Act, no person other than a Nigerian citizen shall establish a profession or take over a company with limited liability without the written consent of the Minister of Interior, usually granted as a Business Permit [16, 44].

quizzes.scenario: An Australian tech firm wants to establish a subsidiary in Nigeria (Foreign Direct Investment). What is the very first step their officials should take regarding immigration?
options: Apply for CERPAC
Obtain a Visa on Arrival
Obtain a Business (Subject to Regularization) Visa from a Nigerian Embassy
Apply for a Residence Permit
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The first step for a foreigner intending to establish a business in Nigeria is to obtain a Business Visa (STR - Subject to Regularization) from the Nigerian embassy in their home country [45-47].

quizzes.scenario: Which agency acts as the primary liaison between foreign enterprises and various Nigerian government departments to facilitate investment?
options: Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC)
Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission (NIPC)
Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN)
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 4 and 27 of the NIPC Act, the NIPC is responsible for coordinating and monitoring investment activities and acting as a liaison between investors and the government [4, 48, 49].

quizzes.scenario: It continues for the remaining 1 year
options: It lapses immediately upon completion of the project
He can start a new project under the same exemption
He must incorporate a company within 30 days
B
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Refer to the relevant legal provision.

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company deliberately includes false information regarding its directors in its application for exemption from registration. What are the legal consequences?
options: The application is simply rejected
Only the company is fined N50,000
The company and its officers commit an offence punishable by fine or imprisonment
The CAC will automatically incorporate them
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 83 of CAMA 2020, providing false information in an application for exemption is a criminal offence for which both the company and the responsible officers are liable on conviction [50].

quizzes.scenario: A Brazilian manufacturing company brings heavy industrial machinery into Nigeria to set up its factory instead of cash. Can they obtain a Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI) for this?
options: No, CCI is only for cash
Yes, via the importation of equipment and raw materials
Only if the machinery is brand new
Only if they have a Nigerian partner
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Foreign capital can be imported into Nigeria through an authorized dealer either in cash or through the importation of raw materials and equipment, for which a CCI will be issued based on the customs value [51, 52].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign investor wants to buy Nigerian debt instruments at a discount abroad and convert them to Naira for investment in a local factory. Which committee implements this scheme?
options: The NIPC Governing Board
The Debt Conversion Committee (DCC) in the CBN
The SEC Administrative Proceedings Committee
The CAC Governing Board
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Debt-Equity Conversion Programme is implemented by the Debt Conversion Committee (DCC) within the Central Bank of Nigeria [53, 54].

quizzes.scenario: An American citizen wants to own 100% of the shares in a new Nigerian logistics company. Is this allowed under current Nigerian law?
options: No, foreigners must have at least 40% Nigerian equity
Yes, the NIPC Act allows for 100% foreign ownership
Only if it is a public company
Only with the President's personal approval
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The NIPC Act provides for liberalized ownership, allowing a non-Nigerian to invest and participate in the operation of any enterprise (except those on the negative list) and own 100% of the shares [8, 55, 56].

quizzes.scenario: A South African investor wants to buy shares in a Nigerian public company via the secondary market. What regulatory body must register this security?
options: Corporate Affairs Commission
Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission
Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
National Office for Technology Acquisition and Promotion
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 8(k) of the Investment and Securities Act, the SEC is required to maintain a register of foreign portfolio investments (FPI) and register the interest of foreigners in Nigerian securities [48, 57, 58].

quizzes.scenario: An Indian company and the Federal Government have an investment dispute. They have failed to reach an amicable settlement and cannot agree on a method for arbitration. What default rules will apply?
options: The Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023
The rules of the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID)
The Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission Rules
The UNCITRAL Arbitration Rules
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 26 of the NIPC Act, where there is a disagreement as to the method of dispute settlement between an investor and the Federal Government, ICSID rules apply [21, 23].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign construction company is in Nigeria to execute a specific bridge project funded by a loan from the World Bank. Under which section of CAMA 2020 can they apply for exemption from incorporation?
options: Section 18
Section 20
Section 80(1)(b)
Section 788
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 80(1)(b) of CAMA 2020, foreign companies in Nigeria for the execution of specific individual loan projects on behalf of an international organization are eligible for exemption [2, 59].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign government-owned company is in Nigeria exclusively for activities aimed at promoting their country's exports. Do they need to incorporate locally?
options: Yes, all foreign companies must
No, they are eligible for exemption under S.80(1)(c) CAMA
Only if they have more than 50 employees
Yes, but they get a tax holiday
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 80(1)(c) of CAMA 2020, foreign government-owned companies engaged solely in export promotion activities are eligible for exemption from registration [60, 61].

quizzes.scenario: What is the tax status of a foreign company that has been granted an exemption from registration under Section 80 of CAMA 2020?
options: They pay 50% of the standard CIT
They are treated as an unregistered company and are exempt from company taxes
They pay the same tax as a local PLC
They only pay VAT but no income tax
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 82 of CAMA 2020, an exempted company has the status of an unregistered company and is exempted from the payment of all company taxes [34, 35, 62].

quizzes.scenario: Once a foreign company is granted an exemption, they must notify the CAC. Which specific CAC Form is used for this notice?
options: Form CAC 1.1
Form CAC 7
Form CAC 21
Form CAC 23
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to the Companies Regulations, the notice of exemption of a foreign company is made using Form CAC 21 (or Form CAC 23 in older references) [32, 33].

quizzes.scenario: An exempted foreign company is required to file an annual report with the CAC. Which form is used for this purpose?
options: Form CAC 5
Form CAC 22
Form CAC 10
Form CAC 20
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Every exempted foreign company must deliver to the Commission an annual report in the prescribed form, which is Form CAC 22 [33].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company carries on business in Nigeria without incorporation or a valid exemption. What is the potential penalty for the company?
options: A fine of N10,000 only
A fine as may be specified by the CAC from time to time
Automatic winding up by the court
Disqualification of all directors for 10 years
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 79 of CAMA 2020, any foreign company that fails to comply with the incorporation or exemption requirements commits an offence and is liable to such penalty as the Commission shall specify by regulation [6, 18, 63].

quizzes.scenario: Under the Investment and Securities Act 2025, which body is specifically tasked with maintaining a register of foreign portfolio investments (FPI)?
options: The Central Bank of Nigeria
The Corporate Affairs Commission
The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
The Nigerian Stock Exchange
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 13(l) of the ISA 2025 and SEC rules mandate that the SEC maintains a register of foreign portfolio investments coming into the Nigerian capital market [57, 58, 64].

quizzes.scenario: What is one significant advantage of obtaining a Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI) for a foreign investor?
options: It exempts them from all local audits
It enables the foreigner to open a domiciliary account with an Authorized Dealer
It grants them automatic Nigerian citizenship
It allows them to bypass NAFDAC regulations
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: One of the primary advantages of a CCI is that it enables the foreigner to open a domiciliary account and a special non-resident Naira account for capital inflows and repatriation [65, 66].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign tech firm enters a technology transfer agreement. NOTAP refuses registration because the price is deemed excessively high compared to the technology's value. Is this a valid ground?
options: No, price is a matter of private contract
Yes, registration can be refused if the price is not commensurate with the value
Only if the Nigerian partner objects
No, NOTAP only checks for security threats
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: NOTAP may refuse the registration of a contract where the price or other obligations are not commensurate with the technology or services acquired [26, 42].

quizzes.scenario: A foreigner wishes to reside and work in Nigeria. Which document serves as the combined residence permit and aliens card?
options: CERPAC
Visa on Arrival
Business Permit
Expatriate Quota
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: CERPAC stands for "Combined Expatriate Residence Permit and Aliens Card" and is the document that allows a foreigner to live and work in Nigeria [12, 16].

quizzes.scenario: An American citizen arrives in Nigeria on a Tourist Visa but decides he wants to stay and regularize his status for business. What type of visa should he have originally obtained?
options: STR (Subject to Regularization) Visa
E-Visa
Visa on Arrival
Diplomatic Visa
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Foreigners intending to stay and work or do business must obtain an STR (Subject to Regularization) Visa, which is later regularized into a residence permit [31, 67].

quizzes.scenario: Two Nigerians and an American partner intend to incorporate a company. What type of agreement should they execute to define their relationship and governance before incorporation?
options: Partnership Deed
Articles of Association
Joint Venture Agreement
Memorandum of Understanding
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A Joint Venture Agreement is commonly used in pre-incorporation stages, especially when the business involves both Nigerians and aliens [68-70].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign investor imports $10 million for a factory. The bank guarantees that she can convert and send back her dividends in US Dollars after paying taxes. This is known as:
options: Sovereign Guarantee
Unconditional Transferability
Diplomatic Immunity
Capital Waiver
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 24 of the NIPC Act and Section 15 of FEMMPA guarantee the unconditional transferability of dividends and profits in a freely convertible currency through an authorized dealer [13, 14, 71].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company has a paid-up capital of N40 million. How many automatic expatriate quota positions are they typically entitled to?
options: 1 position
2 positions
4 positions
10 positions
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: For companies with capital of N30 million and above, the Nigerian immigration policy generally allows for 4 automatic expatriate quota positions [16].

quizzes.scenario: NOTAP refuses to register a contract because it involves the transfer of technology that Hans (the applicant) knows is already widely and freely available in Nigeria. Is this ground legal?
options: No, NOTAP must register all tech contracts
Yes, registration is refused for technology freely available in Nigeria
Only if the price is below N1 million
No, as long as the parties are willing
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: NOTAP Act specifications for refusal include instances where the technology is already freely available in Nigeria [26, 42].

quizzes.scenario: Which agency acts as a liaison between foreign investors and other government agencies to simplify the process of obtaining various approvals?
options: CAC
NIPC
SEC
NIS
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The NIPC Act empowers the commission to act as a liaison between the enterprise and relevant government departments for the purpose of facilitating investment [48, 49].

quizzes.scenario: Under the ISA 2025, which document must a foreign portfolio investor file to register their security with the SEC?
options: Form CAC 1.1
Form SEC 6F
Form NIPC 1
Form STR 2
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to SEC Rules, the application for registration of securities by a foreign portfolio investor is made using Form SEC 6F [72].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company executive executes a deed in London on behalf of his company for use in Nigeria. What must be done to ensure its validity in Nigeria?
options: It must be re-signed in Abuja
It must be authenticated by a Nigerian Notary Public
It must be executed under the company's official seal for use abroad
It must be published in a Nigerian newspaper
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 99 of CAMA 2020, a company may have an official seal for use abroad, and documents signed by an authorized person under such seal have the same effect as if signed by the company itself [73].

quizzes.scenario: A citizen of Ghana wants to reside in Nigeria for 6 months. Does he need a visa under ECOWAS protocols?
options: No, he has 90 days visa-free but needs a residence permit for longer stays
Yes, he needs an STR visa from day one
No, he can stay indefinitely without any permit
Yes, but only if he is working
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Based on the ECOWAS Free Movement Protocol, member state citizens have a 90-day visa-free entry but require residence permits (like the ECOWAS Residence Card) for longer stays or work [16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: An agricultural processing company with 100% foreign ownership is established in an economically disadvantaged area. What incentive are they eligible for?
options: 100% tax holiday for 7 years
Exemption from NIPC registration
Automatic Nigerian citizenship for directors
50% reduction in CAC filing fees
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Companies located in economically disadvantaged local government areas are entitled to a 100% tax holiday (Pioneer Status) for up to 7 years [74-76].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign exporter uses a Duty Drawback Scheme in Nigeria. What benefit are they receiving?
options: A refund of import duties paid on raw materials used for exported goods
A waiver of NIPC registration fees
A reduction in their electricity bill
An exemption from using NOTAP
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Duty Drawback Scheme allows exporters to get a refund on import duties paid for raw materials or packaging items used in the production of goods that are subsequently exported [74, 77].

quizzes.scenario: As a legal practitioner, you are asked to advise a client on incorporating a company to manufacture "psychotropic substances" for general street sale. What is your ethical duty?
options: Help them register but warn them of the risks
Advise them that this business is illegal and falls under the Negative List
Register it as a "Pharmacy" to hide the object
Only help them if they have a Nigerian partner
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 15 of the Rules of Professional Conduct (RPC) requires a lawyer to represent a client within the bounds of the law; manufacturing items on the Negative List is illegal and a solicitor must advise against it [6, 78, 79].

quizzes.scenario: Segun, aged 16, and his father, Chief Ade, decide to incorporate a private company, "Ade & Sons Ltd." They are the only subscribers to the memorandum. The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) refuses the registration. Why is the CAC right?
options: An infant is completely prohibited from forming a company under CAMA
A private company must have at least two adult subscribers if an infant is involved
A minor cannot be a member of any company in Nigeria
Segun must be at least 18 years old to be a subscriber in any circumstance
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 20(2) of CAMA 2020, an infant may join in forming a company provided there are at least two other subscribers of full age and capacity. In this scenario, there is only one adult subscriber (Chief Ade). [1-3]

quizzes.scenario: Alhaji Buba was declared an undischarged bankrupt by a High Court in 2023. He intends to join his friends in forming a new tech company, "Z-Tech Ltd," as a subscriber. What is his legal status regarding this formation?
options: He is eligible since he is over 18 years old
He is disqualified unless he obtains leave from the court
He is disqualified until he is discharged from bankruptcy
He can only join if the other subscribers are not bankrupt
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 20(1)(c) of CAMA 2020 specifically disqualifies an undischarged bankrupt from taking part in the formation of a company. [2, 4, 5]

quizzes.scenario: Three investors want to incorporate "Swift Logistics Ltd" as a private company. They propose an issued share capital of N60,000. Will the CAC register this company?
options: Yes, provided they pay the registration fees
No, the minimum issued share capital for a private company is N100,000
No, the minimum issued share capital for a private company is N500,000
Yes, if it is a small company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 27(2)(a) of CAMA 2020 provides that the minimum issued share capital for a private company shall not be less than N100,000. [6-8]

quizzes.scenario: "Oceanic Oil Plc" is being formed as a public company. The promoters propose an initial issued share capital of N1,500,000. Is this proposal compliant with CAMA 2020?
options: Yes, since it exceeds the private company threshold
No, public companies must have a minimum issued share capital of N2,000,000
No, public companies must have a minimum issued share capital of N5,000,000
Yes, provided it is fully paid up
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 27(2)(a) of CAMA 2020, the minimum issued share capital for a public company is N2,000,000. [7, 9, 10]

quizzes.scenario: "Alpha Ventures Ltd," a private company, currently has 48 members. It intends to issue shares to 5 new employees who will become shareholders. Will this breach the membership limit for private companies?
options: Yes, because the membership will exceed 50
No, because employees and former employees who are members are excluded from the limit
No, because private companies can have up to 100 members under the 2020 Act
Yes, unless they become joint holders
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(3) of CAMA 2020 limits the membership of a private company to 50, but provides that employees and former employees who became members during their employment are not counted toward this limit. [6, 11, 12]

quizzes.scenario: Three siblings, Funmi, Tunde, and Bolaji, hold 1,000 units of shares jointly in "Siblings Trading Ltd," a private company. For the purpose of determining the membership limit, how many members are they counted as?
options: Three members
Two members
One member
They are not counted until the shares are split
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(4) of CAMA 2020 states that where two or more persons hold one or more shares in a company jointly, they shall be treated as a single member for the purpose of the membership limit. [6, 11]

quizzes.scenario: A group of alumni wants to form "Great Scholars Ltd/Gte" to promote education. They intend to include a clause allowing the distribution of 10% of annual profits as dividends to members. Is this permissible?
options: Yes, as long as the majority of profit goes to the object
No, profits of a company limited by guarantee must be applied solely toward its objects
Yes, if the Attorney General of the Federation consents
No, companies limited by guarantee cannot make profit
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 26(1) and (4) of CAMA 2020 provide that the income and property of a company limited by guarantee shall be applied solely toward the promotion of its objects, and no portion shall be paid or transferred directly or indirectly to members as dividends. [13-15]

quizzes.scenario: Promoters of a charitable foundation applied to the Attorney General of the Federation (AGF) for consent to register "Health for All Ltd/Gte." 45 days have passed without a decision from the AGF. What is the next legal step?
options: They must wait indefinitely for the AGF's consent
They should assume consent is denied and sue the AGF
They must advertise the application in three national daily newspapers to invite objections
They can proceed to register with the CAC directly without further steps
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 26(7) and (8) of CAMA 2020, if the AGF does not decide within 30 days, promoters shall advertise the application in three national newspapers and, if no objections arise within 28 days, the CAC may proceed with registration. [16, 17]

quizzes.scenario: "Unity Bank" wants to incorporate a public limited company. They propose the name "Unity Global Ltd." Is this name appropriate?
options: Yes, "Ltd" is a standard abbreviation for all companies
No, the name of a public company limited by shares must end with "Public Limited Company" or "Plc"
Yes, if it is a subsidiary of a private company
No, "Ltd" can only be used by companies limited by guarantee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 29(2) of CAMA 2020 stipulates that the name of a public company limited by shares shall end with the words "Public Limited Company" or the abbreviation "Plc". [18-20]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Obi is the sole shareholder of "Obi Graphics Ltd." The company owes a debt of N5 million to a supplier. The supplier sues Mr. Obi personally for the debt. What is the likely outcome?
options: Mr. Obi is liable because he is the sole owner
The suit will fail because the company is a separate legal entity from Mr. Obi
Mr. Obi is liable up to the amount of his personal assets
The supplier must sue both Mr. Obi and the company jointly
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Based on the principle of corporate personality established in Salomon v Salomon and recognized under Section 42 of CAMA 2020, an incorporated company is a juristic entity distinct from its members. [21-24]

quizzes.scenario: Segun wants to be the sole member of "Segun Global Plc," a public company. Is this allowed under Nigerian law?
options: Yes, CAMA 2020 allows one-man companies
No, only private companies can have a minimum of one member
Yes, provided he has a minimum of two directors
No, all companies in Nigeria must have at least two members
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 18(2) of CAMA 2020 permits one person to form a private company, but Section 18(1) implies that for other types (like public companies), the minimum remains two persons. [25-27]

quizzes.scenario: 25 individuals form an unregistered association to carry on a consultancy business for profit. They have operated for 3 months. What is the legal implication of this?
options: It is a valid partnership at will
It is a prohibited association as it exceeds 20 persons without incorporation
It is valid provided they pay their taxes
It is only prohibited if they have more than 50 members
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 19(1) of CAMA 2020 prohibits any association or partnership of more than 20 persons from carrying on business for profit unless it is registered as a company. [26, 28, 29]

quizzes.scenario: A promoter seeks to reserve the name "Federal Power and Energy Ltd." The CAC refuses the name. Why?
options: The name is identical to an existing company
The name is restricted as it suggests government patronage
The name is too long
The name does not include the word "Nigeria"
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 852(2)(a) and (b) of CAMA 2020 provides that names which include "Federal," "National," or suggest the patronage of the Government are restricted and require the consent of the Commission. [30-32]

quizzes.scenario: Mrs. Vera was found to be of unsound mind by a court in Ghana. She wants to be a subscriber to a new company in Nigeria. Can she participate?
options: Yes, the disqualification only applies to Nigerian courts
No, disqualification applies if found of unsound mind by a court in Nigeria or elsewhere
Yes, if she has a medical report showing recovery
No, but she can be a director
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 20(1)(b) of CAMA 2020 disqualifies any person found to be of unsound mind by a court in Nigeria or elsewhere from forming a company. [2, 4, 33]

quizzes.scenario: "Prime Retail Ltd" is a private company with an annual turnover of N20 million and net assets of N10 million. All members are Nigerians, and it has no government participation. Is this a "small company"?
options: No, its turnover is too low
Yes, it meets the criteria for a small company
No, because it is a company limited by shares
Yes, but only if it has a company secretary
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 394(3) of CAMA 2020 (as modified by the Finance Act) defines a small company as a private company with a turnover of not more than N25 million (or N120m in the main Act) and net assets not exceeding N60 million, with no alien or government participation. [34-36]

quizzes.scenario: "Global Ventures Ltd," a small company, wants to appoint a foreign national, Mr. Smith, as a shareholder. What is the effect of this on its status?
options: None, foreigners can be members of small companies
It will cease to be a small company
It must apply for NIPC approval to remain a small company
It will become a public company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 394(3)(c) of CAMA 2020, a small company must not have any of its members as an alien. Introducing a foreign shareholder removes it from the "small company" category. [35-37]

quizzes.scenario: The directors of "Beta Ltd" hold 40% of the company's equity share capital. The company meets all other turnover and asset criteria for a small company. Does it qualify?
options: Yes, turnover and assets are the only criteria
No, directors must hold at least 51% of the equity share capital
No, directors must hold 100% of the equity
Yes, if they are all Nigerians
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 394(3)(e) of CAMA 2020 requires that for a company to be classified as small, its directors must hold at least 51% of its equity share capital. [35-37]

quizzes.scenario: "X-Class Ltd," a private company, intends to issue new shares. Its Articles are silent on pre-emption. Must it offer these shares to existing members first?
options: No, pre-emption is only for public companies
Yes, existing members have a statutory right of first refusal for 21 days
No, directors can allot to anyone at their discretion
Yes, but only if the members are Nigerians
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 142(1) of CAMA 2020, as amended by the Business Facilitation Act, provides that a private company shall not allot newly issued shares unless they are first offered to existing shareholders, with a 21-day acceptance period. [6, 38-40]

quizzes.scenario: "Rugged Ventures Unlimited" is facing insolvency. The company's assets are insufficient to pay its creditors. What is the liability of the members?
options: Their liability is limited to the amount unpaid on their shares
They have no liability once the company is wound up
They are liable to contribute to the assets of the company without limit
They are only liable if they committed fraud
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 21(1)(c) of CAMA 2020 defines an unlimited company as one where there is no limit on the liability of its members. [41-43]

quizzes.scenario: A group of 5 friends wants to start a small business with N300,000 capital and minimal regulatory burden. They want to ensure they aren't personally liable for debts. What is the most suitable entity?
options: General Partnership
Business Name
Private Company Limited by Shares
Company Limited by Guarantee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A private company limited by shares offers limited liability, allows for a small membership (up to 50), and has a minimum capital of N100,000, making it ideal for SMEs. [44-46]

quizzes.scenario: Investors are forming "Green Field Plc." They decide not to appoint a company secretary at incorporation because they want to save costs. Is this legal?
options: Yes, small companies do not need secretaries
No, every company except a small company must have a secretary
Yes, if they appoint one within 2 years
No, only private companies are exempt
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 330(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that every company, other than a small company, shall have a secretary. As a public company (Plc), it cannot be a small company. [14, 47-49]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Chu, a Chinese citizen, wants to register a business in Nigeria as the 100% owner. Which option is available to him?
options: He can register a Business Name
He must incorporate a company with the CAC and register with NIPC
He must have at least one Nigerian partner with 51% shares
He is completely prohibited from doing business in Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 17 of the NIPC Act and Section 20(4) of CAMA, a foreigner may participate in Nigerian business but must incorporate a company and register with the NIPC. Business names are generally for citizens. [50-52]

quizzes.scenario: A promoter reserved the name "Z-Line Ltd" on June 1st. He attempts to file incorporation documents using this name on September 15th. What will happen?
options: The registration will proceed normally
The reservation has expired as it lasts only 60 days
The reservation has expired as it lasts only 30 days
He must pay a late filing fee to use the name
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 31(2) of CAMA 2020 provides that a name reservation is valid for a period of 60 days, after which it expires unless renewed. [53-55]

quizzes.scenario: "Star Holding Ltd" is currently in liquidation. It attempts to act as a subscriber in the formation of "New Star Ltd." Is this valid?
options: Yes, a company is a juristic person until dissolved
No, a corporate body in liquidation shall not join in forming a company
Yes, provided the liquidator signs the memorandum
No, unless it is a public company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 20(3) of CAMA 2020 expressly prohibits a corporate body in liquidation from joining in the formation of a new company. [1, 2, 5, 56]

quizzes.scenario: Shareholders of "Quick Fix Ltd," a private company, want to pass a resolution to change the company's auditors without holding a physical meeting. Can they do this?
options: No, auditors can only be changed at an AGM
Yes, private companies can pass written resolutions signed by all members
Yes, but only if they have less than 10 members
No, written resolutions are only for board meetings
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 259(1) of CAMA 2020 allows a private company to pass a resolution in writing signed by all members entitled to vote, which is as valid as if passed at a general meeting. [6, 49, 57]

quizzes.scenario: "Legacy Plc" was incorporated 7 months ago. It has not yet held its statutory meeting. What is the legal consequence?
options: None, it has 18 months to hold its first meeting
It is a ground for compulsory winding up by the court
The directors will be fined N1,000 daily
It will automatically be converted to a private company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 235(1) of CAMA 2020 requires every public company to hold a statutory meeting within 6 months of incorporation. Failure to do so is a ground for winding up under Section 571(b). [9, 58-60]

quizzes.scenario: An association of 30 chartered accountants wants to practice together without incorporating a company. Is this allowed?
options: No, they must incorporate a company as they exceed 20 persons
Yes, partnerships of accountants are exempt from the 20-person limit
No, they must register as a Limited Liability Partnership
Yes, but they cannot use a business name
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 19(2)(b)(ii) of CAMA 2020 exempts partnerships of accountants (and legal practitioners) from the rule that associations of more than 20 persons must be incorporated. [28, 29, 61, 62]

quizzes.scenario: A group of traders wants to register "Lagos Trade Chamber Ltd." The CAC refuses the name. Which authority's consent is missing?
options: The Minister of Trade
The Governor of Lagos State
The Attorney General of the Federation
The Registrar General of CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 852(2)(f) of CAMA 2020 provides that any name containing the words "Chamber of Commerce" requires the consent of the Attorney General of the Federation. [30, 32, 63]

quizzes.scenario: Promoters of "Joy Foundation Ltd/Gte" want to ensure that if the company is dissolved, the remaining assets go to the members' children. Is this clause valid?
options: Yes, it is their personal property
No, residue assets must be transferred to another company with similar objects
Yes, if stated in the memorandum
No, the assets must be forfeited to the Federal Government
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 26(15) of CAMA 2020 (Cypress Doctrine) requires that residue assets of a company limited by guarantee be transferred to another company with similar objects or for charitable purposes, not distributed to members. [43, 63, 64]

quizzes.scenario: "Mega Tech Plc" has only one director. For how long can it continue to carry on business before the director becomes personally liable for debts?
options: 30 days
60 days
90 days
6 months
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 271(1) and (3) of CAMA 2020 provide that if a company (other than a small company) carries on business with fewer than two directors for more than 60 days, every director/member aware of this is personally liable for liabilities incurred. [48, 65, 66]

quizzes.scenario: In a private company, a member wants to sell his shares to his cousin who is not a member. The other members object. Can the company prevent this?
options: No, shares are freely transferable property
Yes, a private company must by its articles restrict the transfer of its shares
No, unless the cousin is a minor
Yes, but only if they buy the shares themselves
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(2) of CAMA 2020 requires every private company to restrict the transfer of its shares by its articles of association. [11, 39, 67, 68]

quizzes.scenario: A private company has two subscribers: a Nigerian adult and a registered Nigerian company. Is this formation valid?
options: No, subscribers must be natural persons
Yes, a company is a "person" with capacity to form another company
No, because there is only one natural person
Yes, but they must have a third subscriber
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 18(1) and (2) of CAMA 2020 allows two or more persons (including corporate bodies) to form a company. A company has the capacity of a natural person under Section 43(1). [24, 25, 69, 70]

quizzes.scenario: Promoters are incorporating "Standard Builders Ltd." They submit a "Statement of Compliance." Who is authorized to sign this document?
options: Only a Chartered Accountant
Only a Legal Practitioner
The applicant or his agent
Any of the first directors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 40(1) of CAMA 2020 introduced the "Statement of Compliance," which can be signed by the applicant or his agent, replacing the old "Statutory Declaration" which required a lawyer. [62, 71-73]

quizzes.scenario: "Fast Track Ltd" has an annual turnover of N10 million and total assets of N5 million. It is owned 100% by the Federal Government. Does it qualify as a small company?
options: Yes, it meets the financial threshold
No, small companies cannot have government participation
No, its assets are too low
Yes, if it has only two directors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 394(3)(d) of CAMA 2020, a small company must not have any government, government corporation, or agency as a member. [35, 36, 66]

quizzes.scenario: Investors are deciding between a Limited Partnership (LP) and a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP). What is a key difference?
options: Only LLP is a body corporate with separate legal personality
Only LP requires a partnership agreement
Only LLP has partners with limited liability
Only LP can be registered with the CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 746(1) of CAMA 2020, an LLP is a body corporate with perpetual succession and a legal personality separate from its partners, whereas an LP is not. [23, 74-76]

quizzes.scenario: A subscriber to the memorandum of "Fine Wines Ltd" fails to specify the number of shares he is taking. What is the effect?
options: He is deemed to take 1 share
The memorandum is invalid for that subscriber
He is deemed to take 25% of the capital
He must pay for all the unallotted shares
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 27(3) of CAMA 2020 requires each subscriber to write opposite his name the number of shares he takes, and no subscriber shall take less than one share. [77-79]

quizzes.scenario: "Blue Sky Plc" wants to offer its shares to the general public. Must it issue a prospectus?
options: No, only private companies issue prospectuses
Yes, any invitation to the public to subscribe for shares must be by a prospectus
No, it can use a simple letter of allotment
Yes, but only if it is a small company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Investment and Securities Act (ISA) and Section 41(4) of CAMA 2020, public companies offering shares to the public must comply with prospectus requirements. [71, 80-82]

quizzes.scenario: "Safe Haven Ltd/Gte" makes a profit of N2 million from a charity auction. The members decide to use N500,000 to pay for their annual vacation. Is this allowed?
options: Yes, it is a reward for their hard work
No, no portion of the income of a GTE shall be paid to members
Yes, if they pass a special resolution
No, unless the AGF approves the vacation
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 26(4) of CAMA 2020 strictly prohibits the payment of any portion of the income or property of a company limited by guarantee to its members, except as permitted for promotion of objects. [13, 14, 83]

quizzes.scenario: Promoters want to form a company for the purpose of "identifying and retrieving lost treasures from the Atlantic Ocean." Is this object valid?
options: Yes, any object is valid
No, a company cannot be formed for an illegal purpose
Yes, if they pay the treasure tax
No, because it is too vague
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 18(3) of CAMA 2020 provides that a company shall not be formed for an illegal purpose. Retrieving "lost treasures" may conflict with laws regarding state ownership of such items. [4, 84-86]

quizzes.scenario: "Tech Hub Ltd" was registered as a private company. Within the first year, it invited the public to deposit money with it for fixed interest. What is the consequence?
options: It will be fined N50,000
It ceases to be entitled to the privileges of a private company
It must immediately convert to a public company
The directors will be imprisoned for 2 years
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 23(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that if a private company fails to comply with the conditions of its status (e.g., inviting the public), it ceases to be entitled to private company privileges. [45, 87]

quizzes.scenario: Five people want to register "Unity Cooperative Society Ltd" as a company under Part B. The CAC refuses. Why?
options: Only the Registrar of Cooperatives can register it
The name contains the word "Cooperative," which is restricted
They must have at least 10 members
Cooperative societies cannot be companies
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 852(2)(c) of CAMA 2020 provides that any name containing the word "Cooperative" is restricted and requires the consent of the Commission. [30, 32]

quizzes.scenario: "Zenith Ventures Ltd" intends to have its shares listed and traded on the Nigerian Stock Exchange. Is this possible in its current state?
options: Yes, all companies can be listed
No, only public companies (Plc) can have their shares traded on the Stock Exchange
Yes, if it has more than 50 members
No, unless it is a small company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(5) of CAMA 2020 prohibits private companies from inviting the public to subscribe for shares, which is a prerequisite for listing on the Stock Exchange. [6, 11, 88]

quizzes.scenario: A promoter prepares the memorandum for "Success Ltd" but fails to include a "Registered Office Clause." What is the status of the document?
options: It is valid as the address is on the application form
It is non-compliant as the registered office must be stated to be in Nigeria
It is valid if the directors are all Nigerians
The CAC will supply the clause automatically
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 27(1)(b) of CAMA 2020 requires the memorandum of every company to state that the registered office of the company is situated in Nigeria. [86, 89-91]

quizzes.scenario: "Alpha & Beta Ltd" has two members. One member dies, leaving the other as the sole member. The survivor continues business for 10 months without adding a new member. What is the survivor's liability?
options: None, one-man companies are allowed
Personal liability for all debts incurred after the first 6 months
Liability is limited to his shareholding
He must pay a fine of N500 daily
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Sections 571(c) and 572 of CAMA, if a company (other than a private one-man company) carries on business with one member for more than 6 months, the survivor becomes personally liable for debts. [59, 60, 92]

quizzes.scenario: A group wants to form a company where they take full responsibility for all company debts to build trust with high-value clients. What is the best option?
options: Public Limited Company
Company Limited by Guarantee
Unlimited Company
Limited Liability Partnership
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An unlimited company, as defined in Section 21(1)(c) of CAMA 2020, has no limit on the liability of its members, making them fully responsible for the company's debts. [41-43, 93]

quizzes.scenario: Promoters of "Bright Future Ltd" decide to adopt the "Model Articles" provided by the Minister. How much of the Model Articles applies to them?
options: None, they must still draft their own
The Model Articles apply in full unless they expressly exclude or modify them
Only the sections relating to directors apply
They only apply if the CAC stamps them separately
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 33 and 34 of CAMA 2020 allow companies to adopt Model Articles, which apply to the extent they are not excluded or modified by the company's registered articles. [17, 20, 94, 95]

quizzes.scenario: "New Age Ltd" wants to change its status to a public company. What is the primary requirement for this conversion?
options: Unanimous resolution of all members
An ordinary resolution of the board
A special resolution and consequential alterations to the Memart
Approval from the Attorney General
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 56(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that a private company may re-register as a public company if a special resolution is passed and the required alterations are made to its documents. [96-98]

quizzes.scenario: A professional firm wants to register as an entity that has its own legal personality but allows partners to have limited liability. They don't want to be a company. What is the best choice?
options: Limited Partnership
Business Name
Limited Liability Partnership (LLP)
Incorporated Trustee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An LLP under Part C of CAMA 2020 is a body corporate with its own legal personality, providing limited liability to all its partners. [74, 75, 99]

quizzes.scenario: Promoters are starting "Global Trade Ltd." They have N5 million in capital. They are advised that as a private company, they must have a secretary. Is this always true?
options: Yes, every company must have a secretary
No, small companies may operate without a secretary
Yes, but the secretary can be one of the directors
No, if they have only two members
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 330(1) of CAMA 2020 makes the appointment of a company secretary optional for small companies. [47, 48, 100, 101]

quizzes.scenario: "Eco-Friendly Ltd/Gte" is being wound up. After paying all debts, there is N10 million left. The members vote to donate it to the "Save the Children" charity, which has similar goals. Is this legal?
options: No, it must be returned to the members
Yes, this follows the Cypress Doctrine for companies limited by guarantee
No, it must be given to the CAC
Yes, but only if the AGF approves the specific charity
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 26(15) of CAMA 2020 requires surplus assets of a company limited by guarantee to be transferred to another similar institution, not distributed to members. [43, 63, 64]

quizzes.scenario: Omega Ltd recently incorporated and has its registered office in Lagos. The directors decide to display the company's name only on the internal reception desk and not at the entrance of the building.
options: This is lawful as long as the name is inside the building
The company is only required to publish its name on official documents
The company must paint or affix its name in a conspicuous position on the outside of every office or place of business
The company only needs to display the name at its headquarters
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 729(1)(a) of CAMA 2020, every company must paint or affix its name on the outside of every office or place of business in a conspicuous position and in letters easily legible [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: The Board of Directors of Swift Logistics Ltd wants to change the company's Articles of Association to remove the requirement for a common seal. They pass a Board Resolution to this effect.
options: The change is valid as it is a board matter
The change is invalid because the Articles can only be altered by a Special Resolution of the members
The change is valid but must be confirmed by the CAC
The change is invalid because every company must have a common seal
