quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 53(1) of CAMA 2020, a company may only alter its Articles of Association by passing a Special Resolution in a general meeting [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: A shareholder of Alpha Ltd, a private company, wishes to inspect the register of members. The company secretary refuses, stating that the register is currently being kept at the Chairman's private residence for safekeeping.
options: The secretary is right to protect the register
The register must be kept at the company's registered office and be open for inspection
The register can be kept anywhere as long as the CAC is notified
Only directors are allowed to inspect the register
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 110(1) of CAMA 2020 requires that the register of members be kept at the company's registered office, and Section 113 provides that it shall be open to the inspection of any member [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: Beta Plc wishes to change its name to "Beta Oil & Gas Plc". The members pass a Special Resolution, but the company secretary argues they also need the written consent of the Corporate Affairs Commission.
options: The secretary is wrong; a Special Resolution is sufficient
The secretary is right; the change of name requires the written consent of the Commission
The company needs a court order, not CAC consent
Only a board resolution is needed for a name change
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 30(1) of CAMA 2020, a company may change its name by special resolution and with the written approval of the Commission [7, 8].

quizzes.scenario: Gamma Ltd passes a Special Resolution to alter its objects clause to include real estate development. Mr. Ade, who holds 18% of the company's shares, voted against the resolution and wants to challenge it in court.
options: Mr. Ade cannot challenge a majority decision
Mr. Ade can apply to the Federal High Court for the cancellation of the alteration within 28 days
Mr. Ade must apply to the CAC first for a review
Mr. Ade can only challenge the alteration if he holds 51% of the shares
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 51(1) and (2) of CAMA 2020 allows holders of not less than 15% of the nominal value of the company's issued share capital to apply to the court for the cancellation of an alteration to the objects within 28 days of passing the resolution [9, 10].

quizzes.scenario: Delta Ltd, a private company, has 45 members. It wants to re-register as a public company (Plc). The directors realize their current issued share capital is N500,000.
options: They can re-register as a Plc with the current capital
They must first increase their issued share capital to at least N2,000,000
Public companies do not have a minimum share capital requirement
They only need to change the name to "Delta Plc"
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 27(2)(a) of CAMA 2020 provides that the minimum issued share capital for a public company shall not be less than N2,000,000 [11, 12].

quizzes.scenario: Epsilon Plc wants to convert into a private company (Ltd). A group of members holding 6% of the company's shares opposes the move.
options: They cannot stop the conversion as the majority rules
They can apply to the court for the cancellation of the resolution within 28 days
Only the CAC can cancel the conversion
They must hold at least 15% to object
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 64(1) of CAMA 2020, members holding at least 5% in nominal value of the company's issued share capital may apply to the court for the cancellation of a resolution to re-register a public company as private [13, 14].

quizzes.scenario: Zeta Ltd, a company limited by shares, intends to re-register as an unlimited company. The Board holds a meeting and passes a Special Resolution with 75% support.
options: The conversion is validly approved
The conversion requires the assent of all members of the company
The conversion is prohibited under CAMA 2020
Unlimited companies do not require member consent
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 68(1)(b) of CAMA 2020 specifies that a company limited by shares may re-register as unlimited provided all the members assent to its being so re-registered [15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: An investor wants to verify if "Titanium Ltd" has any registered charges on its assets before granting a loan. He instructs his lawyer to conduct a search.
options: The lawyer must apply to the Federal High Court
The lawyer should conduct a corporate search at the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC)
Charges are not public record
The search must be conducted at the Land Registry only
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Corporate searches are conducted at the Corporate Affairs Commission to verify a company's status, including registered charges and encumbrances [17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: Theta Ltd, a small company, was incorporated on June 1, 2023. The directors are unsure when they are required to file their first annual return.
options: They must file by June 30, 2023
They are not required to file an annual return in the year of incorporation or the following year
They must file within 42 days of incorporation
Annual returns are only for public companies
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 417 of CAMA 2020 and relevant regulations, small companies are exempted from filing annual returns in the year of incorporation or the year following incorporation [19, 20].

quizzes.scenario: Iota Ltd wishes to authenticate a power of attorney. The board is unsure if they must use the company's common seal.
options: Every document must be sealed
The document is authenticated if signed by a director and the secretary or other authorized officer without a seal
Only the Chairman's signature is required
A seal is mandatory for all deeds
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 101 of CAMA 2020 provides that a document requiring authentication by a company may be signed by a director, secretary, or other authorized officer and does not need to be under the common seal [21, 22].

quizzes.scenario: Kappa Ltd is an insurance company. Beside the reception desk at its head office, it displays its Certificate of Incorporation and its latest Statement of Affairs.
options: This is a waste of space and not required
This is mandatory for insurance and banking companies
Only the Certificate of Incorporation is required
Only the Statement of Affairs is required
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 733 of CAMA 2020 requires every banking or insurance company to display its Statement of Affairs in a conspicuous place in its registered office and every branch [20, 23].

quizzes.scenario: The directors of Lambda Ltd want to increase the company's share capital to fund a new project. They note that the Business Facilitation Act has amended the procedure.
options: They must call a General Meeting for a Special Resolution
They can now increase the share capital by a Board Resolution
Share capital can only be increased by the CAC
Only the Chairman can increase share capital
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Following the amendment by the Business Facilitation Act 2022 to Section 127(1) of CAMA, the board of directors can now increase share capital by a board resolution [24, 25].

quizzes.scenario: Mu Plc has successfully changed its name from "Mu Ltd". The company secretary is told to surrender the old certificate of incorporation.
options: The secretary should keep it as a historical record
The original certificate must be surrendered to the Commission for cancellation and a new one issued
The old certificate remains valid alongside the new one
Surrender is only required for liquidations
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Upon a change of name or re-registration, the company is required to surrender its original certificate of incorporation to the CAC for cancellation [26, 27].

quizzes.scenario: Nu Ltd passes a Special Resolution to change its registered office address from Abuja to Kano. The company secretary prepares Form CAC 6.
options: The notice must be filed within 7 days
The notice of change must be filed with the Commission within 15 days of the resolution
No filing is needed for address changes
A board resolution is enough; no form is needed
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 102 of CAMA 2020 (read with Section 50(5)) requires that notice of change of the registered office be given to the Commission within 15 days of the resolution [28, 29].

quizzes.scenario: Xi Ltd, an unlimited company, wants to convert to a company limited by shares to protect its members from personal liability.
options: Unlimited companies can never convert to limited companies
They may convert by passing a Special Resolution and applying to the CAC
They only need to change their name
This requires a 100% unanimous vote only
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 71(1) of CAMA 2020 permits an unlimited company to re-register as a limited company by passing a special resolution [30, 31].

quizzes.scenario: Omikron Ltd passes a resolution to reduce its share capital because it has excess funds. A creditor, Bank X, objects to the reduction.
options: Creditors have no say in share capital matters
A company cannot reduce its share capital without an order of the court confirming the reduction
Creditors must be paid in full before any reduction
The CAC can approve it without the court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 131(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that a company limited by shares may, by special resolution and subject to confirmation by the court, reduce its share capital [32, 33].

quizzes.scenario: Pi Ltd is a private company that has just hit 52 members (excluding employees). What is the legal implication?
options: Nothing, the limit is 100 members
It ceases to be entitled to the privileges of a private company unless the default is accidental
It is automatically dissolved
It must immediately re-register as a Plc
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(3) and 23 of CAMA 2020 limit private company membership to 50, and failure to comply results in the loss of private company privileges [34, 35].

quizzes.scenario: Rho Ltd wants to start using an official seal for its branch office in Ghana.
options: Official seals are prohibited for private companies
A company whose objects require transactions in foreign countries may have an official seal for use outside Nigeria
Only one seal is allowed per company
Branch offices must use the common seal of the head office
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 99(1) of CAMA 2020 allows a company to have an official seal for use outside Nigeria, which must be a facsimile of the common seal with the name of the territory where it is to be used [21, 36].

quizzes.scenario: Sigma Ltd issues its first bill of exchange. The name of the company is written as "Sigma" instead of "Sigma Ltd".
options: The document is still valid
The person who signed it may be personally liable if the company fails to pay
The CAC will automatically correct the error
Only public companies need to use full names
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 729(2) of CAMA 2020 provides that if an officer signs a bill of exchange on behalf of the company and the company's name is not mentioned correctly, they may be personally liable [2, 37].

quizzes.scenario: Tau Ltd is a private company. Can it pass a resolution to appoint its company secretary without holding a formal physical meeting?
options: No, all secretary appointments must be in a physical meeting
Yes, a private company can pass written resolutions signed by all members
Only the Board can appoint the secretary, not members
Physical meetings are mandatory for all public and private companies
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 259(1) of CAMA 2020 allows a private company to pass a resolution in writing signed by all members entitled to attend and vote [34, 38].

quizzes.scenario: Upsilon Ltd, a private company, has only one director. Is this legally permissible?
options: No, every company must have at least two directors
Yes, if it is a small company, it may have a single director
One director is only allowed for public companies
The Chairman must always be a second director
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 271(1) of CAMA 2020 requires every company (other than a small company) to have at least two directors [39, 40].

quizzes.scenario: Phi Ltd wants to register its first allotment of shares. Within what timeframe must it file the return of allotment (Form CAC 5)?
options: Within 3 months
Within 15 days of the allotment
Return of allotment is not required for private companies
Within 24 hours
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 154(1) of CAMA 2020 (as amended by BFA) requires a company to deliver a return of allotment to the CAC within 15 days [41, 42].

quizzes.scenario: Chi Ltd is found to have obtained its name by fraud or through misleading information during incorporation.
options: The CAC can ignore it since it's already registered
The Commission may direct the company to change its name within 6 weeks
The company must be wound up immediately
Fraudulent names are allowed if no other company has the name
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 31(3) of CAMA 2020 empowers the Commission to direct a company to change its name if it was discovered that approval was obtained through fraud or misleading information [8, 43].

quizzes.scenario: Psi Ltd is preparing for its first Annual General Meeting (AGM). The directors are unsure how many days' notice to give to members.
options: 7 days
21 days
14 days
30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 242(1) of CAMA 2020 requires that the notice of all general meetings (including AGM) shall be at least 21 days [44, 45].

quizzes.scenario: Omega Ltd, a public company, holds its AGM. Mr. X is nominated as a director. Mr. X is 72 years old.
options: He is disqualified because of his age
He can be appointed provided special notice is given disclosing his age to members
He can be appointed only if he is a life director
Public companies have no age limit for directors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 282 of CAMA 2020 provides that where a person is 70 years or older, special notice must be given for their appointment as a director in a public company [46, 47].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer conducts a corporate search for a client and finds that Alpha Ltd has not filed annual returns for 12 years.
options: The lawyer should ignore it
Failure to file annual returns for 10 years is a ground for striking off the company's name from the register
The company must pay double tax
The directors must be arrested immediately
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 425 of CAMA 2020 allows the Commission to strike a company off the register if it has failed to file annual returns for 10 consecutive years [20, 48].

quizzes.scenario: Beta Ltd wants to re-register as a public company. They have recently resolved to reduce their share capital by half.
options: They can proceed with re-registration
A company cannot re-register as public if it has resolved to reduce its share capital unless the court orders otherwise
Reduction of capital is a prerequisite for going public
The CAC will decide the capital later
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 57(5)(a) of CAMA 2020 prohibits re-registration as a public company if the company has resolved to reduce its share capital [49, 50].

quizzes.scenario: Gamma Ltd intends to change its objects. They pass the special resolution on July 1st. When can they apply to the CAC to register the change if there are no objections?
options: Immediately on July 2nd
After the expiration of 28 days from the date the resolution was passed
Within 7 days
Objects cannot be registered with the CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 51(7) of CAMA 2020 requires the company to wait 28 days for potential minority objections before applying to the CAC to register an object alteration [26, 51].

quizzes.scenario: Delta Ltd, a private company, wishes to restrict the transfer of its shares to outsiders in its Articles.
options: This is prohibited for all companies
Private companies are required by Section 22(2) to restrict the transfer of their shares in their Articles
Only public companies can restrict transfers
It must be in the Memorandum, not the Articles
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(2) of CAMA 2020 states that the Articles of a private company shall restrict the transfer of its shares [52, 53].

quizzes.scenario: Epsilon Ltd is newly incorporated. The subscribers want to appoint the first company secretary.
options: They must wait 6 months
They must appoint a secretary not later than 6 months after incorporation
Small companies must have a secretary
Only the CAC appoints the first secretary
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 330(1) of CAMA 2020 requires every company (except small companies) to have a secretary, and they must be appointed within 6 months of incorporation [54, 55].

quizzes.scenario: Zeta Plc holds its statutory meeting 4 months after incorporation. The secretary sends the statutory report to members 7 days before the meeting.
options: This is correct procedure
The statutory report must be sent at least 21 days before the meeting
Statutory reports are optional
14 days notice is required for all public reports
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 235(2) of CAMA 2020 requires the directors to send the statutory report to every member at least 21 days before the statutory meeting [56, 57].

quizzes.scenario: Eta Ltd is a small company. Its turnover is N20 million and its net asset value is N10 million. It has no foreign or government members.
options: It is a public company
it qualifies as a "small company" under CAMA 2020
It must have at least 10 directors
It is exempt from all corporate laws
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 394(3) defines a small company as a private company with a turnover of not more than N120m (amended by Finance Act to N25m for tax) and net assets not more than N60m [58, 59].

quizzes.scenario: Theta Ltd has 3 directors. One dies, and the remaining two want to appoint a replacement until the next AGM.
options: They must wait for the AGM
They can appoint a "casual director" to fill the vacancy
Only the court can fill a vacancy
The deceased's family chooses the new director
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 274(1) of CAMA 2020 allows directors to fill a casual vacancy, subject to approval at the next AGM [60, 61].

quizzes.scenario: Iota Ltd wants to conduct a major asset transaction involving the sale of 60% of its total assets.
options: A board resolution is enough
It must be approved by a Special Resolution of the members
Major assets cannot be sold
Only the Chairman can approve this
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 342 of CAMA 2020 requires a special resolution for any major asset transaction which involves selling or transferring more than 50% of the total assets of the company [38, 62].

quizzes.scenario: Kappa Ltd wants to change its registered office address. Which form must they submit to the CAC?
options: Form CAC 1.1
Form CAC 6
Form CAC 7
Form CAC 3
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Form CAC 6 is the statutory form for the Notice of Change of Registered Address [28, 63].

quizzes.scenario: Lambda Ltd, a private company, has hit 55 members because 5 employees recently resigned but kept their shares.
options: It must convert to a Plc
Resigned employees who were members while employed are excluded from the 50-member count
It is fine, the limit is 100
The company must forfeit the extra shares
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(3) of CAMA 2020 excludes current and former employees (who became members during employment) from the 50-member limit for private companies [34, 64].

quizzes.scenario: Mu Ltd is an insurance company. It fails to display its Statement of Affairs at its branch in Port Harcourt.
options: No penalty applies
The company and its officers are liable to a fine for every day of the default
Port Harcourt is not a headquarters so it's fine
Display is only for the head office
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 733(3) of CAMA 2020 imposes a penalty for failure to display the statement of affairs in every office where the company carries on business [20, 23].

quizzes.scenario: Nu Ltd has lost its Certificate of Incorporation. What should the directors do?
options: Close the business
Apply to the CAC for a Certified True Copy (CTC) of the certificate
Register a new company
Use a photocopy of the memo as proof
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A company can apply for a Certified True Copy of its certificate of incorporation from the CAC if the original is lost [65, 66].

quizzes.scenario: Xi Ltd, a public company, wants to appoint "Alpha Ltd" as its company secretary.
options: A company cannot be a secretary
A public company may appoint a body corporate as secretary if its members are qualified professionals
Only individuals can be secretaries
Only legal practitioners can be secretaries
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 332(e) of CAMA 2020 permits a body corporate or firm to be appointed as a secretary for a public company [55, 67].

quizzes.scenario: Omikron Ltd wants to increase its share capital. It sends notice of the increase to the CAC 20 days after the resolution was passed.
options: It is within time
The notice must be given within 15 days of passing the resolution
30 days is the standard time
No notice to CAC is needed for capital increases
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 127(2) of CAMA 2020 requires that notice of an increase in share capital be given to the Commission within 15 days [63, 68].

quizzes.scenario: Pi Ltd is a private company. Can its secretary be a 20-year-old student with no professional qualifications?
options: No, they must be a lawyer
Yes, for private companies, directors only need to ensure the person has requisite knowledge
21 is the minimum age
A student cannot be a secretary
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 332 of CAMA 2020 sets strict qualifications only for secretaries of public companies; for private companies, the board decides suitability [39, 69].

quizzes.scenario: Rho Ltd wants to conduct its AGM via Zoom. It is a private company with members in 3 different countries.
options: Electronic meetings are illegal
A private company may hold its general meetings electronically
Only public companies can have Zoom meetings
Members must all be in Nigeria for any meeting
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 240(2) of CAMA 2020 explicitly allows private companies to hold general meetings electronically [70, 71].

quizzes.scenario: Sigma Ltd wishes to inspect the register of directors of another company, Tau Plc, for a potential merger.
options: Only the CAC can see the register
Sigma Ltd can inspect the register during business hours upon paying a fee
Registers are private documents
Only shareholders of Tau Plc can see it
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 320(1) and (2) of CAMA 2020 provide that the register of directors and secretaries shall be open to the inspection of any person on payment of a fee [6, 72].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer conducts a search on Alpha Ltd and receives a report showing no annual returns for 5 years and a missing register of members.
options: This is a "clean" company
This indicates the company is not in good standing and in breach of statutory duties
missing registers are common and legal
The lawyer should tell the client to buy the company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Keeping statutory books and filing annual returns are mandatory post-incorporation requirements; failure to do so indicates non-compliance [5, 73].

quizzes.scenario: Beta Ltd is a small company. The directors want to know if they must have an Audit Committee.
options: Yes, all companies must
No, audit committees are mandatory only for public companies
Only banking companies need them
It is optional for everyone
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 404(2) of CAMA 2020 requires every public company to have an audit committee; private and small companies are not so mandated [46, 74].

quizzes.scenario: Gamma Plc intends to convert to Gamma Ltd. The resolution is passed. On the 20th day after the resolution, a shareholder applies to the court to stop it.
options: The application is too late
The application is within the 28-day window allowed for objections
Applications must be within 15 days
Shareholders have no right to object to re-registration
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 64(1) of CAMA 2020 allows 28 days for members to apply to the court for cancellation of a public-to-private conversion [14, 75].

quizzes.scenario: Delta Ltd wants to decrease its share capital. It passes a Special Resolution. When does the reduction take effect?
options: Immediately
When the court confirms it and the order is registered by the CAC
After 3 weeks of advertisement
when the board signs the minutes
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 131 and 135 of CAMA 2020 provide that a reduction of share capital is subject to court confirmation and registration of the court order with the CAC [32, 33].

quizzes.scenario: Epsilon Ltd wants to use its common seal on a contract. The seal has the company's name but not the registration (RC) number.
options: This is perfectly legal
The company's name and registration number must be engraved in legible characters on the seal
Seal numbers are for government only
The seal is only valid for international deals
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 729(1)(b) of CAMA 2020 requires the company's name and registration number to be on its common seal [2, 20].

quizzes.scenario: Zeta Ltd passes a resolution to change its name. The company secretary fails to surrender the old certificate.
options: The CAC will issue a new one anyway
The CAC will not issue the new certificate until the old one is surrendered for cancellation
The secretary will be arrested
The name change is automatically cancelled
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Procedure for change of name requires the surrender of the original certificate of incorporation for cancellation before a new one is issued [26, 27].

quizzes.scenario: Ade, a 17-year-old tech prodigy, wants to join the formation of Zenith PLC alongside two adults who are not disqualified. Can Ade validly subscribe to the memorandum?
options: Yes, because there are two other persons not disqualified subscribing to the memorandum
No, because he is an infant and cannot be a member
Only if he has a court order
Only if the company is a private company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 20(2) of CAMA 2020, a person shall not be disqualified as an infant if two other persons not disqualified have subscribed to the memorandum [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: Blue-Ray Ltd, a private company, has 48 individual members. They want to admit a husband and wife who will hold their shares jointly. Will this exceed the membership limit?
options: Yes, they will have 50 members
No, joint holders are treated as a single member
Yes, unless they are employees
No, because the limit is 100 for private companies
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(3) and (4) of CAMA 2020 provides that the membership limit for a private company is 50, and two or more persons holding shares jointly are treated as a single member [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: Chidi subscribed to the memorandum of Alpha Ltd during incorporation, but the company secretary forgot to enter his name in the register of members. Is Chidi a member?
options: No, until his name is entered in the register
Yes, subscribers are deemed members upon registration of the company
Only if he has paid for the shares
Only if he attends the first AGM
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 105(1) of CAMA 2020 stipulates that subscribers to the memorandum are deemed to have agreed to become members and shall be entered as members upon registration, regardless of the register entry [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: A creditor of Delta Ltd converts his N10 million loan into equity shares by agreement. At what point does he legally become a member of the company?
options: Upon signing the debt-equity agreement
Upon issuance of the share certificate
When his name is entered in the register of members
After the next AGM
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 105(2) of CAMA 2020, persons who agree in writing to become members become so when their names are entered in the register of members [5, 7].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Bala, a member of Gasket Ltd, has been declared of unsound mind by a High Court in Lagos. What is the immediate effect on his membership?
options: He automatically ceases to be a member
His shares are forfeited to the company
He cannot exercise membership rights personally, and his shares vest in a committee or trustee
The company must wind up
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 106(1) and (4) of CAMA 2020 provides that a person of unsound mind found so by a court cannot become a member, and if already a member, his shares vest in a representative [5, 8].

quizzes.scenario: Efe holds 6% of the voting rights in a public company. Is the company required to include his name in the register of persons with significant control?
options: No, the threshold is 10%
Yes, the threshold is at least 5%
Only if he is also a director
Only if he is a foreigner
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 119 of CAMA 2020 requires every company to keep a register of persons with significant control, defined as holding at least 5% of shares or voting rights [9, 10].

quizzes.scenario: Fola, a member of a public company, wants to inspect the minutes book of the last AGM. The company secretary refuses, stating it is confidential. Is the secretary right?
options: Yes, minutes are for directors only
No, members have a statutory right to inspect the minute book for at least 6 hours daily without charge
Yes, if Fola owns less than 10% shares
No, but Fola must pay a N5,000 inspection fee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 266 and 267 of CAMA 2020 grant members the right to inspect the minute book of general meetings at the registered office for at least 6 hours a day without charge [11, 12].

quizzes.scenario: Global PLC was incorporated on February 1, 2024. By what date must they hold their statutory meeting?
options: August 1, 2024
February 1, 2025
December 31, 2024
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 235(1) of CAMA 2020 requires every public company to hold a statutory meeting within six months of its incorporation [13, 14].

quizzes.scenario: The directors of Horizon Ltd fail to deliver the statutory report to the CAC or hold the statutory meeting as required. What can the members do?
options: Nothing, it is a board matter
Petition the court for the winding up of the company
Sue the CAC for negligence
Automatically remove the directors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 571(b) of CAMA 2020 provides that default in delivering the statutory report or holding the statutory meeting is a ground for compulsory winding up by the court [15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: J-Coy Ltd was incorporated in June 2023. They held their first AGM in December 2024 (18 months later). When is the latest they can hold the next AGM?
options: December 2025
March 2026
September 2025
June 2025
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 237 of CAMA 2020 requires an AGM every year, and not more than 15 months shall elapse between one AGM and the next [17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: K-Limited is a small company with only two shareholders. Are they mandated to hold an AGM under CAMA 2020?
options: Yes, all companies must hold an AGM
No, small companies and single-shareholder companies are exempt
Only if their turnover exceeds N100 million
Yes, but they can hold it every two years
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 237(1) of CAMA 2020 exempts small companies and companies with a single shareholder from the requirement to hold an Annual General Meeting [17, 19].

quizzes.scenario: At the AGM of Luna PLC, the board wants to remove a director before his term expires. Is this ordinary or special business?
options: Ordinary business
Special business
Neither, directors cannot be removed at an AGM
It depends on the Articles
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 238 of CAMA 2020 lists ordinary business (dividends, accounts, reports, election of retiring directors, auditors). Any other business, like removing a director, is special business [20, 21].

quizzes.scenario: A group of minority shareholders in Max PLC holding 12% of the paid-up capital want to discuss a specific urgent matter. Can they force a meeting?
options: No, only the board can call meetings
Yes, they can requisition an EGM by depositing a signed requisition at the registered office
Yes, but only if they have 25% of shares
Only by application to the court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 239(2) of CAMA 2020 allows members holding at least one-tenth (10%) of the paid-up capital to requisition an Extraordinary General Meeting [22, 23].

quizzes.scenario: Members of Nexus Ltd requisitioned an EGM on March 1st. The directors have ignored the request for 21 days. What is the members' remedy?
options: They must apply to the CAC
They can convene the meeting themselves within 3 months of the requisition date
They must sue for breach of contract
The company is automatically wound up
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 239(4) of CAMA 2020 provides that if directors fail to proceed to convene a meeting within 21 days of a requisition, the requisitionists may themselves convene the meeting [24, 25].

quizzes.scenario: Omega PLC wants to hold its AGM. What is the statutory length of notice required to be given to all members?
options: 14 days
15 days
21 days
28 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 241(1) of CAMA 2020 mandates that the notice required for all general meetings shall be 21 days from the date on which the notice was sent out [26, 27].

quizzes.scenario: The board of Prime Ltd realizes they forgot to send notice of the AGM to 2 out of 500 members. Does this invalidate the meeting?
options: Yes, notice must be given to every member
No, if the omission was accidental and not in bad faith
Yes, unless the two members attend anyway
No, but they cannot vote on special resolutions
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 245(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that the accidental omission to give notice to a person entitled to receive it shall not invalidate the proceedings of the meeting [26, 28].

quizzes.scenario: Quick PLC is planning its AGM. Besides members and directors, who else is statutorily entitled to receive notice of the meeting?
options: The company's bankers
The local government chairman
The company's auditors and the CAC
Only the majority shareholders
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 243(1) of CAMA 2020 lists persons entitled to notice, including members, directors, auditors, the secretary (for PLCs), and the CAC (for PLCs) [26, 29].

quizzes.scenario: A public company, Rex PLC, serves notice of its AGM via post. On what day is the notice deemed to have been effectively served?
options: The day it was posted
48 hours after posting
7 days after posting
14 days after posting
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 244(2) of CAMA 2020 states that where a notice is sent by post, service is deemed to be effected at the expiration of seven days after the letter containing the notice is posted [26, 30].

quizzes.scenario: Star Ltd has 15 members. The Articles are silent on quorum. How many members must be present to form a valid quorum for a general meeting?
options: 2 members
5 members
10 members
15 members
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 256(2) of CAMA 2020 provides that if the Articles are silent and members exceed 6, the quorum is one-third of the members (1/3 of 15 = 5) [31, 32].

quizzes.scenario: During a general meeting of Top Ltd, 10 members were present (forming a quorum). 4 members walked out for "insufficient reasons." What happens to the meeting?
options: It must be adjourned immediately
The meeting continues and decisions bind all shareholders
It is dissolved permanently
A new quorum must be formed within 5 minutes
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 256(3) of CAMA 2020 provides that if a member withdraws for insufficient reasons and the quorum is no longer present, the meeting shall continue and its decisions shall bind all shareholders [33, 34].

quizzes.scenario: At an AGM, a member of Union PLC wants to appoint his brother (a non-member) as his proxy. Is this allowed?
options: No, proxies must be members of the company
Yes, a proxy need not be a member
Only if the Articles specifically allow it
Only if the brother is a lawyer
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 254(1) of CAMA 2020 states that a member entitled to attend and vote is entitled to appoint another person (whether a member or not) as his proxy [35, 36].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Ade submitted a proxy form to Val Ltd but decided to attend the meeting in person. He did not formally revoke the proxy in writing. Can he vote personally?
options: Yes, personal attendance automatically cancels the proxy
No, he cannot vote personally unless the proxy has been revoked in writing
He can only vote if the proxy agrees
He can only vote on ordinary resolutions
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 254 of CAMA 2020 implies that if a proxy has not been revoked, the member cannot attend and vote personally [36, 37].

quizzes.scenario: During the AGM of Win PLC, the Chairman declares a resolution passed by a show of hands. A member holding 15% of shares disagrees. What can he do?
options: Nothing, the Chairman's declaration is final
Demand a poll
Sue the Chairman for fraud
Ask for a recount by show of hands
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 248(1) of CAMA 2020 allows a poll to be demanded on any question (except the election of a chairman or adjournment) by members holding at least one-tenth (10%) of the total voting rights [38, 39].

quizzes.scenario: X-PLC wants to pass a resolution to change its name. What type of resolution and notice period are required?
options: Ordinary resolution with 14 days notice
Special resolution with 21 days notice
Written resolution with 28 days notice
Unanimous resolution with 7 days notice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 258(2) of CAMA 2020 requires a special resolution (passed by 75% majority) with 21 days' notice for matters such as changing a company's name [38, 40].

quizzes.scenario: Y-Ltd is a private company with 3 members. They want to pass a resolution to appoint a new secretary without holding a physical meeting. Is this valid?
options: No, all resolutions must be passed at a physical meeting
Yes, private companies can pass written resolutions signed by all members
Only if the CAC gives prior approval
Only for ordinary business
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 259(1) of CAMA 2020 permits private companies to pass a resolution in writing signed by all members entitled to attend and vote, which is as valid as if passed in a general meeting [3, 41].

quizzes.scenario: The Chairman of Zen PLC is late for the AGM by 75 minutes. The Articles are silent. What should the members do?
options: Adjourn the meeting for one week
The directors present may appoint one of their number to be chairman
The meeting is automatically dissolved
The company secretary must act as chairman
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 265(2) of CAMA 2020 provides that if the chairman is not present within one hour after the time appointed for a general meeting, the directors present may appoint one of their number to be chairman [42, 43].

quizzes.scenario: A public company, Aero PLC, held its AGM in London to attract foreign investors. Is this meeting valid under Nigerian law?
options: Yes, meetings can be held anywhere globally
No, statutory and AGMs of public companies must be held in Nigeria
Only if 95% of members agree
Only if held electronically
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 240(1) of CAMA 2020 requires all statutory and annual general meetings of public companies to be held in Nigeria, except for small companies or single-shareholder companies [44, 45].

quizzes.scenario: Beta Ltd, a private company, has 10 members. They want to hold their AGM via Zoom. Is this permissible?
options: No, electronic meetings are not recognized in Nigeria
Yes, private companies may hold general meetings electronically if the Articles allow
Only for board meetings
Only if the CAC attends the meeting
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 240(2) of CAMA 2020 allows private companies to hold general meetings electronically, provided they comply with the company's Articles [44, 46].

quizzes.scenario: Members of Clear Ltd want to pass a special resolution at a meeting in 10 days instead of 21. What majority is needed to agree to this shorter notice?
options: All members (unanimous)
A majority holding at least 95% in nominal value of shares
A simple majority of those present
Shorter notice is never allowed for special resolutions
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 241(2)(b) of CAMA 2020 allows shorter notice for any meeting (other than an AGM) if agreed by a majority holding not less than 95% of the shares or voting rights [47, 48].

quizzes.scenario: At the first Board Meeting of NewCo Ltd, the directors need to adopt the certificate of incorporation and appoint a chairman. What is the quorum if there are 5 directors?
options: 1 director
2 directors
3 directors
5 directors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 290(1) of CAMA 2020 states that if the number of directors is 6 or less, the quorum for a board meeting is two [49, 50].

quizzes.scenario: The Chairman of the Board of Directors of Dash PLC is 10 minutes late for a board meeting. What is the procedure?
options: The meeting must wait for one hour
The directors present may appoint one of their number to be chairman for that meeting
The meeting is cancelled
The Managing Director automatically becomes Chairman
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 289(4) of CAMA 2020 provides that if the chairman is not present within five minutes of the time appointed for a board meeting, the directors may choose one of their number to be chairman [42, 50].

quizzes.scenario: Directors of Fast Ltd passed a resolution via WhatsApp where each director typed "I agree" and signed a PDF. Is this valid?
options: No, board resolutions must be in a physical meeting
Yes, a resolution in writing signed by all directors entitled to notice is as valid as if passed at a meeting
Only if it is a private company
Only if ratified at the next AGM
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 289(8) of CAMA 2020 states that a resolution in writing signed by all the directors entitled to receive notice of a meeting is valid and effectual [51, 52].

quizzes.scenario: A director of Solid PLC is also a director in 6 other public companies. He failed to resign the excess directorships before the next AGM. What is the penalty?
options: He is automatically removed from all companies
He is liable to a penalty specified by the CAC for each day he remains a director in more than 5 companies
His shares are forfeited
He is barred from corporate practice for life
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 307(3) of CAMA 2020 provides a penalty for any person who acts as a director of more than five public companies in violation of the Act [53, 54].

quizzes.scenario: A public company wants to appoint Mr. Gogal, who is 72 years old, as a director. What special procedural requirement applies?
options: He cannot be appointed because he is over 70
A special resolution is required
Special notice must be given to the company, and the company must give special notice to members disclosing his age
He must be appointed for life
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 282 of CAMA 2020 allows the appointment of a director over 70 in a public company provided special notice (28 days) is given, disclosing the person's age [55, 56].

quizzes.scenario: The board of Trust Ltd has a tie in votes during a board meeting. The Articles are silent. Does the Chairman have a second vote?
options: No, resolutions fail in a tie
Yes, the chairman has a second or casting vote
Only if he owns more than 50% shares
Only on ordinary resolutions
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 289(2) of CAMA 2020 grants the chairman a second or casting vote in the event of an equality of votes at a board meeting [52, 57].

quizzes.scenario: A director of Mega PLC wants to call a board meeting because he disagrees with the MD's strategy. Can he call a meeting?
options: No, only the Chairman can call a board meeting
Yes, any director may summon a meeting of the board at any time
Only if he has 10% of shares
Only if the Secretary agrees
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 289(3) of CAMA 2020 provides that a director may, and the secretary on the requisition of a director shall, summon a meeting of the board [58].

quizzes.scenario: Notice of a board meeting was given to 4 out of 5 directors of Small-Biz Ltd. The 5th director was in the same building but was overlooked. Is the meeting valid?
options: Yes, because the majority was present
No, failure to give notice to all directors entitled to receive it invalidates the meeting
Yes, if it was an accidental omission
Only if the 5th director waives his right
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 292(3) of CAMA 2020 stipulates that failure to give notice of a board meeting to all directors invalidates the meeting [57, 59].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. K is an alternate director for Director J. Is he counted for the purpose of quorum at a board meeting if Director J is absent?
options: No, alternate directors never count for quorum
Yes, he is counted in the absence of his appointor
Only if the MD approves
Only if he is a member of the company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 290 of CAMA 2020 implies that alternate directors, acting as proxies for absent directors, are counted for quorum and valid proceedings [57, 60].

quizzes.scenario: The Board of Directors of Link Ltd is deadlocked and cannot form a quorum to take any decision. What can the company do?
options: The company must be wound up
The general meeting may act in place of the board of directors
The CAC will take over management
The MD has the sole power to decide
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 291 of CAMA 2020 provides that the general meeting may act in place of the board if the directors are unable to act due to a deadlock or lack of quorum [61, 62].

quizzes.scenario: A member of a public company wants to propose a resolution to remove an auditor at the next AGM. How much notice must he give the company?
options: 7 days
14 days
21 days
28 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 401(5) and 261 of CAMA 2020, special notice of 28 days is required for a resolution to remove an auditor or appoint someone else in their place [56, 63].

quizzes.scenario: After passing a special resolution to reduce share capital, within how many days must the company file it with the CAC?
options: 7 days
14 days
15 days
21 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 258 of CAMA 2020, combined with general filing rules, requires that a printed copy of every special resolution be forwarded to the CAC within 15 days [64, 65].

quizzes.scenario: Joint holders A and B are on the register. Notice of the AGM was sent only to A. B claims the meeting is invalid for lack of notice. Is B correct?
options: Yes, every individual must get notice
No, notice to the joint holder named first in the register is sufficient service to all
Yes, unless B signed a waiver
Only if B holds more shares than A
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 244(4) of CAMA 2020 provides that for joint holders, service of notice on the person named first in the register is deemed sufficient service to all joint holders [30, 37].

quizzes.scenario: A member of a company died three months ago. The company secretary refuses to send an AGM notice to his executors because they aren't on the register. Is this correct?
options: Yes, only persons on the register get notice
No, personal representatives of deceased members are entitled to receive notice
Yes, unless they have 10% of shares
No, but they cannot attend the meeting
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 243(1)(b) of CAMA 2020 entitles every person upon whom the ownership of a share devolves (like executors) to receive notice of meetings [26, 29].

quizzes.scenario: An AGM notice stated the meeting would be at "The Event Center, Lagos." It failed to specify the time. Is the notice valid?
options: Yes, the date and venue are enough
No, a notice must specify the place, date, and time of the meeting
Yes, if the time is the same as the previous year
No, but the meeting can still hold if 50% of members attend
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 242(1) of CAMA 2020 requires that the notice of a meeting shall specify the place, date, and time of the meeting [66, 67].

quizzes.scenario: At the AGM of Flow PLC, a poll is demanded. How are the votes counted in a poll?
options: One man, one vote
According to the number of shares or voting rights held by each member
The Chairman decides the weight of each vote
Only directors' votes are counted
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 248 of CAMA 2020 provides for voting by poll, where voting is based on the number of shares held rather than a simple show of hands [38, 39].

quizzes.scenario: The board of a company wants to shorten the notice period for an AGM from 21 days to 7 days. Who must agree?
options: A 75% majority of members
All members entitled to attend and vote (unanimous)
Only the independent directors
The CAC Registrar-General
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 241(2)(a) of CAMA 2020 allows a shorter notice for an AGM only if it is agreed to by all the members entitled to attend and vote [27, 47].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Okafor ceases to be a member of a company by transferring his shares. How long does the company have to effect this change in the register?
options: 7 days
14 days
28 days
30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 109 of CAMA 2020 requires that any change in membership (including cessation) be entered in the register of members within 28 days [9].

quizzes.scenario: A member of a company has not received a dividend or notice of meeting for two years. He suspects his name was omitted from the register. What is his court remedy?
options: Petition for winding up
Application for rectification of the register by originating motion
Sue the secretary for damages
File a criminal complaint with the Police
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 115 of CAMA 2020 allows an aggrieved person to apply to the court for rectification of the register if a name is omitted or entered without sufficient cause [68].

quizzes.scenario: A public company has 45 members. Must they keep an index of members?
options: Yes, all companies must keep an index
No, an index is only mandatory for companies with more than 50 members
Only if they have multiple classes of shares
Only if the members request it
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 111(1) of CAMA 2020 mandates that every company having more than 50 members shall keep an index of the names of its members [9].

quizzes.scenario: A company is being wound up. Can the company in liquidation become a member of a new company?
options: Yes, to facilitate asset transfer
No, a corporate body in liquidation cannot join in the formation of a company
Only if the liquidator is a lawyer
Only if it is a private company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 20(3) of CAMA 2020 expressly prohibits a corporate body in liquidation from joining in the formation of a company [2, 8].

quizzes.scenario: Alpha Ltd, a large logistics company with a turnover of 500 million Naira, currently has only one director. The board wants to know if this is legal under CAMA 2020.
options: It is legal as long as they notify the CAC.
It is illegal as only small companies can have one director.
It is legal if the director is also the secretary.
It is illegal because all companies must have at least three directors.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 271(1) of CAMA 2020, every company except a small company must have at least two directors. Alpha Ltd does not qualify as a small company due to its turnover exceeding 120 million Naira [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: During the incorporation of Zenith Tech Ltd, the subscribers failed to name the first directors in the Memorandum. Where should these names be sought?
options: In the Articles of Association.
In a separate board resolution.
From the Corporate Affairs Commission.
In the shareholders' agreement.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 272 of CAMA 2020, if the first directors are not named in the Memorandum, they may be named in the Articles of Association [2, 3].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. John, an undischarged bankrupt, wants to be appointed as a director in a new fintech startup. Can he be validly appointed?
options: Yes, provided he discloses his bankruptcy.
Yes, if the other directors approve.
No, he is disqualified by law.
No, unless he owns at least 50% of the shares.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 283(c) of CAMA 2020, an undischarged bankrupt is expressly disqualified from being appointed as a director of a company [4, 5].

quizzes.scenario: A public company, Globe PLC, intends to appoint Chief Obafemi, who is 75 years old, to its board. What procedural requirement must be met?
options: A special resolution must be passed.
A special notice must be given to members stating his age.
Unanimous consent of the board is required.
No special procedure is required for directors over 70.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 282 of CAMA 2020 requires that for a public company, special notice (28 days) must be given of any resolution appointing a director who is 70 years of age or older, and the notice must disclose his age [6, 7].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Emeka is currently a director in six different public companies. According to CAMA 2020, what is the consequence of this arrangement?
options: He must resign from all but five at the next AGM.
He can remain in all if he pays a fine to the CAC.
He is automatically disqualified from all six.
There is no limit on the number of public directorships.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 307(2) of CAMA 2020 provides that a person shall not be a director in more than five public companies, and any person currently holding more must resign from the excess not later than the next AGM [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: The board of Omega Ltd consists of 9 directors. At a board meeting, only 2 directors are present. Can they validly transact business?
options: Yes, 2 is the default quorum for all companies.
No, the quorum must be one-third of the total number of directors.
Yes, if the Chairman is one of the two.
No, the quorum is fixed at 5 for a board of 9.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 290 of CAMA 2020, if the number of directors is more than 6, the quorum is one-third of the total number, which for 9 directors would be 3 [10-12].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Ade is not officially appointed as a director of Beta Ltd, but the board of Beta Ltd is accustomed to following his specific instructions. What is Mr. Ade's legal status?
options: He is a Managing Director.
He is a Shadow Director.
He is an Interloper Director.
He has no legal status as he wasn't appointed.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 270 of CAMA 2020 defines a shadow director as any person on whose instructions and directions the directors are accustomed to act [13-15].

quizzes.scenario: The members of Delta Ltd are unhappy with the performance of Director Kalu and wish to remove him before his term expires. What is the correct procedure?
options: A board resolution is sufficient.
An ordinary resolution with special notice.
A special resolution is required.
Only the court can remove a director before his term ends.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 288(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that a company may by ordinary resolution remove a director before the expiration of his period of office, notwithstanding anything in its articles, provided special notice (28 days) is given [16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: Chief Musa is a Life Director at Zeta Ltd. The members want to remove him due to gross misconduct. Chief Musa claims he cannot be removed because of his "Life" status. Is he correct?
options: Yes, Life Directors are permanent.
No, he can be removed via Section 288.
Yes, unless he is convicted of a crime.
No, but only if the Articles are first amended.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 281 of CAMA 2020, a person may be appointed a life director, but he is still subject to removal under Section 288 like any other director [13, 17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Bello, a director of Shine Ltd, resides in London. The company is holding a board meeting in Lagos. Is the company required to send him a notice of the meeting?
options: Yes, all directors must receive notice.
No, unless he has provided an address in Nigeria for service.
Yes, but only if the meeting is an AGM.
No, directors outside Nigeria are never entitled to notice.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 292(2) of CAMA 2020, it is not necessary to give notice of a meeting to a director who is absent from Nigeria unless he has provided an address in Nigeria for such purposes [11, 19].

quizzes.scenario: Small Ventures Ltd is a registered private company with a turnover of 20 million Naira. Does it need to appoint a company secretary?
options: Yes, every company must have a secretary.
No, small companies are exempt from the mandatory appointment.
Yes, but only if they have more than one director.
No, if they have at least one legal practitioner on the board.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 330(1) of CAMA 2020 exempts small companies from the mandatory requirement to have a company secretary [20, 21].

quizzes.scenario: The board of Mega PLC, a public company, wants to appoint their Chief Accountant's sister, who is a high school graduate with 10 years of experience as a receptionist, as the Company Secretary. Is she qualified?
options: Yes, if the board believes she has the requisite knowledge.
No, secretaries of public companies must have specific professional qualifications.
Yes, if she undergoes a 6-month training.
No, because she is related to the Chief Accountant.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 332 of CAMA 2020 stipulates that a secretary of a public company must be a legal practitioner, chartered accountant, chartered secretary, or have held the office for 3 of the last 5 years in a public company [22-24].

quizzes.scenario: The directors of Swift Ltd appointed Mr. Tunde as the Company Secretary. Who has the legal power to remove him?
options: The shareholders at a general meeting.
The Board of Directors.
The Managing Director alone.
The Corporate Affairs Commission.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 333(1) of CAMA 2020, the power to appoint and remove a company secretary is vested in the board of directors [25, 26].

quizzes.scenario: The board of First Bank PLC intends to remove its Company Secretary for serious misconduct. What is the minimum notice period the secretary must be given to defend himself?
options: 3 working days.
7 working days.
14 days.
21 days.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 333(2) of CAMA 2020 requires that the board of a public company give the secretary at least 7 working days to make a defense or resign before removal [27, 28].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Chidi is both a Director and the Company Secretary of Star Ltd. A legal document requires the signature of a director and the secretary. Can Mr. Chidi sign in both capacities?
options: Yes, to save time.
No, a person cannot sign in dual capacities where the law requires both.
Yes, if authorized by the Articles.
No, a director can never be a secretary.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 331 of CAMA 2020 provides that where a document is required to be signed by a director and the secretary, it shall not be satisfied by the same person acting in both capacities [29, 30].

quizzes.scenario: A board meeting of Apex Ltd was held without giving notice to Director Jane. At the meeting, the board voted to sell a major asset. What is the status of that meeting?
options: The meeting is valid if the majority were present.
The meeting is invalid due to failure to give notice.
The meeting is valid if Jane later ratifies the decision.
The meeting is voidable at the discretion of the CAC.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 292(3) of CAMA 2020 and the rule in Longe v. First Bank, failure to give notice of a board meeting to any director entitled to receive it invalidates the meeting [19, 31].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Yusuf is a director of a public company. He also wants to serve as the Company Secretary of the same public company. Which body's code of conduct would most likely discourage this dual role?
options: The Criminal Code.
The Nigerian Code of Corporate Governance (NCCG) 2018.
The Immigration Act.
The Stamp Duties Act.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The NCCG 2018 and other codes of corporate governance generally advocate for the separation of roles to ensure checks and balances [18, 32].

quizzes.scenario: A casual vacancy occurred on the board of Sunshine Ltd due to the death of a director. The remaining directors appointed Mr. Ray to fill the vacancy. When does Mr. Ray's term expire?
options: He serves for life.
At the next Annual General Meeting, subject to member approval.
Immediately after the next board meeting.
He completes the remainder of the deceased director's term.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 274(1) of CAMA 2020 allows directors to fill a casual vacancy, but the person appointed holds office only until the next AGM where he must be approved by members [13, 33].

quizzes.scenario: At an AGM of a public company, a resolution is proposed to appoint three new directors using a single resolution. No unanimous resolution was passed beforehand. What is the status of this appointment?
options: It is valid.
It is void.
It is voidable by the CAC.
It is valid only if the directors are from the same family.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 287(1) of CAMA 2020 states that for public companies, the appointment of two or more directors by a single resolution is void unless a unanimous resolution to do so was first agreed upon [34, 35].

quizzes.scenario: Director Ada is being removed from the board of Prime Ltd. She has a valid service contract that hasn't expired. Can she sue for damages despite the company's power to remove her?
options: No, the power to remove is absolute.
Yes, removal does not deprive a director of compensation for breach of contract.
No, unless the removal was for fraud.
Yes, but only if the CAC approves the suit.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 288(6) of CAMA 2020 provides that the removal of a director does not deprive them of any compensation or damages payable in respect of the termination of their appointment as a director or any other appointment [36, 37].

quizzes.scenario: A person aged 17 years is nominated as a director of a private company along with two other directors aged 30 and 40. Is this appointment valid?
options: No, the minimum age is 18.
Yes, if he is represented by a guardian.
No, unless he is a shareholder.
Yes, the law allows minors if two other directors are adults.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 283(a) of CAMA 2020, any person under the age of 18 is disqualified from being a director. Note: This differs from the rule for forming a company where a minor is allowed if two others are adults [4, 38].

quizzes.scenario: A company's Articles of Association state that a director must hold at least 500 shares to be qualified. Mr. Obi is appointed but does not hold any shares. How long does he have to acquire them?
options: 7 days.
14 days.
2 months.
6 months.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 277(1) of CAMA 2020, if a share qualification is required by the articles, a director must obtain his qualification within 2 months of his appointment [2].

quizzes.scenario: Director Funke of Glass Ltd has a personal interest in a contract the company is about to enter into. What is her primary duty under CAMA 2020?
options: She must resign immediately.
She must disclose the nature of her interest to the board.
She must vote against the contract.
She must ensure the company makes a profit from her interest.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 306 of CAMA 2020 places a duty on directors to avoid conflicts of interest, and Section 303 requires disclosure of the nature of their interest in contracts [39, 40].

quizzes.scenario: Who is considered the "mind and brain" of a company according to the case of Bolton Engineering v Graham & Sons?
options: The shareholders.
The Company Secretary.
The Directors.
The Auditors.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In Bolton Engineering v Graham & Sons, directors were described as the mind and brain (alter ego) of the company, distinct from mere servants [41, 42].

quizzes.scenario: A director of a public company fails to attend board meetings for an entire year without a valid excuse. Which code suggests he should be subject to re-election only if he attended at least two-thirds of meetings?
options: The Criminal Code.
The SEC Code of Corporate Governance.
The Finance Act.
The NOTAP Act.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The SEC Code of Corporate Governance (2011) and the NCCG (2018) emphasize attendance, with the SEC Code specifically mentioning a two-thirds attendance requirement for re-election [43].

quizzes.scenario: The board of a bank in Nigeria is being constituted. According to the CBN Code of Corporate Governance, what is the maximum number of directors allowed?
options: There is no maximum.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to the Code of Corporate Governance for Banks, the board of directors must not be less than 5 and should have a maximum of 20 directors [44, 45].

quizzes.scenario: An independent director is being appointed to the board of a public company. To qualify as "independent" under Section 275 of CAMA 2020, he must not have been an employee of the company within the last how many years?
options: 1 year.
2 years.
5 years.
10 years.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 275(3)(a) of CAMA 2020 defines an independent director as one who, among other things, has not been an employee of the company within the preceding 2 years [46, 47].

quizzes.scenario: A director who is also the Managing Director of a company is removed from his position as a director under Section 288. What happens to his position as Managing Director?
options: He remains Managing Director.
His position as MD automatically terminates.
He becomes a shadow director.
The members must pass a separate resolution to remove him as MD.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In Yalaju Amaye v AREC Nig Ltd, it was held that if a person's directorship is terminated, his appointment as Managing Director (which is dependent on being a director) also terminates [37, 48, 49].

quizzes.scenario: During a board meeting, there is an equal split of votes on a proposal. Who has the power to cast a deciding vote under Section 289 of CAMA 2020?
options: The Managing Director.
The Company Secretary.
The Chairman.
The oldest director present.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 289(2) of CAMA 2020 provides that in the case of an equality of votes, the chairman shall have a second or casting vote [50, 51].

quizzes.scenario: A director diverts a business opportunity meant for the company to his personal business. Which fiduciary duty has he primarily breached?
options: Duty to act for a proper purpose.
Duty to observe utmost good faith and act in the best interest of the company.
Duty of care and skill.
Duty to attend all meetings.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 305(1) of CAMA 2020, a director stands in a fiduciary relationship and must observe utmost good faith towards the company in any transaction [39, 52].

quizzes.scenario: The Company Secretary of a public company, XYZ PLC, is being removed for a reason other than fraud. The board suspends her instead of immediate removal. What must the board do next?
options: Fire her after 30 days.
Notify the members at the next general meeting.
Apply to the Federal High Court for an order of dismissal.
Re-appoint her if she apologizes.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the procedure for removing a secretary in a public company, if the ground is not fraud or serious misconduct, the board may only suspend her and must report this to the next general meeting [53, 54].

quizzes.scenario: Who is typically responsible for issuing the notice of a board meeting upon the instruction of the Chairman?
options: The Managing Director.
The Chief Internal Auditor.
The Company Secretary.
The external solicitor.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Company Secretary's primary duties include preparing and sending notices of board and general meetings [10, 55, 56].

quizzes.scenario: A public company has been incorporated for 8 months but has still not appointed a Company Secretary. Is this a violation of CAMA 2020?
options: No, secretaries are optional for the first year.
Yes, a secretary must be appointed within 6 months of incorporation.
No, only the first directors are mandatory.
Yes, but only if they have more than 50 members.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 330 of CAMA 2020 requires every company (except small ones) to have a secretary, and Section 332 implies this should be done promptly; sources suggest a 6-month window for appointment post-incorporation [25].

quizzes.scenario: A firm of Legal Practitioners, "Legal Eagles & Co", is appointed as the Company Secretary of a public company. Is this legally permissible?
options: No, only individuals can be secretaries.
Yes, a firm can be appointed if all its members are qualified.
No, unless it's a private company.
Yes, but the firm must have at least 10 partners.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 332(e) of CAMA 2020 allows a body corporate or firm to be appointed as a secretary if all its members are qualified (e.g., as legal practitioners or chartered accountants) [25, 26].

quizzes.scenario: A Company Secretary of a public company makes a secret profit from a transaction involving the company. Under Section 334 of CAMA 2020, is she liable to the company?
options: No, she is not a director.
Yes, she owes a fiduciary duty if acting as an agent.
No, unless she is also a shareholder.
Yes, but only if she is a legal practitioner.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 334 of CAMA 2020 provides that a secretary shall not owe fiduciary duties unless acting as an agent, in which case she is bound to account for secret profits [29, 57].

quizzes.scenario: The board of a public company wants to delegate its power to allot shares to a committee. Is this allowed?
options: No, only the general meeting can allot shares.
Yes, the board can delegate powers to a committee of its members.
No, allotment must be done by the full board.
Yes, but the committee must include at least one shareholder.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 289(5) of CAMA 2020 allows directors to delegate any of their powers to a committee consisting of such member or members of their body as they think fit [50, 58].

quizzes.scenario: A company's Articles are silent on the number of directors. What is the minimum required for a non-small company?
options: One.
Two.
Three.
Five.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 271(1) of CAMA 2020 stipulates that every company other than a small company shall have at least two directors [42, 59].

quizzes.scenario: In a private company, three directors are being appointed. Can they be appointed by a single resolution?
options: No, each must have a separate resolution.
Yes, private companies can appoint multiple directors with one resolution.
No, unless it's a family business.
Yes, but only if they are also shareholders.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Unlike public companies, private companies are not restricted by Section 287 and can appoint multiple directors via a single resolution [34, 35].

quizzes.scenario: A director of a public company is also a substantial shareholder. He fails to disclose this interest at a general meeting. What is the consequence under Section 278?
options: He is removed from office.
He is liable to a penalty specified by the Commission.
The company is dissolved.
His shares are forfeited.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 278(3) of CAMA 2020 provides that failure to disclose required facts (like multiple directorships or age in public companies) makes the director liable to a penalty [60].

quizzes.scenario: The Nigerian Code of Corporate Governance 2018 is issued by which regulatory body?
options: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN).
Financial Reporting Council of Nigeria (FRCN).
Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC).
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The NCCG 2018 was released and is administered by the Financial Reporting Council of Nigeria [61, 62].

quizzes.scenario: A public company has 12 directors. How many of them must retire by rotation at the second AGM according to the default rule in Section 285?
options: All of them.
3 directors.
4 directors.
6 directors.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 285(1) of CAMA 2020 states that one-third of the directors shall retire by rotation at subsequent AGMs. One-third of 12 is 4 [63, 64].

quizzes.scenario: At a board meeting of a public company, the Chairman is absent 10 minutes after the scheduled start time. What should the directors do?
options: Adjourn the meeting.
Appoint one of their number to be chairman for that meeting.
The Managing Director automatically becomes chairman.
The meeting must proceed without a chairman.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 289(6) of CAMA 2020 states that if the chairman is not present within five minutes after the time appointed, the directors present may choose one of their number to be chairman of the meeting [58, 65].

quizzes.scenario: A director is found to be of unsound mind by a qualified medical doctor but not yet by a court. Can he be disqualified from remaining a director?
options: No, only a court order suffices.
Yes, for directors, a medical finding is sufficient for disqualification.
No, he must first resign.
Yes, but only in a public company.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 283(b) of CAMA 2020 disqualifies a person of unsound mind "found by a court... or elsewhere", which is interpreted to include medical findings for directorship [66].

quizzes.scenario: Which theory of corporate governance posits that directors are agents of the shareholders and must act in their best interest?
options: Stewardship Theory.
Stakeholder Theory.
Agency Theory.
Resource Dependency Theory.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Agency Theory views directors as agents who must carry out the wishes and best interests of their principals, the shareholders [67, 68].

quizzes.scenario: Under the SEC Code of Corporate Governance for public companies, how often should the board meet?
options: Once a month.
Once a quarter.
Once every six months.
Once a year.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The SEC Code for public companies and the Code for banks generally require board meetings to be held at least once every quarter [45, 69].

quizzes.scenario: A public company must have what fraction of its board as independent directors under Section 275 of CAMA 2020?
options: One-half.
One-third.
At least three directors.
At least one director.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 275(1) of CAMA 2020 explicitly requires every public company to have at least three independent directors [46, 70].

quizzes.scenario: For a private company that is not a small company, who has the primary responsibility to ensure a secretary is appointed?
options: The shareholders.
The Board of Directors.
The Chairman.
The Managing Director.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 333(1) of CAMA 2020 vests the power of appointment of a subsequent company secretary in the board of directors [25, 26].

quizzes.scenario: A public company secretary's removal is being debated. If the ground is fraud, can the board remove her immediately after her failed defense?
options: No, they must wait for the AGM.
Yes, the board can remove her and report to the next general meeting.
No, they must get a court order.
Yes, but only if the CAC approves.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 333(4) of CAMA 2020 allows the board to remove a secretary abruptly if the ground is fraud or serious misconduct, provided they report it to the next AGM [27, 54].

quizzes.scenario: A director of 72 years is appointed to a public company without his age being disclosed in the special notice. What is the status of his appointment?
options: It is valid.
It is voidable by the board.
He is liable to a penalty, but the appointment may stand if validated.
He is automatically disqualified for life.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Failure to disclose age is a breach of Section 282, which carries a penalty under Section 278, though the appointment itself follows specific validation rules [60].

quizzes.scenario: Who among the following is not entitled to receive notice of a general meeting in a public company under CAMA 2020?
options: Every member.
The Auditor.
The Company Secretary.
Every employee of the company.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 243(1) of CAMA 2020 lists those entitled to notice, including members, directors, auditors, the secretary (in PLCs), and the CAC (in PLCs), but not general employees [71].

quizzes.scenario: The Board of Directors of Zandora Ltd, a private company, has just concluded its first meeting after incorporation. They are deliberating on the requirement to set a date for the company's financial year-end.
options: The directors must set the date at their first meeting and notify the CAC within 14 days.
The directors have 6 months to decide on the date.
The date is automatically December 31st of every year.
Only public companies are required to set a financial year-end.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 377(4) of CAMA 2020 provides that directors shall at their first meeting determine the date to which the financial statements shall be made up and shall, within 14 days of the determination, notify the Commission of that date. [1], [2]

quizzes.scenario: GreenLife Ltd has been operating for 7 years. The company secretary suggests disposing of all accounting records from the first three years of operations to create space in the archive.
options: Records must be kept for at least 10 years.
Records must be kept for at least 6 years from the date they were made.
Records can be disposed of after the annual audit.
Only public companies are required to preserve records for more than 2 years.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 375(2) of CAMA 2020, accounting records shall be preserved by the company for a period of at least six years from the date on which they were made. [3]

quizzes.scenario: Chief Okafor, a director in Zenith PLC, wishes to inspect the company's accounting records at the registered office on a Tuesday morning. The company secretary refuses, stating only the auditor can see them.
options: The secretary is correct; only auditors have a right of access.
Any member of the public can inspect records.
All officers of the company, including directors, have a right to inspect records at all times.
Only shareholders with more than 10% stake can inspect records.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 375(1) of CAMA 2020, accounting records shall at all times be open to inspection by the officers of the company. Section 868 defines officers to include directors and secretaries. [3]

quizzes.scenario: BlueRay Ltd, a private company, is preparing its annual financial statements. The accountant insists on including a statement of cash flow and a five-year financial summary.
options: These are mandatory for all companies under CAMA.
Private companies are exempted from including a statement of cash flow and a five-year financial summary.
