quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Rule 3(2) of the Companies Proceedings Rules, applications for an order extending the time for delivery of any document required to be delivered to the Commission must be made by originating motion [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: A dispute has arisen between two capital market operators regarding a transaction on the floor of the Nigerian Exchange. Before approaching the IST, they consider using an Administrative Proceedings Committee. Which agency's APC should they approach?
options: Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC)
Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN)
Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS)
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Administrative Proceeding Committee of the SEC is a quasi-judicial body set up to hear perceived contraventions of the ISA or SEC Rules by capital market operators [26, 27].

quizzes.scenario: The APC of the CAC is hearing a matter. The Chairman and three other members are present. A decision is reached where two members vote in favor and two vote against. How is this tie resolved?
options: The matter is adjourned for a full committee
The Chairman has a second or casting vote
The status quo remains
The Minister makes the final decision
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In case of a tie in the Administrative Proceedings Committee, the chairman or the member presiding is entitled to a casting vote [4, 16].

quizzes.scenario: An application is made under Section 358 of CAMA 2020 for an order declaring that the affairs of a company ought to be investigated by an inspector appointed by the CAC. What is the correct mode of application?
options: Petition
Originating Summons
Originating Motion
Ex-parte Motion
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Rule 3(4) of the Companies Proceedings Rules, applications for an order declaring that the affairs of a company ought to be investigated by an inspector must be made by originating motion [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign investor in a Nigerian enterprise has a dispute with the Federal Government. They cannot reach an amicable settlement and disagree on the method of dispute resolution to adopt. What rules apply in this scenario?
options: The CAMA 2020 Rules
The IST Procedure Rules
The International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID) Rules
The Federal High Court Rules
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 26 of the NIPC Act, if there is a disagreement between an investor and the Federal Government as to the method of dispute settlement to adopt, the ICSID Rules shall apply [28, 29].

quizzes.scenario: The Administrative Proceedings Committee of the CAC wants to include a representative from the Association of Shareholders in its meeting to gain more insight. In what capacity can this representative participate?
options: As a voting member
As a co-opted person (observer) without voting rights
As the Secretary of the Committee
As the Vice-Chairman
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 851(2) of CAMA 2020, the Committee may co-opt representatives of associations like shareholders to sit as observers, but they do not have the right to vote [4, 30].

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to cancel a variation of rights attached to its preference shares after a class meeting. What is the required mode of commencement for the aggrieved shareholders to challenge this?
options: Originating Motion
Originating Summons
Petition
Writ of Summons
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Rule 4(7) of the Companies Proceedings Rules, an application to cancel any variation or abrogation of the rights attached to any class of shares must be made by petition [1, 24].

quizzes.scenario: A person is dissatisfied with a decision of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and intends to appeal. Within how many days of receiving the order must the appeal be filed at the IST?
options: 14 days
21 days
30 days
60 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Appeals against the decisions of the SEC must be filed at the Investment and Securities Tribunal within 30 days of receiving the order [12, 22].

quizzes.scenario: A dispute arises concerning the appointment of a trustee in an Incorporated Trustee association. The matter is referred to the APC of the CAC. Is this within the APC's jurisdiction?
options: No, the APC only deals with private companies
Yes, the APC's jurisdiction includes disputes concerning the appointment or removal of trustees of incorporated trustees
No, such matters must go to the IST
Yes, but only if the association is for-profit
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Regulation 38 of the Companies Regulation 2021 provides that the APC of the CAC has jurisdiction over non-criminal disputes concerning the appointment or removal of trustees of incorporated trustees [23, 31].

quizzes.scenario: A public company has been struck off the register by the CAC. An aggrieved member seeks an order to restore the company's name to the register. What is the correct mode of application?
options: Originating Motion
Originating Summons
Petition
Writ of Summons
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 4(9) of the Companies Proceedings Rules provides that an application for an order restoring the name of a company to the register after it has been struck out must be made by petition [24].

quizzes.scenario: The Investment and Securities Tribunal (IST) consists of 12 members. What is the minimum number of members required to form a quorum at any sitting of the Tribunal?
options: Five members
Seven members
Three members
Six members
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The quorum for any sitting of the Investment and Securities Tribunal is three members [32].

quizzes.scenario: The APC of the CAC is adjudicating a matter. Which evidence-related law should guide its proceedings?
options: The CAMA Evidence Rules
The Federal High Court Rules
The Evidence Act
The Criminal Procedure Act
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Regulation 45 of the Companies Regulations 2021, the proceedings of the Administrative Proceedings Committee shall be guided by the provisions of the Evidence Act [11].

quizzes.scenario: A company was dissolved without being wound up, and a member wants the court to declare that dissolution void under Section 691(1) of CAMA. How should this be commenced?
options: Petition
Originating Summons
Originating Motion
Writ of Summons
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 3(7) of the Companies Proceedings Rules specifies that an application for an order declaring a dissolution of a company which has not been wound up to have been void must be made by originating motion [2].

quizzes.scenario: A Capital Market Operator intends to institute an action at the IST against the SEC. What is the statutory requirement regarding notice to the SEC before commencing the action?
options: No notice is required
14 days' written notice of intention to sue
30 days' pre-action notice
7 days' verbal notice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A person intending to institute an action against the SEC must give the Commission 14 days' notice in writing of their intention to do so [12, 22].

quizzes.scenario: The APC of the CAC has a legal practitioner as its Secretary. What is the minimum post-call experience required for this Secretary?
options: 5 years
8 years
10 years
15 years
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Administrative Proceedings Committee of the CAC shall have a legal practitioner secretary of at least 10 years experience [4].

quizzes.scenario: An officer of a company is facing potential liability and applies to the court for relief under Section 738 of CAMA 2020. What is the correct mode of commencement?
options: Originating Motion
Originating Summons
Petition
Writ of Summons
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 4(10) of the Companies Proceedings Rules provides that an application for relief from liability of an officer of a company under Section 738 must be made by petition [24].

quizzes.scenario: Who is responsible for appointing the members of the Investment and Securities Tribunal (IST)?
options: The Chief Justice of Nigeria
The President of Nigeria
The Minister of Finance
The Chairman of the SEC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The members of the Investment and Securities Tribunal are appointed by the President on the recommendation of the Minister of Finance [7].

quizzes.scenario: The APC of the CAC decides to recommend a director for criminal prosecution because the evidence revealed fraud. Does the APC have the power to prosecute the director itself?
options: Yes, it has special criminal powers
No, it can only make recommendations for criminal prosecution
Yes, but only if the Registrar-General consents
No, it must refer the matter to the IST
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 851(10) of CAMA 2020, the Administrative Proceedings Committee can only make recommendations for criminal prosecution if matters revealed criminal conduct; it cannot prosecute [23, 33].

quizzes.scenario: A private company has defaulted in complying with the conditions that constitute it as a private company. A member applies to the court for relief from the consequences of this default. What is the correct mode?
options: Petition
Originating Summons
Originating Motion
Writ of Summons
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Rule 3(1) of the Companies Proceedings Rules, applications for an order that a company be relieved from the consequences of default in complying with conditions of a private company must be made by originating motion [1, 34].

quizzes.scenario: For a dispute to be validly settled under the NIPC Act's ADR mechanisms, what condition must be met regarding the parties involved?
options: Both parties must be Nigerian
The dispute must concern any level of government (Federal, State, or LGA) and an investor
It must be a criminal dispute
The dispute must involve an amount above 100 million Naira
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: For an investment dispute to be tenable under the NIPC Act, it must concern any level of government and relate to an enterprise in Nigeria (FDI or FPI) [18].

quizzes.scenario: Every court process for corporate proceedings must follow a specific titling format. What must be included in the title according to the rules?
options: Only the name of the plaintiff and defendant
The name of the company and "In the Matter of the Companies and Allied Matters Act"
Only the RC number of the company
The name of the Judge hearing the matter
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 21(1) of the Companies Proceedings Rules requires every originating process to be titled "In the matter of (name of company) and in the matter of the Companies and Allied Matters Act" [35, 36].

quizzes.scenario: A capital market operator is aggrieved by a disciplinary action taken by the Nigerian Exchange (a self-regulatory organization). Which body has the jurisdiction to review this action?
options: Federal High Court
Administrative Proceeding Committee of the SEC
The CAC
The Supreme Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The APC of the SEC has the jurisdiction to review disciplinary actions taken by security exchanges (SROs) against their members [26, 27].

quizzes.scenario: The Administrative Proceedings Committee of the CAC is composed of 7 members. One of these must be a representative of which Federal Ministry?
options: Ministry of Finance
Ministry of Justice
Ministry of Industry, Trade and Investment
Ministry of Labor
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 851(1)(c) of CAMA 2020 specifies that one representative of the Federal Ministry of Industry, Trade and Investment, not below the rank of Director, shall be a member [4, 30].

quizzes.scenario: A member of a company wants the court to confirm a reduction of the share capital of the company under Section 131(1) of CAMA. How should this application be brought?
options: Originating Motion
Originating Summons
Petition
Writ of Summons
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Rule 4(6) of the Companies Proceedings Rules, an application to confirm a reduction of the share capital of a company must be made by petition [1, 24].

quizzes.scenario: A Chairman of the IST is being appointed. What is the statutory requirement for his professional qualification?
options: A retired Justice of the Court of Appeal
A legal practitioner with at least 15 years post-call experience in capital markets
An accountant with 20 years experience
A former Governor of the Central Bank
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Chairman of the Investment and Securities Tribunal must be a full-time legal practitioner with at least 15 years post-call experience in capital markets [7].

quizzes.scenario: Service of an originating summons on a company is being attempted. The bailiff delivers it to the receptionist at a branch office. Is this valid service under the rules?
options: Yes, service at any branch is valid
No, it must be delivered to a director, secretary, or principal officer, or at the registered office
Yes, provided the receptionist signs for it
No, service can only be done via the CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 104 of CAMA and Order 6 Rule 8 of the Federal High Court Rules, service on a company must be at its registered office or by delivery to a director, secretary, or other principal officer [37, 38].

quizzes.scenario: The Investment and Securities Tribunal (IST) is hearing a case involving a merger between two public companies. Which procedural rules should guide the Tribunal's proceedings?
options: Companies Proceedings Rules 1992
Federal High Court Civil Procedure Rules
Investment and Securities Tribunal (Procedure) Rules
Companies Winding up Rules
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The procedural rules of the Investment and Securities Tribunal are governed by the Investment and Securities Tribunal (Procedure) Rules [7, 39].

quizzes.scenario: A dispute concerning shares in a public company is brought before the Administrative Proceedings Committee of the CAC. Does the APC have jurisdiction to hear this?
options: Yes, it has jurisdiction over all share disputes
No, it only has jurisdiction over shares in private companies
Yes, but only if the public company is not listed
No, such matters are for the FIRS
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Regulation 38 of the Companies Regulation 2021 limits the APC of the CAC's jurisdiction regarding shares to those in private companies [23, 31].

quizzes.scenario: A company seeks an order to cancel the alteration of a condition contained in its memorandum under Section 52(1) of CAMA. What is the required mode of commencement?
options: Originating Motion
Originating Summons
Petition
Writ of Summons
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 4(2) of the Companies Proceedings Rules specifies that an application to cancel the alteration of a condition in a company's memorandum must be made by petition [40].

quizzes.scenario: A party intends to use arbitration to resolve an investment dispute with a government agency under the NIPC Act. If the investor is a Nigerian, which law governs the arbitration?
options: The English Arbitration Act
Any bilateral agreement
The Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023
The CAMA 2020
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: For a Nigerian investor, the arbitration shall be in accordance with the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023 (formerly ACA) [41, 42].

quizzes.scenario: The CAC wants to apply for an order that the affairs of a company be investigated because it suspects fraud. The application is made by originating motion. Is this correct?
options: Yes, Rule 3 of the CPR requires an originating motion for investigation orders
No, it must be by petition
No, it must be by originating summons
Yes, but only if the company is public
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 3(4) of the Companies Proceedings Rules and Section 358 of CAMA 2020 provide that applications for investigation of a company's affairs must be made by originating motion [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: An aggrieved party is appealing a decision of the IST to the Court of Appeal. On what grounds can this appeal be made?
options: Only on points of fact
On points of law or mixed law and fact for final decisions
Only on interlocutory decisions of fact
Ground of amount of money involved
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Appeals to the Court of Appeal from the IST can be on points of law for interlocutory decisions, and on points of law or mixed law and fact for final decisions [12, 13].

quizzes.scenario: The APC of the CAC includes five representatives from the operational departments of the Commission. What is the minimum rank required for these representatives?
options: Assistant Manager
Deputy Director
Not below the rank of Director
Registrar
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 851(1)(b) of CAMA 2020 requires these representatives to be not below the rank of a Director [4, 5].

quizzes.scenario: A creditor wants to apply for an order removing restrictions on the debentures of a company under Section 372 of CAMA. How should the application be commenced?
options: Petition
Originating Summons
Originating Motion
Writ of Summons
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Rule 3(6) of the Companies Proceedings Rules, an application for an order removing restrictions on shares or debentures must be made by originating motion [1, 40].

quizzes.scenario: A capital market dispute is being heard by the IST. The operator argues that owing to the complexity of the matter, it should be heard by the Federal High Court instead. What is the Supreme Court's position?
options: The Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction over all SEC-related matters
The IST has exclusive jurisdiction over capital market disputes due to their complexity and specialized nature
The operators can choose either the IST or FHC
The State High Court has the final say
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In Ajayi v SEC, the Supreme Court affirmed the exclusive jurisdiction of the IST over capital market disputes, rationalized by the need for a specialized court to handle their complexity [17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: Blue Ridge Industries Ltd is unable to pay its trade creditors as their debts fall due, although its total assets still exceed its total liabilities on the books. What specific type of insolvency is the company experiencing?
options: Cash-flow insolvency
Balance-sheet insolvency
Regulatory insolvency
Technical liquidation
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule under Nigerian corporate law is that cash-flow insolvency occurs when a company is unable to pay its debts as they become due, regardless of whether its total assets exceed liabilities [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: Zenith Tech Ltd has total assets valued at 50 million Naira, but its total liabilities and documented debts have reached 75 million Naira. Which category of insolvency does this represent?
options: Cash-flow insolvency
Balance-sheet insolvency
Commercial insolvency
Operational insolvency
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that balance-sheet insolvency occurs when a company's total debts and liabilities exceed the value of its total assets [2, 3].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Okafor, a legal practitioner with 3 years of post-qualification experience and a valid certificate from BRIPAN, applies to the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) for accreditation as an insolvency practitioner. Will his application be successful?
options: Yes, because he is a member of BRIPAN
No, because he lacks the minimum 5 years of post-qualification experience
Yes, because he is a qualified legal practitioner
No, because only accountants can be insolvency practitioners
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 705(b) of CAMA 2020 provides that a person is only qualified to act as an insolvency practitioner if they have at least 5 years of post-qualification experience in insolvency matters [4, 5].

quizzes.scenario: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) discovers that Mr. Musa, an accredited insolvency practitioner, has been convicted by a High Court for a crime involving official corruption. What is the immediate regulatory consequence?
options: He is suspended for six months
His accreditation is renewed with a fine
The Commission may revoke his authorization
He is required to work under supervision
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the CAC may revoke an insolvency practitioner's authorization if the holder ceases to be a fit and proper person, including convictions for fraud or corruption [6, 7].

quizzes.scenario: A leading accounting firm, Ledger & Co., is approached to act as an administrator for a failing manufacturing company. Can the firm itself be appointed as the administrator?
options: Yes, as a body corporate
No, only individuals can be insolvency practitioners
Yes, provided it is registered with ICAN
No, only legal practitioners can act as administrators
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that an insolvency practitioner must be an individual who meets the qualifications of degree, experience, and authorization; a body corporate is generally disqualified from acting in such capacities like a receiver or liquidator [8-10].

quizzes.scenario: The directors of struggling Apex Ltd propose a CVA to their creditors to reschedule debt payments. Who must supervise the implementation of this CVA?
options: The Board of Directors
A nominee who is a qualified insolvency practitioner
The Registrar-General of the CAC
Any senior management staff
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule under Section 704 of CAMA 2020 is that a CVA proposal must be submitted through an appointed nominee or supervisor who is a qualified insolvency practitioner [8, 11, 12].

quizzes.scenario: During a CVA process for Silverline Ltd, the nominee is required to submit a report to the court. What is the statutory timeline for this submission?
options: 7 days after the meeting
14 days of receiving the proposal
21 days of the creditors' request
28 days of receiving notice of the proposal
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that within 28 days of receiving notice of the CVA proposal, the nominee must submit a report to the Federal High Court [11, 13].

quizzes.scenario: The directors of Matrix Ltd, currently undergoing a CVA, wish to maintain control over the day-to-day management of the business while the arrangement is implemented. Is this permissible?
options: No, management must pass to the nominee
Yes, directors retain control during a CVA
No, management must pass to the creditors' committee
Yes, but only with court approval
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that unlike administration, a Company Voluntary Arrangement allows the company's directors to retain control over management while the business continues as a going concern [14, 15].

quizzes.scenario: A CVA is proposed for Delta Corp. The proposal includes a clause that would prevent a secured creditor, Mega Bank, from enforcing its fixed charge over the company's warehouse without its consent. Is this clause valid?
options: Yes, CVAs bind all creditors equally
No, a CVA cannot affect the rights of a secured creditor without their consent
Yes, if 75% of other creditors agree
No, only the court can restrict secured creditors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that a CVA proposal cannot affect the right of a secured creditor to enforce its security unless the secured creditor specifically consents to it [14, 16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: A minority member of Zenith Ltd believes the recently approved CVA is unfairly prejudicial to his interests. Within what timeframe must he challenge the CVA in the Federal High Court?
options: 14 days
21 days
28 days
30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 440 of CAMA 2020 provides that a CVA can be challenged in court within 28 days of the report of the meeting outcome [16, 18].

quizzes.scenario: The nominee of a CVA for Prime Ltd fails to submit his report to the court within the statutory 28-day period. What action can the proposer take?
options: The CVA is automatically cancelled
Apply to the court to replace the nominee with another qualified practitioner
Appoint themselves as the nominee
Proceed with the meetings without the report
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that if the nominee fails to submit the report within 28 days, the court may, upon application, replace the nominee with another qualified insolvency practitioner [16, 19].

quizzes.scenario: A CVA for Logistics Pro Ltd has been approved by the creditors' meeting but rejected by the members' meeting. What is the status of the CVA?
options: It is void and of no effect
It is fully effective despite the members' rejection
It is effective subject to a court order allowing the creditors' decision to prevail
A new proposal must be submitted within 7 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that where decisions differ between meetings, a member or creditor may apply to the court within 28 days, and the court may order the members' decision to prevail or issue other directives [16].

quizzes.scenario: The directors of insolvent FastTrack Ltd want to appoint an administrator to rescue the business. Which body has the jurisdiction to make such an appointment via an administration order?
options: The Corporate Affairs Commission
The Federal High Court
The State High Court
The Securities and Exchange Commission
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction over corporate proceedings, including the appointment of an administrator by court order [20, 21].

quizzes.scenario: A holder of a floating charge over the assets of SolarWay Ltd decides to appoint an administrator out of court. What is a primary requirement for the instrument creating the charge?
options: It must be under the company seal
It must state that Section 452 of CAMA applies to the floating charge
It must be witnessed by a notary public
It must have been registered within 30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that for a floating charge holder to appoint an administrator, the instrument creating the charge must state that Section 452 of CAMA applies to the charge [22, 23].

quizzes.scenario: An administrator is appointed for MegaBuild PLC. What is the primary legal objective the administrator must pursue first?
options: To liquidate the assets and pay the bank
To rescue the company as a going concern
To sell the company to the highest bidder
To lay off all employees to save costs
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 444(2) of CAMA 2020 states that the primary purpose of administration is to rescue the company, or any part of its undertaking, as a going concern [24, 25].

quizzes.scenario: Following the appointment of an administrator for G-Tech Ltd, a trade creditor attempts to institute a debt recovery suit against the company. Can the suit proceed?
options: Yes, creditors have an unfettered right to sue
No, there is an automatic moratorium on legal processes
Yes, if the debt is above 1 million Naira
No, unless the creditor is a secured creditor
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that administration grants an automatic moratorium, staying legal proceedings and protecting the company from creditor actions without the administrator's consent or court permission [26-28].

quizzes.scenario: An administrator is appointed for Apex Logistics. Within 14 days of appointment, the administrator must send a notice of appointment to whom?
options: Only the directors
Only the bank
The company and the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC)
The nearest State High Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that an administrator must, within 14 days of appointment, send a notice of appointment to the company and the CAC [29].

quizzes.scenario: During the administration of Foodies Ltd, the administrator requests a statement of the company's affairs from the directors. What is the statutory period for the directors to comply?
options: 7 working days
11 working days
14 days
21 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that a person required to provide a statement of affairs to the administrator must do so within 11 working days of receiving the notice [30].

quizzes.scenario: The administrator of RetailPlus Ltd determines that the company has insufficient property to pay all creditors in full. Does he still need to convene an initial creditors' meeting?
options: Yes, it is mandatory in all cases
No, unless requested by creditors holding at least 10% of the total debt
No, only the court can decide
Yes, but only with 28 days' notice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the administrator need not convene an initial creditors' meeting if the property is insufficient, unless requested by creditors holding at least 10% of the company's debts [31, 32].

quizzes.scenario: An administrator for TechFlow Ltd wishes to remove one of the company's directors who is obstructing the rescue process. Does the administrator have this power?
options: No, only the members can remove directors
Yes, the administrator may remove or appoint a director
No, only the court can remove a director
Yes, but only with the consent of 75% of creditors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that an administrator of a company has the power to manage the affairs, business, and property of the company, which includes the power to remove or appoint directors [31, 33].

quizzes.scenario: A company is currently in liquidation following a court order. Can a floating charge holder still apply to the court for an administration order?
options: No, liquidation is final
Yes, and if granted, the court must discharge the winding-up order
Yes, but only if the liquidator agrees
No, unless the debt is cleared
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that a floating charge holder can apply for administration during liquidation, and if granted, the court must discharge the winding-up order and specify the administrator's powers [34].

quizzes.scenario: A holder of a floating charge intends to appoint an administrator out of court for PowerGrid Ltd. There is a prior floating charge held by another bank. What must the holder do?
options: Nothing, they have priority
Give at least two working days' written notice to the prior holder
Obtain a court order
Pay off the prior holder first
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that a floating charge holder cannot appoint an administrator unless they have given at least two working days' written notice to any prior floating charge holder or obtained their written consent [22, 23].

quizzes.scenario: An administrator has been in office for TechSource Ltd for 12 months. What is the general rule regarding the duration of his appointment?
options: It lasts until the company is solvent
It expires after one year unless extended by the court or creditors
It is permanent until he resigns
It lasts for 6 months only
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the appointment of an administrator shall cease to have effect at the end of one year from the date it took effect, unless extended by the court for up to 6 months or by creditors' consent [35, 36].

quizzes.scenario: BlueChip Ltd defaults on a debenture secured by a floating charge. The debenture holder appoints Mr. Ben, the company's current auditor, as a receiver manager. Is this appointment valid?
options: Yes, because he knows the company's accounts
No, an auditor of the company is disqualified from being appointed as a receiver
Yes, if the court approves
No, only lawyers can be receivers
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 550 of CAMA 2020 provides that a director or auditor of the company is disqualified from being appointed as a receiver [9, 37].

quizzes.scenario: A receiver is appointed for Alpha Mills Ltd by the Federal High Court. To whom is the receiver primarily an agent?
options: The creditor who applied
The company's directors
The Court
The Corporate Affairs Commission
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that if a receiver is appointed by the court, they act as an agent of the court, whereas a receiver appointed out of court is an agent of the creditor [38, 39].

quizzes.scenario: Upon being appointed as a receiver manager for a company's charged assets, what happens to the powers of the company's board of directors regarding those specific assets?
options: They continue as normal
They are shared with the receiver
They are effectively suspended or in abeyance
They are transferred to the CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that once receivership is initiated, the powers of the board of directors over the assets covered by the charge are suspended, and the receiver assumes control [40, 41].

quizzes.scenario: A receiver manager is appointed for Delta Manufacturing. What is the deadline for the creditor to notify the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) of this appointment?
options: 24 hours
7 days
14 days
30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that once a receiver is appointed, the creditor is required to notify the CAC within 7 days of the appointment [40].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Tunde is appointed receiver manager for a failing hotel. He decides to continue running the hotel business to pay off the secured debt. What is his legal status while doing so?
options: He is a trustee for the members
He stands in a fiduciary relationship to the company
He is an employee of the bank
He is a partner in the business
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that a receiver manager of the whole or part of an undertaking is deemed to stand in a fiduciary relationship to the company and must observe utmost good faith [39].

quizzes.scenario: An infant, who is a genius in finance, is nominated by a debenture holder to act as a receiver for Zenith Ltd. Is this permissible under CAMA 2020?
options: Yes, age is not a factor for genius
No, an infant is disqualified from appointment as a receiver
Yes, if he has a guardian
No, unless he is at least 16 years old
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 550(a) of CAMA 2020 explicitly disqualifies an infant from being appointed or acting as a receiver or manager of any property of a company [9, 10].

quizzes.scenario: A receiver has been appointed over the entire undertaking of Alpha PLC. The company is required to provide a statement of its affairs. Within what timeframe must this be delivered to the receiver?
options: 7 days
14 days
21 days
28 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that within 14 days of appointment (or as directed by the court), the company must provide its statement of affairs to the receiver [42, 43].

quizzes.scenario: A receiver manager discovers that the assets charged are insufficient to cover the debenture debt. He wants to sell the company's uncharged delivery vans. Can he do this?
options: Yes, he has total control
No, his powers are limited to the assets covered by the charge
Yes, if the directors agree
No, unless he becomes a liquidator
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that a receiver assumes control over the company's assets covered by the charge, while any assets outside the charge remain under the control of the directors [41, 44].

quizzes.scenario: Ms. Ada has a degree in Accountancy and 6 years of experience in insolvency law. She is a member of ICAN but not BRIPAN. Can she be accredited as an IP?
options: No, only BRIPAN members qualify
Yes, members of ICAN are eligible for accreditation
No, she needs a law degree
Yes, but she must also be a member of NBA
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that members of recognized bodies like ICAN, NBA, ANAN, and ICSAN may be accredited as insolvency practitioners provided they meet the experience and degree requirements [45, 46].

quizzes.scenario: A CVA is in effect for Beta Ltd. The supervisor wants to seek clarity on a specific clause regarding asset distribution. What action should he take?
options: He must call a new general meeting
He should apply to the Federal High Court for directions
He must consult the Registrar-General of CAC
He should follow the directors' instructions
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the supervisor of a CVA may apply to the court for directions in relation to any matter arising under the arrangement [47, 48].

quizzes.scenario: The directors of struggling "Clean Water Ltd" decide to appoint an administrator out of court. They file a notice of intention to appoint with the CAC. What document must support this filing?
options: A bank statement
A statutory declaration of solvency
A statutory declaration that the company is or is likely to become unable to pay its debts
A special resolution
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the notice of intention to appoint an administrator by a company or its directors must be supported by a statutory declaration stating the company is or is likely to become unable to pay its debts [49, 50].

quizzes.scenario: A receiver manager for "Global Tech" fails to file his regular accounts and returns with the CAC. Who can apply to the court to compel him to make good this default?
options: Only the secured creditor
Any member or creditor of the company, or the CAC
Only the company secretary
No one, receivers are independent
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the court may, on application by a member, creditor, or the Commission, make an order directing the receiver to make good a default in filing accounts or returns [51].

quizzes.scenario: An accredited insolvency practitioner's authorization has just been revoked by the CAC. He was notified of this on the 1st of July. By what date must he have been notified according to the rules?
options: Within 24 hours
Within 7 days of the revocation
Within 14 days
Within 30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that where an insolvency practitioner's authorization is revoked, they must be notified within 7 days [6].

quizzes.scenario: A CVA is proposed for "Express Fly Ltd" while the company is already undergoing winding up by the court. Who makes the proposal in this scenario?
options: The Board of Directors
The Liquidator
The Official Receiver
The Chairman of the company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that where a company is in liquidation, the liquidator is the party authorized to initiate and propose a Company Voluntary Arrangement [11, 12].

quizzes.scenario: An administrator is appointed for "Build-It Ltd." During his tenure, he realizes that the purpose of administration cannot be achieved. What is his statutory obligation?
options: He must wait until the one-year term expires
He shall apply to the court to have the appointment cease to have effect
He must immediately start liquidation without notice
He should hand control back to the directors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that an administrator shall apply to the court for the appointment to cease if he thinks the purpose of administration cannot be achieved in relation to the company [36].

quizzes.scenario: "Future Pharms Ltd" has defaulted on a loan to "Equity Bank." The bank claims the company is insolvent because it hasn't paid for 4 weeks after a demand letter. What type of insolvency is being alleged?
options: Balance-sheet insolvency
Cash-flow insolvency
Asset-based insolvency
Dividend insolvency
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that cash-flow insolvency occurs when a company cannot pay its debts as they become due, often evidenced by a failure to pay following a statutory demand [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: A receiver is appointed over a specific piece of machinery owned by "PrintPro Ltd" under a fixed charge. Does this receivership suspend the directors' powers over the rest of the company's business?
options: Yes, all powers cease
No, powers are only suspended regarding the specific machinery charged
Yes, but only for 14 days
No, directors remain in full control of all assets
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that receivership assumes control over the company's assets covered by the charge, while any assets outside the charge remain under the control of the directors [41, 44].

quizzes.scenario: "Quick-Fix Ltd" is in administration. The administrator wants to use the company's common seal to execute a contract. Does he have the legal authority to do so?
options: No, only the directors can use the seal
Yes, an administrator has the power to manage the affairs and use the company's seal
No, the seal is retired during administration
Yes, but only with court supervision
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that an administrator manages the affairs, business, and property of the company and possesses powers such as using the company seal and executing documents [31, 52].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. James, an insolvency practitioner, has been appointed as a "nominee" for a CVA. What is his role once the arrangement is approved and implemented?
options: He becomes a director
He is referred to as the supervisor
He acts as the liquidator
He becomes the company's legal adviser
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the person supervising the CVA after its approval is referred to as the supervisor [48, 53].

quizzes.scenario: A CVA proposal for "SteelWorks Ltd" involves a modification that would rank an unsecured creditor higher than a preferential creditor. The preferential creditor does not consent. Is this modification valid?
options: Yes, if approved by the majority
No, a CVA cannot result in a preferential debt being paid out of priority without consent
Yes, if sanctioned by the court
No, CVAs cannot involve preferential debts
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that a CVA proposal cannot result in a preferential debt being paid otherwise than in priority to its non-preferential debts without the consent of the relevant preferential creditor [16, 54].

quizzes.scenario: A company is in administration. A landlord wishes to exercise a right of forfeiture by peaceable re-entry due to unpaid rent. Can the landlord proceed?
options: Yes, property rights are absolute
No, not without the consent of the administrator or permission of the court
Yes, if the rent is over 6 months overdue
No, landlords have no rights during administration
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that where a company is in administration, no landlord can exercise a right of forfeiture by peaceable re-entry except with the consent of the administrator or permission of the court [28].

quizzes.scenario: An individual with a degree in Law and 10 years of experience in corporate litigation, but no specialized experience in insolvency, wants to be an IP. Does he meet the experience requirement?
options: Yes, 10 years of law is enough
No, he needs at least 5 years of post-qualification experience specifically in insolvency matters
Yes, provided he passes an exam
No, only accountants meet the experience requirement
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that a person must have at least 5 years of post-qualification experience specifically in insolvency matters to be qualified as an insolvency practitioner [4, 5].

quizzes.scenario: "Apex PL" is in administration. The administrator issues a new invoice to a client. What must be stated on this invoice regarding the company's status?
options: Nothing special is required
It must state that the company is being managed by an administrator and give his name
It must state "In Liquidation"
It must be signed by the former managing director
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 482 of CAMA 2020 requires that business documents of a company in administration must state that its affairs are being managed by an administrator and provide his name [55].

quizzes.scenario: A receiver manager for "Blue Marine" has successfully realized the charged assets and repaid the secured creditor in full. What is his next statutory step?
options: He becomes the owner of the company
He must file a notice of cessation to act with the CAC
He must start a CVA
He remains as a director
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that once the receiver's role is complete, they must file a notice of cessation to act as a receiver or manager with the Corporate Affairs Commission [56, 57].

quizzes.scenario: The meetings for a CVA for "Green Fields Ltd" are being summoned. Who is entitled to receive a notice for the creditors' meeting?
options: All citizens of Nigeria
Every creditor whose claim and address the person summoning the meeting is aware of
Only creditors with debts above 1 million Naira
Only the top 5 creditors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the persons to be summoned to a creditors' meeting are every creditor of the company whose claim and address the person summoning the meeting is aware of [16, 19].

quizzes.scenario: A person who was recently declared an undischarged bankrupt by a court wants to act as an administrator. Is this allowed?
options: Yes, bankruptcy does not affect professional skills
No, an undischarged bankrupt is disqualified from acting as an insolvency practitioner/administrator
Yes, if he is a member of BRIPAN
No, unless the creditors agree
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that an undischarged bankrupt is disqualified from being appointed or acting as an insolvency practitioner, including roles as an administrator or receiver [9, 10].

quizzes.scenario: The administrator of "DataPro Ltd" determines that achieving the rescue of the company is no longer possible but he can achieve a better result for creditors than immediate winding up. Is he allowed to continue?
options: No, rescue is the only goal
Yes, achieving a better result for creditors is a valid secondary purpose of administration
No, he must resign immediately
Yes, but only with members' approval
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 444(2) of CAMA 2020 provides that administration may aim to achieve a better result for the company's creditors as a whole than would be likely if the company were wound up [24, 25].

quizzes.scenario: TechSolutions Inc, a company registered in the United States but not incorporated in Nigeria, discovers that a local Nigerian firm is using its proprietary software without a license. TechSolutions Inc files a suit at the Federal High Court for copyright infringement. What is the likely outcome regarding their capacity to sue?
options: The suit will succeed because foreign companies have automatic capacity.
The suit will be dismissed because a company not incorporated in Nigeria cannot sue for copyright infringement.
The suit will succeed if TechSolutions Inc provides a pre-action notice to the CAC.
The suit will be heard at the State High Court.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Nigerian corporate and IP principles, a company not incorporated in Nigeria lacks the capacity to sue for the infringement of its copyright. This is based on a combined reading of the Copyright Act and corporate law as seen in Pandum Technical Sales v Island Records [1],[2].

quizzes.scenario: Amina uses a generative AI tool to create a marketing jingle for her company, "Amina Designs Ltd." She provides a very specific and detailed prompt that shapes the entire structure of the output. Amina wants to register the work. Who is the author under Nigerian law?
options: The AI software developer.
The AI tool itself.
Amina, because she provided the prompt and exercised control.
No one, because AI works cannot have authors.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Nigerian law recognizes only human authors. The person who provides the prompt or exercises control over the output is regarded as the author, provided there is sufficient human intervention [3].

quizzes.scenario: A software developer creates a new application but does not register it with the Nigerian Copyright Commission (NCC). A competitor copies the code immediately. Can the developer seek legal remedies?
options: No, registration is mandatory for copyright to exist.
Yes, because copyright exists automatically upon creation and fixation in a tangible medium.
No, copyright only exists after a certificate is issued by the NCC.
Yes, but only if they register within 30 days of the infringement.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Copyright exists automatically upon creation and fixation in a tangible medium. While registration is advisable for evidence, it is not a prerequisite for the subsistence of the right under the Nigerian Copyright Act 2022 [4],[3].

quizzes.scenario: Z-Tech Ltd develops an autonomous AI system that generates music entirely on its own without any human input or prompts. The company attempts to register the music for copyright. Is the music registrable?
options: Yes, because the company owns the AI.
No, because copyright will not subsist where AI operates autonomously without human input.
Yes, because the music is original and fixed.
Only if the AI developer is a Nigerian citizen.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under current Nigerian IP principles, where AI operates autonomously without human input, copyright will not subsist because the law recognizes only human authors [3].

quizzes.scenario: A dispute arises between two tech companies in Lagos regarding the ownership of a specific computer program. The plaintiff files the action at the Lagos State High Court. How should the court respond?
options: The court should hear the case as it involves a commercial contract.
The court should strike it out for lack of jurisdiction, as the Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction over copyright matters.
The court should transfer it to the Magistrate Court.
The court should hear the case because both companies are in Lagos.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate matters arising from the operation of the Copyright Act and intellectual property rights in Nigeria [5],[6].

quizzes.scenario: "Fast-Track Ltd" registers its new logo with the Nigerian Copyright Commission. Six months later, it sues a rival for using the logo. In court, "Fast-Track Ltd" presents its registration certificate. What is the legal significance of this certificate?
options: It is conclusive proof of ownership that cannot be challenged.
It is a prima facie evidence of ownership.
It is merely a decorative document with no legal weight.
It entitles the company to triple damages automatically.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Registration with the Nigerian Copyright Commission serves as prima facie evidence of ownership and aids in the independent verification of authorship [4].

quizzes.scenario: An AI company trains its model using thousands of digital paintings scraped from Nigerian artists' websites without obtaining licenses. The artists sue for infringement. What is the primary legal issue?
options: The paintings were not registered with the CAC.
Use of copyrighted works for AI training without a license may amount to infringement.
AI has legal personality and can be sued directly.
The artists are not employees of the AI company.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Training datasets for AI that contain copyrighted works used without a license may amount to infringement, raising issues of consent and fair use [7].

quizzes.scenario: A group of developers creates an original game but fails to record it on any disk or server, only keeping the idea in their minds. They sue a company that releases a similar game. Will their suit for copyright infringement succeed?
options: Yes, because the idea was original.
No, because the work must be fixed in a tangible medium for copyright to subsist.
Yes, if they can prove they thought of it first.
No, because they did not incorporate a company.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: To be registrable or protected, a work must be original and fixed in a tangible medium. Ideas alone are not protected by copyright [3].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company, "Global-Music LLC," is an exempted company under Section 80 of CAMA 2020. It discovers its songs are being pirated in Nigeria and files a suit. What is its status in the litigation?
options: It can sue because it is exempted from registration.
It has the status of an unregistered company and cannot sue for copyright infringement.
It can sue only if it pays profit tax.
It can sue because it has a registered office in the US.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The status of an exempted company is that of an unregistered one. A company not incorporated in Nigeria cannot sue for the infringement of its copyright [1],[2].

quizzes.scenario: Which body is primarily responsible for the registration and regulation of copyright in Nigeria?
options: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC).
The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
The Nigerian Copyright Commission (NCC).
The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC).
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Copyright Commission (NCC) is the regulatory body responsible for copyright registration and enforcement in Nigeria [4],[8].

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to protect a new invention that is a technical solution to a problem. Should they apply for copyright?
options: Yes, copyright covers all inventions.
No, they should apply for a patent under the Patents and Designs Act.
Yes, if they write the invention down.
No, inventions are handled by the CAC.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Technical inventions are protected under the Patents and Designs Act, while copyright protects original literary, musical, and artistic works [6].

quizzes.scenario: An author publishes a book in 2024 and dies in 2030. How long will the copyright in the literary work generally last in Nigeria?
options: 50 years from the date of publication.
70 years after the death of the author.
Perpetually, like a company's succession.
20 years from the date of creation.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Nigerian Copyright Act, protection for literary works lasts for the life of the author and 70 years after their death (Outside the specific source text details but within general Nigerian Bar standard; the source mentions the Act as the principal enactment [6]).

quizzes.scenario: A company hires a freelancer to design a website. No written agreement is signed regarding the IP. Who owns the copyright in the website design?
options: The company, because they paid for it.
The freelancer, as the creator, unless there is a written assignment to the contrary.
The CAC, as part of the incorporation.
Both the company and the freelancer jointly.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Authorship initially vests in the creator. In a contract for services (freelancer), the creator retains ownership unless it is assigned in writing (General principle of the Copyright Act cited in the source as the principal enactment [6]).

quizzes.scenario: "Data-Drive Ltd" uses personal data of 1,000 citizens to train its generative AI without their consent. Which law must they primarily comply with to avoid liability?
options: The Companies and Allied Matters Act.
The Nigeria Data Protection Act, 2023.
The Investment and Securities Act.
The Immigration Act.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Compliance with the Nigeria Data Protection Act is mandatory for AI systems relying on large volumes of personal data to avoid unlawful processing [7].

quizzes.scenario: A director of "Soft-Dev Ltd" creates a code for the company as part of his official duties. Who owns the copyright?
options: The director personally.
The company ("Soft-Dev Ltd"), as the employer.
The Nigerian Copyright Commission.
The shareholders of the company.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Works created in the course of employment generally belong to the employer, subject to any agreement to the contrary (Principle of the Copyright Act [6]).

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian musician wants to sue a local radio station for playing his songs without payment. Where should he file his claim?
options: The High Court of the State where the station is located.
The Federal High Court.
The National Industrial Court.
The Investment and Securities Tribunal.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction over matters related to the Copyright Act [5],[6].

quizzes.scenario: "Alpha PLC" wants to verify if a screenplay it just bought is original and who the previous owner was. What is the best administrative step to take?
options: Conduct a search at the CAC.
Check the registry at the Nigerian Copyright Commission.
Apply to the SEC for investigation.
Publish a notice in the Gazette.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Registration with the NCC aids in the independent verification of authorship and tracing ownership [4].

quizzes.scenario: A tech startup is sued for using a "Fair Use" defense after copying excerpts of a competitor's manual for AI training. Who determines if this defense is valid?
options: The Corporate Affairs Commission.
The Federal High Court.
The Ministry of Trade and Investment.
The Nigerian Stock Exchange.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Federal High Court adjudicates copyright infringement cases and interprets statutory defenses like fair use [6],[7].

quizzes.scenario: "Zenith Books Ltd" is not incorporated in Nigeria but has a branch in Ghana. It wants to sue a Nigerian publisher for copyright theft. Can it proceed in a Nigerian court?
options: Yes, because it is an ECOWAS company.
No, a company not incorporated in Nigeria cannot sue for copyright infringement.
Yes, if it registers with the NIPC first.
No, because only individuals can sue for copyright.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Incorporation in Nigeria is a requirement for a company to have the capacity to sue for copyright infringement in Nigerian courts [1],[2].

quizzes.scenario: A developer creates a software tool using only open-source code with no original contribution. He applies for copyright registration. Is the work protectable?
options: Yes, because he put in the effort.
No, the work must be "original" to attract copyright protection.
Yes, once it is fixed on a hard drive.
No, because open-source belongs to the government.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: For copyright to subsist, the work must be original and fixed in a tangible medium [3].

quizzes.scenario: An artist creates a painting on a wall that is scheduled to be demolished tomorrow. Is the work "fixed"?
options: No, because the wall is temporary.
Yes, fixation is satisfied as it is expressed in a tangible medium, regardless of the medium's duration.
Only if he takes a photo of it.
No, paintings must be on canvas to be fixed.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Fixation requires the work to be expressed in a tangible medium of expression [3].

quizzes.scenario: A company secretary of a Public Company is tasked with ensuring the company's IP is protected. Which regulatory body is most relevant for copyrighting the company's annual report?
options: The SEC.
The FRCN.
The Nigerian Copyright Commission.
The CAC.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Copyright Commission is the body that handles copyright registration for literary works like annual reports [4].

quizzes.scenario: "Future-AI Ltd" develops a system that scrapes news articles to generate summaries. They are sued for copyright infringement. They argue the summaries are "new works." What is a major risk for them?
options: They didn't notify the SEC.
Use of the original news articles for training without a license may be an infringement.
The news articles are not original.
They are a private company and cannot own AI.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Using copyrighted works for AI training data without a license raises significant infringement risks [7].

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian citizen writes a song while living in the UK. Can he protect this work under the Nigerian Copyright Act?
options: No, he must be in Nigeria.
Yes, the Act protects works of Nigerian citizens regardless of where they were created.
Only if he registers it in the UK first.
No, copyright is only for companies.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Copyright Act protects works of Nigerian citizens (National eligibility principle under the Act [6]).

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to sue for "passing off" because a competitor is using a similar name and logo. Is this the same as a copyright suit?
options: Yes, they are identical.
No, passing off usually relates to trademarks/goodwill, though a logo can also have copyright.
Passing off is a criminal matter handled by the Police.
Copyright only applies to books, not logos.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: While they overlap, copyright protects the original artistic work of the logo, while passing off protects the brand's reputation [5].

quizzes.scenario: A poet recites a poem at a gala but never writes it down or records it. Someone in the audience publishes it the next day. Can the poet sue for copyright infringement?
options: Yes, because he created it.
No, the work was not fixed in a tangible medium.
Yes, if he has witnesses.
No, because poems are not protected in Nigeria.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Fixation in a tangible medium is a requirement for the subsistence of copyright [3].

quizzes.scenario: "Tech-Innovate Ltd" is a Nigerian company. Its subsidiary in London created a software. Can "Tech-Innovate Ltd" sue for infringement in Nigeria?
options: Yes, as the parent company.
Only if the London subsidiary assigns the copyright to the Nigerian entity and it is incorporated here.
No, because London is not in Nigeria.
Yes, because they share the same name.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The entity suing must be incorporated in Nigeria and hold the rights to the work [1],[2].

quizzes.scenario: A judge is presiding over a case where a plaintiff claims copyright in a "database" of facts. What is the rule for protection?
options: Facts can never be copyrighted.
The selection and arrangement of the facts must be original to attract copyright.
Databases are only protected as patents.
Only government databases are protected.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: For databases, the original selection and arrangement of data is what attracts copyright protection [9],[3].

quizzes.scenario: A company uses ChatGPT to generate its entire code for a new app without any human modification. Who owns the copyright?
options: The company.
OpenAI (the developer of ChatGPT).
No one, as there is no human intervention and the AI operated autonomously.
The person who paid for the ChatGPT subscription.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Where AI operates autonomously without human input, copyright will not subsist in Nigeria [3].

quizzes.scenario: An architect designs a building and is paid by "Build-Right Ltd." The contract is silent on IP. The company wants to use the design for another project. Can the architect stop them?
options: No, the company paid for it.
Yes, the architect is the author and owner unless there is a written assignment.
The local government owns the design.
Only if the architect is a director.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Ownership initially stays with the creator (author) unless assigned in writing, even if paid for in a contract for services [6].

quizzes.scenario: "Nig-Arts Ltd" wants to register its collection of digital arts. Which administrative head oversees the body they should apply to?
options: The Registrar-General of the CAC.
The Director-General of the Nigerian Copyright Commission.
The Chairman of the SEC.
The Governor of the CBN.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Director-General heads the Nigerian Copyright Commission (NCC), which manages copyright registrations [4].

quizzes.scenario: A filmmaker uses a famous song as background music in his movie without permission. He argues the movie is an "original work." Is he liable?
options: No, the movie is a new work.
Yes, using a copyrighted work in a new work without a license is an infringement.
Only if the movie makes a profit.
No, background music is always fair use.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Unauthorized use of copyrighted material in a derivative or new work constitutes infringement [7].

quizzes.scenario: A company finds that its trade secrets were leaked and published in a blog. Can they sue for copyright infringement?
options: Yes, information is always copyrighted.
No, copyright protects the "expression," while trade secrets protect confidential "information."
Yes, if the information was written down.
No, trade secrets are handled by the NAFDAC.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Copyright protects the original expression of ideas, not the ideas or confidential information itself [9].

quizzes.scenario: A developer creates a new programming language. Is the language itself copyrighted?
options: Yes, it is a literary work.
No, programming languages are systems/methods, though the specific code written in them can be copyrighted.
Only if it is registered with NOTAP.
Yes, but only for 10 years.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Copyright does not protect systems, methods, or languages, but rather the original expression within them [9].

quizzes.scenario: "Soft-Bank Ltd" discovers a former employee is selling the company's source code. What is the most effective immediate court order to seek at the FHC?
options: An order for winding up.
An injunction to restrain the former employee from further conduct.
A petition for oppressive conduct.
A criminal warrant for arrest.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An injunction is a common remedy to restrain a person from engaging in infringing conduct [10],[5].

quizzes.scenario: A company is in the "Negative List" under the NIPC Act. Can it own copyright in its internal training manuals?
options: No, because it is prohibited from doing business.
Yes, copyright subsists in original works regardless of the entity's business status, but it may face capacity issues in court.
Only if the manuals are about weapons.
No, the manuals belong to the Ministry of Defence.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Copyright subsists in original works, but a prohibited entity may face challenges in legal enforcement due to its corporate status [11],[12].

quizzes.scenario: Which of these is NOT a recognized category of work under the Nigerian Copyright Act?
options: Literary works.
Musical works.
Scientific theories.
Artistic works.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Copyright protects expressions such as literary, musical, and artistic works, but not abstract scientific theories or ideas [9].

quizzes.scenario: A blogger copies an entire article from a newspaper but gives full credit to the original author. Is this still an infringement?
options: No, credit avoids infringement.
Yes, acknowledgment does not substitute for a license if the entire work is used without permission.
Only if the newspaper is a Public Company.
No, articles are public property.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Acknowledgment is not a defense to using a substantial part of a copyrighted work without authorization [7].

quizzes.scenario: "Edu-Tech Ltd" wants to use a 100-year-old book in its app. Do they need a license?
options: Yes, copyright is perpetual for companies.
No, the work has likely entered the public domain as the term of protection (Life + 70 years) has expired.
Yes, they must pay the NCC.
No, only if the book was written by a Nigerian.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Once the statutory term of protection expires, works enter the public domain and no license is required [6].

quizzes.scenario: A startup uses a human-AI hybrid process to create a logo. The human designer selects the colors, fonts, and layout, while the AI generates the shapes based on those selections. Is this registrable?
options: No, AI was involved.
Yes, there is sufficient human intervention and control to establish authorship.
Only if the AI software is Nigerian.
Yes, but the AI must be listed as a co-author.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: AI-generated works are registrable where there is sufficient human intervention and control over the output [3].

quizzes.scenario: A company sues for copyright infringement but fails to provide a pre-action notice to the CAC. Will the suit be dismissed?
options: Yes, Section 17 of CAMA requires it.
No, pre-action notice is for suits AGAINST the Commission, not for IP suits between private parties.
Only if the defendant is the SEC.
Yes, all corporate suits require it.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pre-action notice under Section 17 of CAMA is required for those intending to sue the Corporate Affairs Commission, not for private copyright disputes [13].

quizzes.scenario: A foreign investor buys 100% of a Nigerian tech company. Who owns the copyright in the company's existing software?
options: The foreign investor personally.
The Nigerian company, as it has a separate legal personality.
The NIPC.
The US Government.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The company remains the owner of its assets, including IP, due to its separate legal personality [14],[15].

quizzes.scenario: "Media-Link Ltd" broadcasts a live football match. Does it have any rights?
options: No, only the players have rights.
Yes, it has "neighboring rights" in the broadcast as a broadcasting organization.
Only if the match is in Nigeria.
Yes, but only if the referee agrees.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Broadcasting organizations have specific rights related to their broadcasts under the Copyright Act [6].

quizzes.scenario: A student copies a chapter of a textbook for his personal research. The publisher sues. What is the most likely defense?
options: Lack of jurisdiction.
Fair dealing (or Fair Use) for personal research.
The textbook was not registered at the CAC.
The student is an infant.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Copyright Act allows for "fair dealing" for purposes such as private study or research [7].

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to use a competitor's copyrighted code to make its own software "interoperable." Is this allowed?
options: No, that is always infringement.
Yes, it may fall under specific exceptions for decompilation/interoperability.
Only if they pay the CAC.
No, unless the competitor is in liquidation.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Some IP laws provide exceptions for interoperability to promote competition [6].

quizzes.scenario: "Cloud-App Ltd" is a cloud service provider. Its users upload pirated movies. Is the company liable?
options: Yes, automatically.
It depends on "safe harbor" provisions and whether they took down the content after notice.
No, cloud companies are exempted from all laws.
Only if the movies are Nigerian.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Liability of service providers often depends on their knowledge and actions taken after being notified of infringement [8],[7].

quizzes.scenario: A photographer takes a photo of a public building. Does he own the copyright in the photo?
options: No, the building owner does.
Yes, the photo is an original artistic work.
The government owns it because the building is public.
Only if he registers it with the CAC.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A photograph is protected as an original artistic work [6].

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to protect its "Customer List." Is copyright the best option?
options: Yes, as a literary work.
Trade secret protection or a database copyright (if original in arrangement) is better.
No, customer lists belong to the SEC.
Yes, but only if the customers are directors.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: While it can be a database work, trade secret law is often more suitable for customer lists [9].

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian court is asked to apply a US copyright law rule in a local case. What is the rule?
options: The court must apply it.
The court is guided by the Nigerian Copyright Act 2022 as the principal enactment.
US law applies to all tech companies.
The court should refer the matter to the UN.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Nigerian courts are bound by Nigerian statutes, specifically the Nigerian Copyright Act 2022 [6].

quizzes.scenario: A company name is "Apple-Tech Ltd." It sues someone for using the word "Apple" in a book. Will a copyright suit succeed?
options: Yes, the name is copyrighted.
No, copyright does not protect short names or titles; they are protected under Trademark law.
Only if the book is about technology.
No, names are handled by the NAFDAC.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Copyright protection generally does not extend to names, titles, or short phrases [9].

quizzes.scenario: ABC Ltd owes a vendor N500,000. The vendor serves a demand notice at the registered office. After 14 days of non-payment, the vendor files a petition for compulsory winding up.
options: The petition is valid as the debt is due.
The petition is premature as the statutory period has not elapsed.
The debt is too small under the new BFA rules.
The court lacks jurisdiction over trade debts.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 572(a) of CAMA 2020, a company is only deemed unable to pay its debts if it neglects to pay for three weeks after the service of a formal demand letter under the hand of the creditor. [1-3]

quizzes.scenario: XYZ Ltd has its registered office in Lagos but has conducted 80% of its business and management operations in Abuja for the last 8 months. A creditor wants to file a winding-up petition.
options: Federal High Court, Lagos.
State High Court, Abuja.
Federal High Court, Abuja.
Court of Appeal, Lagos.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 570(1) and (2) of CAMA 2020, jurisdiction lies with the Federal High Court where the company has longest had its principal place of business (head office) during the six months preceding the petition. [4-6]

quizzes.scenario: The directors of PQR Ltd want to wind up voluntarily but are unable to make a statutory declaration that the company will be able to pay its debts in full within 12 months.
options: Members' Voluntary Winding Up.
Creditors' Voluntary Winding Up.
Compulsory Winding Up by Court.
Court-Supervised Winding Up.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Section 625 and 634 of CAMA 2020, if a declaration of solvency is not made and delivered to the Commission, the winding up is classified as a creditors' voluntary winding up. [7-9]

quizzes.scenario: During the winding up of JKL Ltd, funds are insufficient to meet all claims. There are unpaid workers, outstanding local taxes, and the liquidator's remuneration. Who has the highest priority?
options: Unpaid junior workers.
Federal Inland Revenue Service.
The Liquidator.
Secured creditors with floating charges.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 657 of CAMA 2020 and Section 647, all costs, charges, and expenses properly incurred in the winding up, including the liquidator's remuneration, are payable out of the assets of the company in priority to all other claims. [10-12]

quizzes.scenario: A liquidator is appointed for MNO Ltd in a members' voluntary winding up. The Managing Director, intending to save the company, signs a new supply contract the following day.
options: The contract is valid and binding.
The contract is voidable by the members.
The directors' powers have ceased upon appointment of the liquidator.
The liquidator must sign jointly with the Director.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 623 of CAMA 2020 (and Section 565(9) note) provides that on the appointment of a liquidator, all the powers of the directors shall cease, except so far as the liquidator or the company in general meeting sanctions their continuance. [13-15]

quizzes.scenario: SSS Ltd passes a special resolution for voluntary winding up on June 1st. A creditor, unaware of the resolution, files a compulsory winding-up petition on June 10th. When is the winding up deemed to have commenced?
options: June 1st.
June 10th.
June 14th.
The date the court makes the order.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 578(1) of CAMA 2020 states that where a resolution for voluntary winding up is passed before the presentation of a petition, the winding up is deemed to have commenced at the time of the passing of the resolution. [16-18]

quizzes.scenario: A company was formed solely to exploit a specific patent. The patent is declared invalid by a court, and the company has no other business. A minority shareholder petitions for winding up.
options: Default in delivering statutory report.
Substratum of the company is gone.
Inability to pay debts.
Fraudulent and illegal purpose.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 571(f) of CAMA 2020, the court may wind up a company if it is of the opinion that it is just and equitable to do so, such as when the main object (substratum) for which the company was formed can no longer be achieved. [19-21]

quizzes.scenario: Tunde acquired shares in ABC Ltd by transfer two months ago. He notices the company is being mismanaged and petitions the court to wind it up compulsorily.
options: He is eligible as a current member.
He is ineligible as he has not held the shares for at least 6 months.
He is eligible only if the MD is bankrupt.
Only the CAC has the locus to petition in this case.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 573(1)(a)(ii) of CAMA 2020, a contributory is not entitled to present a petition unless the shares were held by him and registered in his name for at least six months during the 18 months before the commencement of the winding up. [22-24]

quizzes.scenario: For a Creditors' Voluntary winding up of GGG Ltd, the directors must summon a meeting of creditors. When must this meeting take place?
options: At least 14 days after the members' meeting.
On the same day or the day following the members' meeting.
Before the Board of Directors meeting.
Within 3 months of the statutory meeting.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Per Section 635(1) of CAMA 2020, in a creditors' voluntary winding up, the company shall cause a meeting of the creditors to be summoned for the same day or the next day following the meeting at which the resolution for voluntary winding up is proposed. [9, 25, 26]

quizzes.scenario: A company has completed its winding-up process and distributed all assets. The liquidator applies to the court for a final order. At what point does the company lose its legal personality?
options: Upon appointment of the liquidator.
Upon passing the special resolution to wind up.
Upon the making of the dissolution order by the court.
Upon filing the final liquidation account with CAC.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 617 of CAMA 2020, a company is dissolved and its legal existence terminated only when the court makes an order that the company be dissolved from the date of the order. [27-29]

quizzes.scenario: After a petition is filed for the compulsory winding up of HHH Ltd, the directors sell the company's head office building to a third party.
options: The sale is valid if for fair value.
The sale is void unless the court orders otherwise.
The sale is voidable at the option of the liquidator.
The sale is valid if approved by 75% of members.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 576 of CAMA 2020 provides that in a winding up by the court, any disposition of the property of the company made after the commencement of the winding up is void, unless the court orders otherwise. [16, 30, 31]

quizzes.scenario: LMN PLC, a public company, fails to hold its statutory meeting within the period required by law. A shareholder petitions for a compulsory winding up.
options: The court must grant the winding-up order.
The court may order the meeting to be held instead of winding up.
The shareholder lacks locus standi for this ground.
Only private companies can be wound up on this ground.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 574(3) of CAMA 2020, on a petition based on default in holding the statutory meeting, the court may, instead of making a winding-up order, direct that the statutory meeting be held or the report be delivered. [22, 32, 33]

quizzes.scenario: QQQ Ltd passes a resolution for voluntary winding up. Within how many days must the company give notice of this resolution in the Official Gazette or two daily newspapers?
options: 7 days.
14 days.
21 days.
28 days.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 621 of CAMA 2020 requires that when a company has passed a resolution for voluntary winding up, it shall within 14 days give notice of the resolution by advertisement in the Gazette or two daily newspapers. [34, 35]
