quizzes.scenario: A client tells Barrister Tunde during an interview that he committed a murder three years ago for which someone else was wrongly convicted. The client does not intend to turn himself in. Can Tunde reveal this to the authorities?
options: Yes, because it is a serious crime
No, it is a past crime protected by lawyer-client privilege
Yes, but only with the Judge's permission
No, unless the client is currently being tried for that murder
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 19 of the RPC and Section 192 of the Evidence Act protect communications regarding past crimes. The exception for commission of a crime applies to future or ongoing crimes, not those already completed [7-9].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Chidi uses highly technical Latin terms like "consensus ad idem" and "caveat emptor" throughout an interview with a rural farmer who has little formal education. Which skill has Chidi failed to apply effectively?
options: Research skill
Advocacy skill
Verbal communication skill
Drafting skill
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Effective verbal communication requires using proper, positive, and simple common words, avoiding legal jargon or "legalese" that the client may not understand [9, 32].

quizzes.scenario: According to the "Law School Version" of the stages of a client interview, what are the five stages?
options: Listening, Questioning, Advising, Execution, Closing
Preparation, Beginning, Client telling story, Analysis, Conclusion
Starting, Gathering, Analyzing, Drafting, Ending
Welcome, Facts, Law, Fees, Goodbye
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Law School version uses the acronym PBCAC: Preparation for the interview, Beginning, Client telling the story, Analysis, and Conclusion [33].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Mike is interviewing a witness of the opposing party in a civil matter without the consent of the opposing counsel. He advises the witness not to talk to the other side anymore. Is this ethical?
options: It is ethical as it is a civil matter
It is ethical because he doesn't need the other lawyer's consent
It is unethical as he must not advise the witness to secrete themselves
It is unethical because he must always get consent to talk to any witness
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 25(1) of the RPC 2023 allows a lawyer to interview an opposing party's witness without consent, provided he does not hide the witness from the opposing party or advise the witness not to communicate with them [34, 35].

quizzes.scenario: During the analysis stage of an interview, Barrister Bola summarizes her impression of the client's story and asks the client if she has captured everything correctly. This is an example of what type of question?
options: Open-ended question
Hypothetical question
Mirror/Confirmatory question
Leading question
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Mirror or confirmatory questions are used by the lawyer to summarize their impression and confirm that they have correctly understood the information divulged by the client [27, 36].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Dare accepts a brief but, after the interview, discovers that the matter is in an area of law he has never practiced and knows nothing about. He decides to handle it alone to keep the entire fee. Is this ethical?
options: Yes, because a lawyer is a "jack of all trades"
No, he must associate with a competent lawyer unless the client objects
Yes, as long as he does some research
No, because he must only practice in one area
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 16(a) of the RPC 2023 provides that a lawyer shall not handle a legal matter which he knows or ought to know that he is not competent to handle without associating with a lawyer who is competent to handle it, unless the client objects [30, 37, 38].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Emeka, a legal practitioner, erects a large billboard in Ikeja with the caption: "Emeka & Co: We have won 95% of our cases in the last five years. Come to us for guaranteed victory."
options: This is proper as it provides accurate information to the public
This is improper as it includes a statement about the success rate of the lawyer's practice
This is proper because lawyers are now permitted to advertise under the 2023 Rules
This is only improper if Mr. Emeka is not a Senior Advocate of Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Rule 39(2)(d) of the Rules of Professional Conduct (RPC) 2023, a lawyer is prohibited from engaging in any advertising which includes statements about the quality of his work, the size of his practice, or his success rate [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer, Mr. Kunle, visits a local land registry to search for defects in the titles of various properties. After finding several irregularities, he contacts the property owners and offers to represent them in litigation against the government.
options: This is a proactive legal service and is professional
This is improper as it constitutes instigating litigation and searching registries for defects with a view to employment
This is proper if Mr. Kunle only charges a fixed fee
This is only improper if the property owners are already represented by counsel
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 47(1) and 47(2)(a) of the RPC 2023 provide that a lawyer shall not foment strife or instigate litigation, and specifically prohibits searching land registries for defects in title with a view to employment or litigation [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Bola enters into an agreement with a hospital ward attendant to pay him 10% of any professional fees earned from accident victims referred to her firm by the attendant.
options: This is a valid referral fee agreement
This is proper provided the client is informed of the arrangement
This is improper as it involves offering rewards to persons to influence legal work and engaging agents to follow up on accidents
This is only improper if the percentage exceeds 50%
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 47(2)(c) and (d) of the RPC 2023 prohibit a lawyer from engaging agents to follow up on accidents or offering rewards to any person to influence legal work in favor of the lawyer [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Tunde, a legal practitioner, distributes handbills at a busy bus park in Lagos, listing his firm's address and claiming to be "The most affordable and efficient lawyer for all criminal matters."
options: This is proper under the general permission for advertising
This is improper solicitation of professional employment via handbills
This is proper if the information on the handbills is accurate
This is only improper if Mr. Tunde is not in full-time practice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(3)(a) of the RPC 2023 explicitly prohibits a lawyer from soliciting professional employment, either directly or indirectly, through the use of handbills, circulars, or advertisements [7, 8].

quizzes.scenario: A law firm, "Justice Seekers LLP," creates a website comparing their professional fees with three other prominent firms in the same locality, claiming their services are superior and cheaper.
options: This is proper competition in a free market
This is proper if the comparison is based on published NBA fee scales
This is improper as it makes comparisons with or criticizes other lawyers
This is proper provided the information is not misleading
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(2)(c) of the RPC 2023 provides that a lawyer shall not engage in any advertising or promotion which makes comparisons with or criticizes other lawyers or other professions [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: Following a major airplane crash, Barrister Chidi immediately sends letters to the grieving relatives of the victims, whom he does not know, offering his legal services to sue the airline for damages.
options: This is a humanitarian act and is professional
This is improper as it constitutes seeking out claimants in respect of personal injuries to gain employment
This is proper if Barrister Chidi has expertise in aviation law
This is only improper if Barrister Chidi charges a contingent fee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 47(2)(b) of the RPC 2023 prohibits a lawyer from seeking out claimants in respect of personal injuries or any other cause of action with a view to being employed by the prospective client [4, 5].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Yusuf, a legal practitioner, publishes a brief biographical profile in a reputable Law Directory, including his name, address, schools attended, and his membership in the Nigerian Bar Association.
options: This is a form of prohibited advertising
This is improper solicitation
This is a permitted form of advertisement
This is only permitted for Senior Advocates of Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(4) of the RPC 2023 permits a lawyer to publish informative data about himself, including name, address, and academic qualifications, in a reputable law list or law directory [9, 10].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer, Mrs. Ada, appears on a national television program to discuss general principles of landlord and tenant law. During the show, she offers to provide free private consultations to viewers who call her personal number displayed on the screen to discuss their individual rights.
options: This is proper as it promotes legal education
This is improper as she accepted employment to advise on individual rights during a public program
This is proper provided she does not charge for the initial consultation
This is only improper if she is robed
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 46(1) of the RPC 2023 allows a lawyer to participate in media programs to give information on the law, but strictly prohibits accepting employment from such programs to advise on inquiries regarding individual rights [11, 12].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Segun, a sole practitioner, uses a business card that describes him as "The Legal Lion: Specialist in Winning Hopeless Cases."
options: This is proper as it showcases his specialty
This is proper provided he has won at least one "hopeless" case
This is improper as it is self-aggrandizing and misleading
This is only improper if the card is distributed to non-clients
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(2)(a) and (d) and 39(3)(e) of the RPC 2023 prohibit advertising that is misleading, includes statements about success rates, or constitutes self-aggrandizement [1, 9, 13].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer, Mr. James, relocates his office to a new address. He sends a dignified notice of this change to his existing clients and also places a small advertisement of the change in a local newspaper.
options: This is improper solicitation of new clients
This is a permitted form of announcement
This is only proper if he obtains permission from the NBA Branch Chairman
This is improper because newspaper advertisements are strictly prohibited
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 43 of the RPC 2023 provides that upon a change of address or telephone number, a lawyer may send a notice to his clients and may also insert an advertisement of such change in a newspaper or journal [12, 14].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Musa, an expert in Intellectual Property law, sends a brief and dignified announcement of his availability to act as a consultant to other legal practitioners in his city.
options: This is improper as he is soliciting from colleagues
This is a permitted form of announcement under the Rules
This is only proper if he is a Senior Advocate of Nigeria
This is improper as it should only be sent to the NBA National Secretariat
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 44 of the RPC 2023 permits a lawyer available to act as an associate or consultant to other lawyers to send a brief and dignified announcement of his availability to lawyers in his locality [12, 14].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Okoro, a lawyer, puts up a sign outside his office building. The sign is 15 feet tall, features flashing neon lights, and includes a large photograph of himself in full legal robes.
options: This is proper as it clearly identifies the office location
This is improper as the sign is not of reasonable size and sober design
This is proper provided he paid the local government signage tax
This is only improper if the photograph was taken before he was called to bar
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 41 of the RPC 2023 requires that signs or notices outside a lawyer's office must be of "reasonable size and sober design," and Rule 39(3)(c) prohibits procuring one's photograph to be published in connection with one's practice [13, 15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: While walking through a shopping mall, Barrister Jane notices a man arguing with a store manager. She approaches the man, hands him her visiting card, and tells him, "You have a great case for breach of contract. Call me and I will sue them for you."
options: This is professional zeal to protect a citizen's rights
This is improper solicitation by personal communication
This is proper if she offers to handle the case pro bono
This is only improper if the man is already represented by another lawyer
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(3)(a) and Rule 47(1) of the RPC 2023 prohibit a lawyer from soliciting professional employment by personal communication or proffering advice to bring a lawsuit without being consulted [3, 7, 8].

quizzes.scenario: A legal practitioner, Mr. Bitrus, writes a scholarly article for a law journal on the "Evolution of Maritime Law in Nigeria." At the end of the article, he lists his name and his professional qualification as "LL.B, B.L, Maritime Law Consultant."
options: This is improper advertising of his specialty
This is proper and permitted under the Rules
This is only proper if the journal is owned by the NBA
This is improper because he should only list his name
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 42 of the RPC 2023 provides that where a lawyer writes an article for publication giving information on the law, he may add his professional qualification after his name [12, 14].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Effiong is representing a high-profile politician in an ongoing corruption trial. During a break in the proceedings, he holds a press conference on the court steps, claiming his client is "100% innocent" and that the judge's previous rulings in the case were "biased and legally illiterate."
options: This is a proper exercise of the right to free speech
This is improper as it involves making extra-judicial statements likely to interfere with a fair trial and brings the profession into disrepute
This is proper if it is done to counter negative media reports about the client
This is only improper if the press conference was paid for by the client
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 33 and Rule 39(2)(b) of the RPC 2023 prohibit a lawyer from making extra-judicial statements likely to prejudice a fair trial or diminish public confidence in the legal profession [2, 17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: A sole practitioner, Mr. Ade, uses the name "Ade, Okon & Partners" for his law firm to give the impression of a larger, more established practice.
options: This is proper as it projects a professional image
This is improper and misleading as a sole practitioner shall not hold himself out as being in a partnership
This is only improper if "Okon" is not a real person
This is proper provided he notifies the NBA within 30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 5(4) of the RPC 2023 prohibits a sole practitioner from using a firm name that suggests he is in a partnership with others, such as by adding "and Co" or "and Partners" [19-21].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Sani, who is also a licensed real estate agent, runs a joint advertisement for his "Legal and Property Management Services," offering a discount on legal fees for any client who buys a house through his agency.
options: This is proper business diversification
This is improper as it involves practicing another profession simultaneously without GCB authority and is used to improperly attract business
This is proper provided the legal fees do not fall below the 2023 Remuneration Order scales
This is only improper if he is not a Senior Advocate of Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 7(1) of the RPC 2023 prohibits a lawyer from practicing another profession simultaneously with the law without authority from the General Council of the Bar, especially where it is designed to improperly attract business [22-24].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer, Miss Toyin, pays for a "Sponsored Post" on Instagram that features a video of her dancing in her wig and gown with a caption: "I am the baddest lawyer in town. My fees are the lowest, and I never lose. DM for your legal needs!"
options: This is proper modern marketing
This is improper solicitation and self-aggrandizement which brings the profession into disrepute
This is proper as long as the "baddest lawyer" claim is subjective opinion
This is only improper if she is not actually the "baddest lawyer"
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(2)(b), (d) and 39(3)(e) of the RPC 2023 prohibit advertising that brings the profession into disrepute, includes claims about success, or involves self-aggrandizement [1, 2, 9, 13].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister John agrees to pay a police officer a fixed sum for every suspect the officer allows him to speak with at the police station for the purpose of securing a brief.
options: This is a valid administrative facilitation fee
This is proper if the suspect is actually in need of a lawyer
This is improper solicitation through touts and influencers
This is only improper if the suspect is eventually convicted
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(3)(a) and Rule 47(2)(d) of the RPC 2023 prohibit soliciting through touts and offering rewards to persons who, by reason of their employment, can influence legal work [5-8].

quizzes.scenario: A law firm, "Global Law Chambers," prints its name, address, and the academic qualifications of its partners on its official envelopes and note-papers in a dignified font.
options: This is a prohibited form of advertising
This is proper and permitted under the Rules
This is only proper if the firm has at least 10 partners
This is improper because only the name of the firm is allowed
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 40 of the RPC 2023 provides that a lawyer may cause to be printed on his note-papers, envelopes, and visiting cards his name, address, and academic and professional qualifications [15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Ade, a legal practitioner, wins a high-profile case and immediately posts a sponsored ad on Instagram stating his firm has a "99% success rate in commercial litigation" to attract more clients.
options: It is a permitted form of advertisement under Rule 39
It is inaccurate but generally acceptable in modern practice
it is improper because statements on success rates are prohibited
It is allowed if the lawyer can provide evidence of the success rate
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(2)(d) of the RPC 2023 prohibits a lawyer from including any statement about the quality of his work, the size or success of his practice, or his success rate in his advertising or promotion. [1], [2]

quizzes.scenario: Lawyer A is aware that Mrs. X is already represented by Lawyer B in a land dispute, but Lawyer A proceeds to discuss a settlement offer directly with Mrs. X at her home without informing Lawyer B.
options: It is acceptable since it is for the purpose of settlement
It is a breach of duty to maintain good faith and fairness with a colleague
It is allowed if the client invited Lawyer A to the meeting
It is only allowed if the matter is non-contentious
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Rule 27(5) of the RPC 2023, a lawyer shall not have any dealing with a person represented by another lawyer in the same matter without giving prior notice to the other lawyer. [3], [4]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Benson, a legal practitioner, hires a group of young men to hang around police stations and the High Court premises to redirect litigants and detainees to his law office for a commission.
options: This is a creative way to ensure access to justice
This amounts to soliciting professional employment through touts
It is allowed if the touts are registered as paralegals
It is permitted if the commission is reasonable
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(3)(a) of the RPC 2023 prohibits a lawyer from soliciting professional employment either directly or indirectly through circulars, handbills, advertisement, or through touts. [1], [5], [6]

quizzes.scenario: A prosecutor, Mr. Idowu, realizes that a witness for the defense is telling the truth which confirms the innocence of the accused, but he chooses to suppress this information to ensure a conviction for the state.
options: His primary duty is to procure a conviction for the State
He is exercising his discretion as an advocate
He has breached his primary duty to see that justice is done
He should only disclose the information if the defense counsel makes a formal request
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 37(6) of the RPC 2023 provides that the primary duty of a lawyer engaged in public prosecution is not to convict but to see that justice is done, and the suppression of facts establishing innocence is unethical. [7], [8], [9]

quizzes.scenario: Chief Emeka, a Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN), and Mr. Balogun, a Life Member of the Body of Benchers, appear in court for different mention matters. Who has precedence to mention his case first?
options: Chief Emeka (SAN) takes precedence as the higher rank
Mr. Balogun (Life Bencher) takes precedence over the SAN
Precedence is determined by whoever arrived in court first
The presiding Judge decides who speaks first based on the cause list
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: By virtue of Section 6(3) of the LPA and Rule 26(2) of the RPC 2023, Life members of the Body of Benchers take precedence over all other legal practitioners, including Senior Advocates of Nigeria (SAN). [10], [11]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. John, a sole practitioner, decides to brand his law firm as "John, Obi & Co" to make his practice appear more established, even though Obi is not a lawyer.
options: It is a smart marketing move for a new practitioner
It is permitted if John plans to hire Obi as a clerk
It is a breach of rules against misleading names and unauthorized partnership
It is allowed as long as John is the only signatory to the account
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 5(4) of the RPC 2023 provides that a lawyer practicing alone shall not use a firm name with the addition of "and Co" or any other name capable of holding out his practice as a partnership. [12], [13]

quizzes.scenario: A legal practitioner, Miss Amina, allows her non-lawyer senior clerk to draft a Deed of Mortgage and sign Amina's name on it to help a client who is in a hurry.
options: Miss Amina has aided the unauthorized practice of law
This is efficient delegation of administrative tasks
It is allowed if Amina orally authorized the signature
It is allowed if the clerk has a Diploma in Law
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 3(1) and (2) of the RPC 2023 prohibit a lawyer from aiding a non-lawyer in the unauthorized practice of law or signing their name on a document prepared by a non-lawyer as if it were prepared by the lawyer. [14], [15]

quizzes.scenario: Lawyer A accepts a brief from a client who has just dismissed Lawyer B. Lawyer A takes the file and continues the case without ensuring that Lawyer B's outstanding professional fees are settled.
options: Lawyer A has no professional obligation regarding Lawyer B's fees
Lawyer A must use his best endeavors to ensure the former lawyer is paid
The former lawyer must sue the client directly for his fees
Only the court can direct a new lawyer to pay the old one
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 29(3)(b) and Rule 27(4) of the RPC 2023 provide that a new lawyer accepting a brief shall use his best endeavors to ensure that all fees due to the former lawyer in the matter are paid. [3], [4], [16]

quizzes.scenario: During a civil trial, Lawyer Z finds a very recent Supreme Court decision that is directly against his client's case. He decides not to mention it, hoping the other side hasn't seen it.
options: He is protecting his client's interest as a loyal advocate
He has a duty to be candid and must disclose the authority to the court
It is the sole duty of the opposing counsel to find unfavorable authorities
He should only disclose the authority if the Judge specifically asks
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 32(1) and Rule 37 of the RPC 2023 require a lawyer to be candid and fair as an officer of the court, which includes a duty to disclose decided cases that are against his client's position. [17], [18]

quizzes.scenario: Lawyer P knows that Lawyer Q represents the defendant. Since Q is ten minutes late for court and hasn't filed an appearance, P immediately moves the court to dismiss the defendant's counter-claim without calling Q.
options: This is an efficient use of court time to favor his client
This amounts to sharp practice and a lack of fairness to a colleague
It is the defendant's fault for hiring a late lawyer
This is required by the duty of dedication to the client
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 27(2)(c) of the RPC 2023 states that when a lawyer knows the identity of the opposing counsel, he should not take advantage by causing a dismissal without first inquiring about the other lawyer's intention to proceed. [19], [20]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Kayode, a legal practitioner, hears about a major gas explosion in a community and immediately goes there to distribute his business cards to the victims to encourage them to sue.
options: He is providing necessary legal awareness to the community
This is prohibited as instigating litigation and seeking out claimants
It is a permitted form of advertisement for his specialization
It is allowed if the victims are indigent and need pro bono help
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 47(1) and (2)(b) of the RPC 2023 prohibit a lawyer from fomenting strife or instigating litigation and specifically from seeking out claimants in respect of personal injuries to gain employment. [21], [22], [23]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Chidi is instructed by his client to bribe a court clerk to "lose" the file of a damaging witness so that the trial is delayed indefinitely.
options: Chidi must follow his client's instructions to win the case
Chidi must refuse and represent the client within the bounds of the law
Chidi should only do it if the client provides a written indemnity
Chidi should stay neutral and allow the client to handle the clerk
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 15(2) and (3)(a) of the RPC 2023 state that a lawyer must keep strictly within the law notwithstanding contrary instructions and shall not give service involving corruption of public officers. [24], [25], [26], [27]

quizzes.scenario: Lawyer X is asked by Lawyer Y to hold his brief in a complex murder trial. Lawyer Y hands over the bulky case file to Lawyer X only five minutes before the Judge sits.
options: Lawyer X should accept as a show of professional fellowship
Lawyer X should not accept if he cannot acquire an adequate grasp of the matter
Acceptance is mandatory because they are learned friends
Lawyer X must accept and then ask for an adjournment in open court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 27(3) of the RPC 2023 provides that a lawyer shall not hand over a brief to another to hold, and the other shall not accept, unless it is handed over in reasonable time to acquire an adequate grasp of the matter. [3], [20]

quizzes.scenario: A Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN), Chief Williams, is personally instructed to appear in a Magistrate Court to argue a motion for bail for his childhood friend.
options: He is demonstrating the dignity of the rank by helping a friend
A SAN is generally prohibited from appearing in an inferior court
He is allowed if it is a criminal matter and he is appearing alone
He must appear with a junior or another SAN regardless of the court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: It is a restriction under the rules that a SAN shall not appear before an inferior court, which includes the Magistrate Court. [28], [29], [30]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Tunde, a lawyer in full-time employment as a director in a Federal Ministry, maintains his private law firm, "Tunde & Associates," and acts as the Managing Partner.
options: It is a legitimate way to manage his private affairs
It is a breach of the Code of Conduct for Public Officers
It is allowed if he does not personally appear in court
It is permitted if he specializes in an area not related to his ministry
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The 5th Schedule of the 1999 Constitution prohibits a public officer employed on a full-time basis from engaging in the management of any private business, profession, or trade except farming. [31], [32], [33]

quizzes.scenario: Lawyer Bola writes a series of educational law articles for a legal journal and adds her LL.M and "Barrister-at-Law" titles after her name.
options: This is improper self-promotion and advertising
This is a permitted form of displaying her professional qualifications
She should only include her name to avoid aggrandizement
She must remove "Barrister-at-Law" as it is a qualification, not a title
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 42 of the RPC 2023 permits a lawyer who writes a book or an article for publication to add his professional qualification after his name. [34], [35]

quizzes.scenario: Lawyer Greg, representing a high-profile defendant in a pending fraud case, holds a press conference to claim the Judge is "manifestly biased" and the trial is a "sham."
options: This is his right to free speech on behalf of his client
This is a breach of trial publicity rules and potential contempt of court
It is allowed if the Judge actually appears biased
This is permitted if the comments are made outside the court premises
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 33 of the RPC 2023 prohibits extra-judicial statements likely to prejudice a fair trial, and Rule 31(2) requires complaints against judicial officers to be made to appropriate authorities, not the press. [36], [37], [38]

quizzes.scenario: A client decides to dismiss Lawyer A and hire Lawyer B mid-trial. Lawyer B promptly files a notice in court but tells the client not to speak to or pay Lawyer A.
options: Lawyer B is being loyal to his new client
Lawyer B has a duty to promptly give notice to Lawyer A
Change of counsel only requires notification to the Registrar
Notice is only required if the court orders it
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 29(3)(a) of the RPC 2023 requires that when a client changes lawyer, the new lawyer shall promptly give notice to the former lawyer. [39], [40], [16]

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer, Mr. Silas, agrees to give 20% of his legal fees to a local community leader who referred a lucrative corporate client to his firm.
options: It is a fair referral commission
It is a breach of the rule against sharing fees with a non-lawyer
It is allowed if the client is aware of the arrangement
It is permitted because it promotes the lawyer's business
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 3(1)(c) and Rule 53 of the RPC 2023 prohibit a lawyer from sharing his legal fees with a non-lawyer, as this aids in the unauthorized practice of law. [14], [41], [42]

quizzes.scenario: Lawyer Sani is in court and hears the court orderly read out an entry in the record of proceedings that is clearly wrong but favors Sani's client. Sani stays quiet to protect the advantage.
options: He is fulfilling his duty of dedication to his client
He has a duty to the state and court to ensure justice and must correct the record
He should only correct it if the opposing counsel objects first
It is the Judge's sole responsibility to ensure the accuracy of the record
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 1 of the RPC 2023 states that a lawyer shall uphold the rule of law and promote the course of justice, which includes an obligation as an officer of the court to be candid and fair. [7], [43], [44], [45]

quizzes.scenario: Chief Amadi, a legal practitioner with 14 years of post-call experience, is being considered for appointment as a Justice of the Supreme Court of Nigeria. Is he qualified for this position?
options: Yes, because he has more than 10 years of experience
No, because he requires at least 15 years of post-call experience
Yes, provided the Senate confirms him
No, because he must be a sitting Justice of the Court of Appeal first
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Section 231(3) of the 1999 CFRN, a person shall not be qualified to hold the office of a Justice of the Supreme Court unless he is qualified to practice as a legal practitioner in Nigeria and has been so qualified for a period of not less than 15 years [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: The office of the Chief Judge of Katsina State becomes vacant. The Governor appoints the most senior Judge of the High Court to act in that capacity for a period of five months without seeking further recommendation. What is the legal status of this appointment?
options: The appointment is valid for the entire 5 months
The appointment is valid only for the first 3 months
The appointment is void from the beginning for lack of NJC recommendation
The appointment is valid only if Katsina State House of Assembly confirms it
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 271(4) and (5) of the 1999 CFRN, the Governor can appoint the most senior judge to act as Chief Judge for a period not exceeding 3 months, and such appointment shall not be reappointed after it lapses except on the recommendation of the NJC [3-5].

quizzes.scenario: Justice Bello has just retired from the High Court of Lagos State at the age of 65. His son is being prosecuted for fraud, and Justice Bello wishes to appear as his lead advocate in court to save costs. Can he do this?
options: Yes, a retired judge can practice as an advocate for family members
No, he is constitutionally barred from appearing as a legal practitioner in any court
Yes, provided he does not wear his judicial robes
No, he can only appear as an advocate in the Magistrate Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 292(2) of the 1999 CFRN and Rule 6(3) of the RPC provide that any person who has held office as a judicial officer shall not, on ceasing to be a judicial officer for any reason, thereafter appear or act as a legal practitioner before any court of law or tribunal in Nigeria [6-9].

quizzes.scenario: The Governor of Kwara State is displeased with the Chief Judge's refusal to grant an injunction in a political matter. The Governor obtains a 2/3 majority vote from the State House of Assembly to remove the Chief Judge and proceeds with the removal. What is the position of the law?
options: The removal is valid as the House of Assembly has 2/3 majority
The removal is invalid because the NJC must recommend the removal first
The removal is valid because the Governor is the appointing authority
The removal is invalid because only the President can remove a State Chief Judge
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In Elelu Habeeb v. AG Federation, the Supreme Court held that a Governor and House of Assembly cannot remove a Chief Judge without the participation and recommendation of the National Judicial Council [10, 11].

quizzes.scenario: Mallam Yusuf is a distinguished Islamic scholar with a recognized qualification in Islamic Law obtained 13 years ago. He is not a lawyer. Can he be appointed as a Kadi of the Sharia Court of Appeal of the FCT?
options: No, only legal practitioners can be appointed as Kadis
Yes, provided he has 12 years of experience and is a distinguished scholar
No, he must have at least 15 years of experience
Yes, but he can only sit in the State Sharia Court, not FCT
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 261(3)(b) of the 1999 CFRN allows for the appointment of a non-lawyer as a Kadi if he has a recognized qualification in Islamic law for at least 12 years and is a distinguished scholar or has considerable experience in Islamic law practice [12-14].

quizzes.scenario: A petition is filed against a Magistrate in Ogun State for receiving bribes. The petitioner sends the complaint directly to the National Judicial Council (NJC) for disciplinary action. What is the likely outcome?
options: The NJC will investigate and remove the Magistrate
The NJC will refer the matter to the Legal Practitioners Disciplinary Committee
The petition will be dismissed for lack of jurisdiction as Magistrates are not judicial officers for NJC purposes
The NJC will direct the Governor to suspend the Magistrate
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Magistrates are not regarded as judicial officers under Section 318(1) of the CFRN; they are under the supervision of the High Courts and fall under the disciplinary control of the State Judicial Service Commission (SJSC), not the NJC [15-17].

quizzes.scenario: Justice Okoro, a Judge of the Federal High Court, is accused of gross misconduct. Who is responsible for recommending his removal to the President?
options: The Federal Judicial Service Commission
The Senate of the National Assembly
The National Judicial Council
The Chief Justice of Nigeria in his personal capacity
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Third Schedule, Part 1, Item 21(b) of the 1999 CFRN, the National Judicial Council has the power to recommend to the President the removal from office of judicial officers, including Judges of the Federal High Court [18, 19].

quizzes.scenario: During the process of appointing new judges for the High Court of Cross River State, the State Judicial Service Commission (SJSC) shortlists 5 candidates for 5 vacant slots. Is this in compliance with the NJC guidelines?
options: Yes, as long as they are all qualified
No, they must shortlist double the required number of candidates
Yes, provided the Governor approves the list
No, they must shortlist three times the required number
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to the NJC Guidelines and Procedural Rules for Appointment of Judicial Officers, the list of shortlisted candidates must be double the required number of judicial officers sought to be appointed [20, 21].

quizzes.scenario: Justice Ade, a sitting judge of the High Court, is offered the traditional title of "Balogun of his Hometown" by his community's monarch. Justice Ade accepts the title and holds a lavish party. Has he breached any code?
options: No, traditional titles are purely social and allowed
Yes, judicial officers are prohibited from accepting chieftaincy titles while in office
No, as long as it does not interfere with his court sittings
Yes, but only if the title comes with a salary
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Code of Conduct for Judicial Officers, a judicial officer should not accept any chieftaincy title while in office [22, 23].

quizzes.scenario: The President wishes to appoint a new President of the National Industrial Court. Which body is responsible for advising the National Judicial Council (NJC) in nominating a candidate for this specific office?
options: The State Judicial Service Commission
The Federal Judicial Service Commission
The Body of Benchers
The Nigerian Bar Association
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Third Schedule, Part 1, Item 13(a) of the 1999 CFRN, the Federal Judicial Service Commission has the power to advise the NJC in nominating persons for appointment to the office of the President of the National Industrial Court [24-26].

quizzes.scenario: Justice Martha of the Court of Appeal reaches the age of 65 and wishes to retire voluntarily. Is she eligible for voluntary retirement at this age?
options: No, she must reach 70 to retire
Yes, the voluntary retirement age for Court of Appeal Justices is 65
No, the voluntary retirement age is 60
Yes, but only with the President's permission
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 291(1) of the 1999 CFRN, a Justice of the Supreme Court or Court of Appeal may retire when he attains the age of 65 years, while the compulsory retirement age is 70 [27].

quizzes.scenario: A litigant is unhappy with a judgment delivered by Justice Danladi of the High Court and files a complaint of misconduct to the NJC 8 months after the judgment was delivered. What is the status of this complaint?
options: The NJC will investigate it immediately
The complaint will be dismissed because it was not made within 6 months of the event
The complaint will be referred to the Court of Appeal
The NJC will investigate it if the Chief Judge supports it
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Judicial Discipline Regulations 2017, a complaint against a judicial officer must be made within 6 months from the date of the event complained of, otherwise it will be dismissed [7, 28].

quizzes.scenario: The NJC concludes an investigation into a Justice of the Court of Appeal and decides to suspend him from office for 6 months as a disciplinary measure. Does the NJC have the power to do this without the President's approval?
options: No, only the President can suspend a Federal Judge
Yes, the NJC has the power to suspend judicial officers as a form of discipline
No, suspension must be confirmed by the Senate
Yes, but only for a maximum of 30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The NJC has the power to exercise disciplinary control over judicial officers, which includes the power to warn, reprimand, or suspend them from office [29-32].

quizzes.scenario: A legal practitioner with 11 years of post-call experience is being considered for appointment as a Justice of the Court of Appeal. Is he qualified?
options: Yes, the minimum requirement is 10 years
No, he requires at least 15 years
No, the minimum requirement is 12 years post-call experience
Yes, provided he is a Senior Advocate of Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 238(3) of the 1999 CFRN provides that a person shall not be qualified to hold the office of a Justice of the Court of Appeal unless he has been qualified to practice as a legal practitioner in Nigeria for a period of not less than 12 years [1, 33, 34].

quizzes.scenario: The Governor of a State removes a Judge of the State High Court (not the Chief Judge) upon the recommendation of the NJC due to proven misconduct. Does the Governor require the confirmation of the House of Assembly?
options: Yes, all judicial removals require legislative confirmation
No, only the removal of Heads of Court requires legislative address and confirmation
Yes, but only if the Judge is a Senior Advocate
No, legislative confirmation is never required for State Judges
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 292(1)(b) of the 1999 CFRN provides that judicial officers other than Heads of Courts can be removed by the President or Governor acting on the recommendation of the NJC without the involvement of the legislature [10, 35, 36].

quizzes.scenario: Justice Silas, a High Court Judge, is seen at a popular night club drinking heavily and engaging in a physical brawl with a patron. What is the nature of this misconduct?
options: It is a breach of the Legal Practitioners Act
It is a contravention of the Code of Conduct for Judicial Officers relating to social functions and personal behavior
It is not a misconduct because it happened outside the court
It is only a misconduct if he was in his robes
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Code of Conduct for Judicial Officers requires judges to avoid social functions that conflict with judicial functions and to maintain high standards of personal integrity and respect for the law [22, 37].

quizzes.scenario: The NJC is meeting to consider the appointment of new Supreme Court Justices. How many members of the NBA sit on the Council for this specific purpose?
options: Three members
Five members with at least 15 years post-call standing
Ten members nominated by the Body of Benchers
All members of the NBA National Executive Committee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Third Schedule, Part 1, Item 20(i) of the 1999 CFRN, the NJC includes five members of the NBA qualified for at least 15 years, who sit only for the purposes of considering appointments to superior courts of record [38, 39].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer in a trial believes the presiding judge is biased because the judge is a first cousin to the opposing party. What is the most appropriate first step for the lawyer?
options: Report the judge to the Police for corruption
File an appeal immediately before the trial ends
Apply to the judicial officer by Motion on Notice to disqualify himself
Write a petition to the President of Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A lawyer aggrieved by judicial misconduct has the option to apply to the judicial officer to disqualify himself from the case on grounds of likelihood of bias [40, 41].

quizzes.scenario: During the appointment process for a new Grand Kadi of the Sharia Court of Appeal of a State, who confirms the Governor's appointment?
options: The Senate
The National Judicial Council
The State House of Assembly
The Chief Justice of Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 276(1) of the 1999 CFRN provides that the appointment of the Grand Kadi of the Sharia Court of Appeal of a State shall be made by the Governor on the recommendation of the NJC, subject to confirmation by the House of Assembly [12, 42].

quizzes.scenario: A Justice of the Supreme Court has become mentally infirm and can no longer perform his duties. What is the procedure for his removal?
options: The President removes him directly upon medical advice
The NJC recommends his removal, and the President acts on an address supported by 2/3 majority of the Senate
He is automatically removed by the Chief Justice of Nigeria
The Body of Benchers will vote for his removal
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 292(1)(a)(i) of the 1999 CFRN, the Chief Justice of Nigeria and other Justices of the Supreme Court can be removed for inability to discharge functions arising from infirmity of mind or body by the President on the recommendation of the NJC acting on an address supported by 2/3 majority of the Senate [35, 43, 44].

quizzes.scenario: Chief Emeka instructs his lawyer, Tunji, to draft a bill to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in Nigerian banking. Tunji begins by researching the 1999 Constitution and existing banking regulations to ensure no conflict. Which stage of the drafting process is Tunji currently performing?
options: Receiving and Understanding Instructions
Analysis of the Instruction
Designing the Draft
Scrutining the Draft
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Analysis stage involves checking the proposed law for consistency with the Constitution and existing laws, as well as its practicability and potential danger areas [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: A drafter is preparing a bill for the National Assembly and writes the heading as "An Act to provide for the welfare of orphans...". An expert advises that for a bill, the format must follow specific preliminary rules. What is the correct opening phrase for the Long Title?
options: AN ACT TO PROVIDE
A BILL FOR AN ACT
BE IT ENACTED
WHEREAS
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A Long Title for a bill must start with the specific phrase "A BILL FOR AN ACT" in block letters for federal legislation [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: While drafting a bill for a new environmental agency, Barrister Musa wants to ensure the Commission has legal personality. He includes a section stating the Commission is a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal. Which segment of the legislation does this belong to?
options: Preliminary provisions
Miscellaneous provisions
Principal provisions
Final provisions
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Principal provisions include the establishment of bodies, their powers, and administrative provisions like corporate status and common seals [5-7].

quizzes.scenario: A drafter is defining "Public Officer" in a new statute. He wants to ensure that the definition is exhaustive and excludes any other category not mentioned. Which word should he use to link the term to its definition?
options: Includes
Means
Comprises
Extends to
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The word "Means" is used where the meaning is closed or restricted, while "Includes" is used where the meaning is open or inexhaustive [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: Provision A of a new Bill states it applies to all residents of Lagos. Provision B states that no part of the Act applies to diplomatic staff. If the drafter wants Provision B to take precedence over Provision A, which phrase should introduce Provision B?
options: Subject to
Provided that
Without prejudice
Notwithstanding
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The phrase "Notwithstanding" serves as a proviso that overrides any other provision, exhaling superiority [10, 11].

quizzes.scenario: A drafter is creating a bill to regulate pharmacies. Section 10(4) of the bill refers to a list of prohibited substances that is very long and technical. To avoid making the main body of the law clumsy, where should this list be placed?
options: Preamble
Marginal Notes
Schedule
Interpretation Clause
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A Schedule is used to banish technical details that would make the main body of the legislation unwieldy or clumsy [12, 13].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Dele is drafting a law for the State House of Assembly. He writes the enacting formula as "ENACTED BY THE NATIONAL ASSEMBLY...". What is the correct enacting formula for a state law in Nigeria?
options: THE STATE ENACTS
BE IT ENACTED BY THE GOVERNOR
ENACTED BY THE HOUSE OF ASSEMBLY OF [STATE NAME]
THE MILITARY GOVERNOR DECREES
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: For state laws, the proper enacting formula is "ENACTED BY THE HOUSE OF ASSEMBLY OF [STATE NAME] as follows:" [14, 15].

quizzes.scenario: A drafter uses the following sentence: "The application for a license shall be made by the applicant to the Minister." An expert suggests changing it to "The applicant shall apply to the Minister for a license." What principle of drafting does this change follow?
options: Avoiding archaic words
Use of the singular
Use of active voice
Avoiding negative expressions
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Draftsmen should avoid passive language and instead use the active voice (Subject-Verb-Object) to ensure the draft is intelligible and direct [16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: A new law is passed on June 1st but the drafter includes a clause stating: "This Act shall be deemed to have come into force on January 1st." What is the status of this clause in modern Nigerian drafting?
options: It is a standard Commencement Clause
It is a Retrospective Commencement and is generally unconstitutional
It is an Appointment Clause
It is an Enacting Formula
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Retrospective commencement dates, which deem a law to have started before it was passed, were common under military regimes but are considered unconstitutional and a feature to avoid in good legislation [18, 19].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Okoro is drafting a law and uses the phrase "said property" and "hereinbefore mentioned" repeatedly. Which drafting habit is he failing to avoid?
options: Intricate expressions
Use of Latin
Use of archaic words
Verbosity
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Draftsmen must avoid archaic words like "said," "aforesaid," "hereinbefore," and "witnesseth" to maintain contemporary and clear English [20, 21].

quizzes.scenario: A sponsor instructs a drafter to create a bill that would prevent any citizen from appealing a decision of the proposed Regulatory Board to any court. What is the drafter's ethical duty?
options: To follow instructions regardless of legality
To advise that the legislation should not oust the jurisdiction of the court
To include a Preamble explaining the ouster
To place the ouster in a Schedule
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A feature of good legislation is that it should not oust the jurisdiction of the court; the drafter has an ethical duty to advise the sponsor on such potential legal problems [22].

quizzes.scenario: In a new Bill, the drafter includes a section that says: "The Board may grant a waiver to any applicant who meets the criteria." The sponsor wants to make this waiver mandatory for the Board. How should the drafter change the word "may"?
options: Shall
Will
Might
Must
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: While "may" is directory and confers discretion, "shall" or "must" suggest an obligation and that the action is mandatory [23-25].

quizzes.scenario: A drafter is organizing a large statute like the Companies and Allied Matters Act. He group clauses into related groups identified by Roman Numerals. What are these groups called?
options: Chapters
Sections
Parts
Schedules
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Large or complicated legislations are segmented into "Parts" for ease of referencing and clarity, usually numbered in Capital Roman Numerals [26, 27].

quizzes.scenario: A drafter is finalizing a bill and notices a spelling error in the name of the established body. He also realizes he forgot to cross-reference a section. Which stage of drafting is this?
options: Design
Composition
Scrutiny
Analysis
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Scrutiny involves editing the bill for grammatical errors, punctuation, spelling, and cross-references, often by an independent eye [28, 29].

quizzes.scenario: A sponsor provides a drafter with a "Party Manifesto" and a "Report of Civil Societies" to help with a bill. At which stage should the drafter primarily consult these materials?
options: Design
Analysis
Receiving and Understanding Instructions
Scrutiny
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: During the first stage of receiving and understanding instructions, the drafter should look at background information such as party manifestos, reports, and government policies [1, 30, 31].

quizzes.scenario: A bill includes an introductory statement: "A Law to regulate the practice of traditional medicine in Lagos State." Which segment of the bill is this?
options: Enacting Clause
Short Title
Long Title
Preamble
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Long Title sets out the general purpose and scope of the legislation; it starts with "AN ACT" or "A LAW" (or "A BILL FOR..." for bills) [5, 32].

quizzes.scenario: A drafter wants to include a provision that the new law will only be valid for a period of five years. Where should this be placed and what is it called?
options: Principal Segment; Duration Clause
Preliminary Segment; Duration Clause
Miscellaneous Segment; Repeal Clause
Final Segment; Saving Clause
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Duration clause, which indicates the period a legislation will be in force, is part of the Preliminary segment [5, 33, 34].

quizzes.scenario: A judge is interpreting a section of a law and looks at the summary phrase written in the margin next to the section. What is this phrase called and what is its legal status?
options: Marginal Reference; Binding part of law
Marginal Note; Not part of the law but an aid to interpretation
Section Heading; Part of the enactment
Footnote; Secondary source
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Marginal notes (or section heads) give a concise indication of a section's content; they are not part of the statute but serve as an aid to interpretation [35, 36].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Ade is drafted to write a bill. He doesn't update the sponsor for three months. When the bill is finally presented, it contains many errors because Ade didn't understand the sponsor's new policy. Which ethical duty did Ade breach?
options: Duty to not instigate litigation
Duty to keep the client informed
Duty to avoid archaic words
Duty to use a Preamble
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Rule 14 of the RPC, a drafter has an ethical duty to keep the client informed of the progress and any important development in the matter [37, 38].

quizzes.scenario: A drafter uses the expression "and/or" in a section describing penalties. A senior partner tells him this is bad practice. Why?
options: It is too contemporary
It is an archaic expression
It creates ambiguity and should be replaced by "both/and" or "either/or"
It is a Latin phrase
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The expression "and/or" must be avoided because it creates ambiguity; draftsmen should use "both/and" for conjunctive meanings and "either/or" for disjunctive meanings [23, 25].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer drafts a contract for a client which includes the statement: "The document was signed and sealed by the vendor." How should this be improved according to modern drafting techniques?
options: Use the passive voice to maintain a formal tone
Change the sentence to "The vendor signed and sealed the document"
Add more adjectives to the sentence for clarity
Replace the word "vendor" with "seller" for variety
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is to use the active voice (subject-verb-object) instead of the passive voice to make the meaning of each statement clearer and more direct [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: In a lease agreement, a lawyer uses the term "Landlord" in the first five sections but switches to the term "Lessor" in the remaining sections to refer to the same party. What drafting habit has been violated?
options: Inconsistency
Verbosity
Intricate expression
Use of archaic words
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule of consistency dictates that a draftsman should never change words if he does not want to change the meaning, as a change in word implies a change in meaning [3-5].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer drafts a deed of assignment and repeatedly uses phrases such as "the said property" and "the aforesaid vendor." What is the best way to improve this draft?
options: Use "the property" or "the vendor" instead
Use more Latin phrases to show expertise
Use more pronouns like "it" and "him" to avoid repetition
Use the word "hereinafter" to link the terms
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is to avoid archaic words and expressions such as "said" or "aforesaid" when simple, contemporary words like "the" can be used more elegantly [3, 6, 7].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is drafting a clause where the client is given the choice to renew a contract but is not legally compelled to do so. Which word is most appropriate to convey this discretion?
options: Shall
Must
May
Forthwith
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the words "will" and "may" suggest what is discretionary, while the words "shall" and "must" suggest an obligation or mandatory action [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is drafting a long list of conditions for a partnership agreement. How should these be structured to ensure the document is most readily understood?
options: Write all conditions in one long continuous sentence
Use a series of "and/or" connections between items
Use paragraphing with an introductory statement and numbered items
Use Roman numerals without any introductory text
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is to use paragraphing techniques, such as two-layered or three-layered text, when conditions are provided for, as it makes the structure more apparent and aids comprehension [10, 11].

quizzes.scenario: A legal sentence in a draft states: "If the applicant reaches 18 years of age, the bank shall open the account." According to the elements of a legal sentence (CaCoSA), what does "If the applicant reaches 18 years of age" represent?
options: The Case
The Condition
The Legal Subject
The Legal Action
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In the CaCoSA arrangement, "Conditions" involve requirements (often using words like unless, until, or if) that must be satisfied before the legal action can be taken [12, 13].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is drafting a legal opinion for a non-lawyer client and uses the phrase "mutatis mutandis" regarding the application of terms. What drafting principle has the lawyer violated?
options: Economy of words
Avoidance of Latin words and phrases
Chronological order
Consistency in nomenclature
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the use of Latin words and phrases should be avoided in drafting because they may not be understood by all clients; plain English that is understood by both lawyers and non-lawyers should be used instead [4, 5, 14].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer drafts the following sentence: "The manager and/or the secretary shall sign the check." Why is this considered a failure in good drafting?
options: It is too concise for a legal document
"And/or" creates ambiguity and should be avoided in favor of clear conjunctive or disjunctive terms
It uses the active voice instead of the passive voice
It lacks a clear legal action
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is to never use the expression "and/or" because it creates ambiguity; draftsmen should use "both/and" for a conjunctive meaning or "either/or" for a disjunctive meaning [8, 15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: In a definition section of a draft, a lawyer writes: "Vehicle includes a motor car." What is the legal effect of using the word "includes" in this context?
options: It closes the definition to only mean motor cars
It extends the meaning beyond the ordinary word to be open or in-exhaustive
It narrows the word beyond its ordinary meaning
It indicates that the word means exactly what follows it
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule for interpretation clauses is that "includes" is used where the meaning is intended to be open or in-exhaustive, while "means" is used when the meaning is closed or restricted [17-19].

quizzes.scenario: A junior lawyer finds a precedent in a form book for a property sale and copies it exactly for a client's transaction without making any changes. What is the correct approach to using such forms?
options: Precedents must be followed exactly to avoid legal errors
Precedents should be adapted wisely according to the specific instructions received
Precedents are only for educational purposes and cannot be used in practice
Precedents should only be used if they are in Latin
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that while precedents are valuable aids, they should be used cautiously and must be adapted and modified to fit the specific instruction and the Nigerian context [20, 21].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer includes the following in a demand letter: "I will use the full weight and extent of the law to deal with you." What drafting principle is violated here?
options: Clarity of thought
Precision of language
Economy of words
Chronological order
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is to maintain economy of words, which involves the non-use of superfluous or redundant phrases like "full weight of the law" [1, 22].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is drafting a list of three separate but related items in a single legal sentence. Which punctuation mark is most appropriate to separate these items instead of repeating a conjunctive word?
options: Full stop
Colon
Semi-colon
Comma
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is to use a semi-colon (;) to separate related sentences or coordinate independent clauses within a list, often as a substitute for conjunctive words like 'and' [23, 24].

quizzes.scenario: A commercial contract drafted by a solicitor states: "The performance of this contract shall commence from 1st January." On which day must performance actually begin according to the rules of time computation?
options: 1st January
2nd January
31st December
1st February
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The general rule in computing time is that the word "From" a named date excludes that date, and computation commences on the following day [25].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer drafts: "Mr. Ade told Mr. Musa that he would pay the debt." Why is this sentence considered poorly drafted in a legal context?
options: It is written in the active voice
The pronoun "he" is ambiguous and should be replaced with the specific noun
It is too short to be a legal provision
It lacks a defined condition
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that nouns should be preferred to pronouns even at the cost of repetition to avoid pitfalls and ambiguity regarding who is being referred to [5, 7, 14].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer wants a specific section of a legal document to be superior to and override any other conflicting provisions found elsewhere in that same document. Which phrase is most effective for this purpose?
options: Subject to
Notwithstanding
Provided that
Without prejudice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that the word "Notwithstanding" serves as a proviso that overrides any other provision and indicates superiority [26, 27].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer drafts a legal text that begins with an introductory statement, followed by several numbered paragraphs listing requirements, and ends with a final concluding statement. What is this technique called?
options: Two-layered text
Three-layered text
CaCoSA arrangement
Ejusdem generis technique
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A three-layered text is a paragraphing technique consisting of an introductory statement, numbered paragraphs, and a concluding statement [10, 28].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer drafts a main clause and then adds: "Provided that the liability shall not exceed N500,000." What is the technical function of this "Provided that" phrase?
options: It introduces the legal subject
It qualifies or restricts the preceding provision
It states the primary case
It provides a definition for "liability"
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is that "Provided that" is used to qualify the provision of the section or to limit its consequences [26, 27].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer drafts a regulation stating: "No person shall bring dogs, cats, or other animals into the office." What rule of drafting suggests that "other animals" should be limited to those similar to dogs and cats?
options: Literal Rule
Golden Rule
Ejusdem Generis Rule
Mischief Rule
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Ejusdem Generis rule applies when general words follow specific ones of the same genus, limiting the general words to things of that same genus [29, 30].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer drafts the sentence: "The said vendor hereinbefore mentioned shall pay the same." What is the primary drafting error in this sentence?
options: Use of the active voice
Use of archaic words and legalese
Being too concise for a legal draft
Failure to use Latin phrases
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The rule is to avoid archaic words and legalese such as "said", "hereinbefore", and "the same" as a substitute for a pronoun, using plain English instead [3, 4, 6, 7].

quizzes.scenario: Chief Okon, a legal practitioner, is accused of misappropriating N10,000,000 belonging to his client, which he received as a judgment debt. The NBA files a petition before the LPDC. Okon argues that since he has not been convicted of a crime by a court, the LPDC cannot hear the matter.
options: The LPDC must wait for a criminal conviction
The LPDC can proceed if the charge is couched as a breach of the RPC
The matter must be referred to the High Court first
Misappropriation is not a professional offence
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Where an allegation of misconduct discloses a criminal element but is couched as a breach of the Rules of Professional Conduct (RPC), the LPDC may try the misconduct without waiting for a criminal trial, as established in Ndukwe v LPDC [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Ada is found guilty of "infamous conduct in a professional respect" by the LPDC for manufacturing non-existing cases in court. The LPDC wants to impose the maximum sanction.
options: Suspension for 5 years
A fine of N200,000
Striking off the name from the roll
Admonition and public apology
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 12(1) of the Legal Practitioners Act (LPA) and Rule 22 of the LPDC Rules 2020, where a lawyer is found guilty of infamous conduct in a professional respect, the committee can direct the Chief Registrar to strike the lawyer's name off the roll [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: A legal practitioner, Mr. Sani, is convicted by a Magistrate Court in Lagos for maintaining a brothel. He appeals the conviction. While the appeal is pending, the NBA brings a charge before the LPDC under Section 12(1)(b) of the LPA.
options: Mr. Sani is liable because he was convicted by a court
The LPDC cannot proceed until the appeal is determined or the time to appeal has elapsed
Only the Supreme Court can discipline him for crimes
Maintaining a brothel is not incompatible with the status of a lawyer
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 12(5) of the LPA, for the purpose of discipline based on a conviction, a person is not deemed convicted until the conviction stands at a time when no appeal is pending or the time for appeal has elapsed [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Bamidele is aggrieved by a direction of the LPDC delivered on June 1st, ordering his suspension from practice. He intends to appeal the decision.
options: He must appeal to the Body of Benchers within 30 days
He must appeal to the Court of Appeal within 14 days
He must appeal to the Supreme Court within 28 days
No appeal lies from the decision of the LPDC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: By virtue of Section 12(7) of the LPA and the decision in Nwalutu v NBA, appeals from the directions of the LPDC lie directly to the Supreme Court and must be filed within 28 days of service of the notice of direction [3, 7].

quizzes.scenario: Musa obtained his qualifying certificate by presenting a forged LLB degree from a non-existent university. This was discovered five years after his call to the Bar.
options: He cannot be disciplined because it has been over three years
He is guilty of obtaining enrolment by fraud under the LPA
Only the university can revoke his certificate
He can only be disciplined if he committed a crime during practice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 12(1)(c) of the LPA, obtaining enrolment by fraud or misrepresentation of facts is a professional offence for which a lawyer's name can be struck off the roll [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Effiong is accused of seducing his client's spouse. The LPDC finds that while this conduct is disgraceful, it was not committed "in the course of his practice" as a lawyer.
options: He is guilty of infamous conduct in a professional respect
He is not liable for any professional offence
He is guilty of conduct incompatible with the status of a legal practitioner
The LPDC has no jurisdiction over personal morality
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 12(2) of the LPA, conduct that is incompatible with the status of a legal practitioner (but not amounting to infamous conduct because it wasn't in a professional respect) is a professional offence [10, 11].

quizzes.scenario: The NBA receives a complaint against Mr. Jude for professional negligence. They file an Originating Application with the LPDC. The Chairman of the LPDC assigns a single member to look at the application first.
options: This is a member of the Investigation Committee
This is the Initial Committee Member (ICM)
This is the Auditor General of the Bar
This member must be a Justice of the Supreme Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Rule 5(1) of the LPDC Rules 2020, every Originating Application is first considered by an Initial Committee Member (ICM) to determine if there is a prima facie case [12, 13].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Tunde was struck off the roll 10 years ago for financial dishonesty. He has since shown genuine remorse and has been working as a law teacher with a clean record. He applies for his name to be restored to the roll.
options: Only the NBA President can restore his name
He must apply to the LPDC or Supreme Court (depending on who struck him off)
A name struck off can never be restored
He must rewrite the Bar Exams first
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 14 of the LPA, a person whose name has been struck off may apply for restoration. The body that meted out the punishment (LPDC or Supreme Court) considers the gravity of the offence, genuine remorse, and whether he is now a fit and proper person [14, 15].

quizzes.scenario: The Chief Justice of Nigeria (CJN) receives a report that Barrister Kemi has been convicted of money laundering. He wants to stop her from practicing immediately while the LPDC case is prepared.
options: The CJN has no power to suspend a lawyer
The CJN can suspend her pending the LPDC's determination
The CJN must wait for the Supreme Court to sit in full
Only the NBA can suspend a lawyer
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 13(2) of the LPA, the CJN may suspend a legal practitioner from practice where it appears that they should be suspended pending proceedings before the Disciplinary Committee [16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: During a trial before the Federal High Court, Barrister X is found to have suppressed a vital document that would have helped the opponent's case. The judge refers the matter to the Supreme Court for discipline.
options: The Supreme Court has no original jurisdiction to discipline lawyers
The Supreme Court can discipline a lawyer for infamous conduct in a matter handled before any court of record
Only the LPDC can hear such a referral
The lawyer must be tried for contempt first
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 13(1) of the LPA, the Supreme Court has original jurisdiction to discipline a legal practitioner who is guilty of infamous conduct in a professional respect in any matter before it or any other court of record in Nigeria [18, 19].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Emeka is charged before the LPDC for a breach of the RPC. He argues that the proceedings are criminal in nature and the NBA must prove the case beyond reasonable doubt.
options: Professional disciplinary proceedings are purely criminal
The standard of proof is balance of probabilities
The standard of proof is proof beyond all shadows of doubt
Professional discipline is a civil matter with no standard of proof
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: As stated in the sources, proceedings before the LPDC are not purely criminal and professional misconduct is proved on the balance of probabilities [20, 21].

quizzes.scenario: The LPDC finds Barrister Y guilty of a misconduct that is "incompatible with the status of a legal practitioner" under Section 12(2) LPA, but specifically finds it does NOT amount to "infamous conduct."
options: They must strike his name off the roll
They can only admonish him
They can suspend or admonish him but cannot strike his name off the roll
They must refer him to the CJN for a fine
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 12(2) of the LPA, if a conduct is incompatible with the status of a lawyer but does not amount to infamous conduct, the LPDC can suspend or admonish him but lacks the power to strike his name off the roll [22, 23].

quizzes.scenario: An Originating Application against Barrister Z is filed at the LPDC. The ICM is unsure if there is a case to answer and refers it to a panel. How many members must be on this panel to certify a prima facie case?
options: Two members
Three members
Five members
All members of the BOB
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Rule 5(3) of the LPDC Rules 2020, where the ICM is in doubt, a panel of three members must consider if there is a case to answer and certify so [13, 24].

quizzes.scenario: A client, Mr. Wale, wants to file a complaint against his lawyer for professional misconduct that occurred 5 months ago. Where should he file the Originating Application under the 2020 Rules?
options: Only with the NBA President
Only at the Supreme Court Registry
To the CJN, AGF, PCA, Chairman of BOB, or the LPDC directly
To the nearest Police Station
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 4(1) of the LPDC Rules 2020 provides that the application can be forwarded to the CJN, AGF, PCA or PJCA, Chairman of the BOB, President or Branch Chairman of the NBA, or the LPDC itself [25, 26].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister G has been convicted of a criminal offence. He denies the allegations and has filed an appeal. The NBA wants to proceed with disciplinary action before the appeal is heard.
options: The LPDC must proceed immediately
The LPDC must stay action until the appeal is concluded
Denying the crime doesn't matter if there was a trial
The NBA must prosecute the appeal themselves
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Where a legal practitioner denies criminal allegations that form the basis of a misconduct charge, the LPDC must stay its proceedings until the criminal trial and any subsequent appeals are concluded to ensure the crime is proved beyond reasonable doubt [2, 27].

quizzes.scenario: The LPDC is being constituted for the year. Which of the following persons is expressly disqualified from being the Chairman of the LPDC?
options: The Attorney-General of the Federation
A retired Justice of the Court of Appeal
A Justice of the Supreme Court
The NBA President
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 11(2) of the LPA, the Chairman of the LPDC shall be a person who is NOT the Chief Justice of Nigeria or a Justice of the Supreme Court [28, 29].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister A is found guilty by the LPDC for failing to open a client account and mixing client funds with his own. This is his first offence.
options: He can only be admonished
This is a serious act of infamous conduct and he can be struck off the roll
Mixing funds is a civil matter and the LPDC has no jurisdiction
He must be tried by the General Council of the Bar first
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Misappropriation or the failure to separate client funds (not opening a client account) is regarded as a serious act of infamous conduct in a professional respect (Onitiri v Fadipe), allowing for the sanction of striking off the roll [1, 30].

quizzes.scenario: After Barrister D is found guilty of infamous conduct, the LPDC issues a direction for him to refund the client's money. Where must this direction be published?
options: In three national newspapers
In the Federal Government Gazette
On the NBA website only
It is not published to protect the lawyer's dignity
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Directions of the LPDC, except in cases of mere admonition, are required to be published in the Federal Government Gazette [21, 31].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister E is served with a notice of hearing for a disciplinary matter before the LPDC. How many days must there be between the date of service and the hearing date?
options: 7 days
15 days
30 days
60 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Rule 12(3) of the LPDC Rules 2020, there shall be at least 15 days between the date of service of the notice and the date fixed for the hearing [32].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister F is convicted of a traffic offence (speeding) and sentenced to a fine. The NBA files a petition to have him disciplined for conviction of an offence incompatible with the status of a lawyer.
options: Speeding is an incompatible offence
Traffic offences are generally excluded from being incompatible with the status of a legal practitioner
Any conviction leads to automatic striking off
He is only liable if he was robed while speeding
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: While convictions for financial dishonesty or moral turpitude are incompatible with the status of an LP, some offences like traffic offences are generally excluded from the scope of Section 12(1)(b) LPA [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Okafor, a legal practitioner, signs a formal letter to a client regarding a property transaction as "Okafor & Co." without including his personal name. The client later attempts to use this letter in a dispute.
options: The letter is validly signed
The letter is improperly signed
The letter is valid only if stamped
The letter is void ab initio
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: It is wrong for a legal practitioner to sign in the name of a law firm without the individual person's name, as established in the Supreme Court decision in Nig. Ltd v Ekenma (2009) [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer drafts a letter of demand to a debtor on behalf of a client but fails to affix his NBA stamp and seal to the document. The debtor challenges the document's validity during a pre-action meeting.
options: The letter is a total nullity
