The letter is irregular but can be regularized
The letter is fully valid as it is not a court process
The letter is void and cannot be amended
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Rule 10(1) and (3) of the RPC, a legal document signed and filed without the NBA stamp and seal is not properly signed, rendering it irregular or voidable until counsel regularizes it by affixing the stamp [3-5].

quizzes.scenario: During a series of intense settlement negotiations, Barrister Ade writes a letter to the opposing counsel titled "Without Prejudice," proposing a settlement sum. The negotiation fails, and Ade's opponent tries to tender the letter in court to prove Ade's client admitted liability.
options: The letter is admissible as a confession
The letter is inadmissible in evidence
The letter is admissible only if signed by the client
The letter is admissible with the court's leave
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: "Without Prejudice" is a term of negotiation covered by Section 26 of the Evidence Act which generally makes such documents inadmissible in evidence against the maker unless the right is expressly waived [6, 7].

quizzes.scenario: A client is negotiating a lease agreement through his solicitor. The solicitor sends a draft agreement marked "Subject to Contract." Before a formal deed is signed, the client claims a binding contract exists based on the draft.
options: There is a binding contract
There is no binding contract
The contract is valid if a deposit was paid
The contract is valid if stamped
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The expression "Subject to Contract" means that all transactions and communications between parties are not binding or enforceable until a formal contract is executed, as held in UBA v Tejumola & Sons Ltd [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: At the conclusion of a contentious Annual General Meeting of a law firm's corporate client, the minutes are prepared. Only the Chairman signs the final document. A disgruntled director challenges the record's authenticity.
options: The minutes are validly executed
The minutes are improperly executed
Only the Secretary needs to sign
Minutes do not require signatures
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Minutes of meetings serve as evidence and proof of the contents of a meeting and should be signed by both the Chairman and the Secretary for proper authentication [10-12].

quizzes.scenario: A junior associate in a large law firm needs to send an internal communication to the Principal Partner regarding a new firm policy. How should the "To/From" section of the memorandum be structured to reflect the hierarchy?
options: FROM: Associate, TO: Principal Partner
TO: Principal Partner, FROM: Associate
Dear Principal Partner
Yours faithfully, Associate
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In an office memorandum, when communication is coming from a lower authority to a higher authority, "TO" is placed first, directing it to the higher authority [13, 14].

quizzes.scenario: An applicant for a position in a top-tier law firm includes his photograph, religion, and marital status on his CV to make it look "professional." The HR manager notes these details.
options: These details are mandatory in Nigeria
These details are generally unnecessary and may lead to identity theft
These details are required by the NBA
These details are required for all SAN firms
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A date of birth, place of birth, religion, and marital status are considered unnecessary in a CV and can lead to identity theft, particularly in international or modern professional applications [15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: A solicitor writes to a client to confirm the details of a telephone conversation they had an hour earlier regarding the client's decision to settle a debt. This letter is classified as a:
options: Status Letter
Opinion Letter
Confirming Letter
Demand Letter
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A Confirming Letter is specifically written to re-affirm an oral discussion which took place previously between the lawyer and the client to clear any uncertainty [17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: The Head of Administration in a law office sends a memo to all staff about a change in the closing time. He uses the salutation "Dear Colleagues" and ends with "Yours faithfully." This structure is:
options: Correct for all internal memos
Incorrect as memos omit salutations and complimentary closes
Correct only if on a letterhead
Mandatory under the RPC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A memorandum is characterized by less formality; usually, the address, salutation, and complimentary closing are omitted, and the writer should go straight to the information [19, 20].

quizzes.scenario: A senior lawyer is applying for an adjunct lecturing position at the Nigerian Law School. Which type of CV is most appropriate for his application to highlight his research and publications?
options: Chronological CV
Functional CV
Academic CV
Teaching CV
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Academic CVs are focused on achievements, research interests, and specialist skills, and are specifically used when applying for lecturing jobs [21, 22].

quizzes.scenario: During a meeting of the Legal Practitioners Remuneration Committee, the secretary attempts to record every single word spoken by every member in the order they spoke. This method is:
options: Mandatory for legal records
Verbatim and generally unnecessary
The preferred method for SANs
Required for evidence in court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Minutes are not taken verbatim; they should only contain the highlight of key issues, deliberations, and decisions reached during the meeting [12, 23].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer wants to provide a client with a report on the current progress of a long-running litigation matter in the High Court. The appropriate type of letter to write is a:
options: Opinion Letter
Demand Letter
Status Letter
Business Letter
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A Status Letter refers to letters which lawyers are requested to write in order to give an overview or update on the current position of a transaction or court case [17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is drafting a formal letter to a Managing Director he has never met. He uses the salutation "Dear Sir." What is the most appropriate complimentary close for this letter?
options: Yours sincerely
Yours faithfully
Yours truly
Best regards
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In formal letter writing, the salutation "Dear Sir/Madam" is traditionally paired with the complimentary close "Yours faithfully" [2, 24].

quizzes.scenario: A Principal Partner sends a memo to the litigation department and includes "CC: Practice Manager." What is the implication of the "CC" in this internal communication?
options: The Practice Manager is the primary recipient
It is a Courtesy Copy for the Manager's awareness
It means the Manager must sign the memo
It is a confidential blind copy
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: "CC" (Courtesy Copy) is used to bring the memo to the notice of persons to whom it is not primarily directed but who should be aware of the issues or directives [1, 20].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer applying for a job realizes he has a two-year gap in his work history due to a period of illness. How should he address this on his CV according to professional standards?
options: He should lie and stretch previous dates
He should ignore the gap entirely
He should explain the gap in his cover letter
He should remove the dates from his CV
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Applicants must always inform a potential employer of a gap in their CV to avoid misinterpretation, and an explanation for the career gap can be provided in the cover letter [25, 26].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is sending a formal letter to a client and has attached a copy of a court ruling. How should he indicate this attachment at the bottom of the letter?
options: ATTACHMENT
ENCL
CC
SUBJECT
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: When there is an attachment to a letter, it is indicated by the expression 'Encl' or 'ENCL(S)' [1, 24].

quizzes.scenario: After the opening prayer and remarks at a board meeting, the Chairman asks the members to review the record of the previous meeting for accuracy. This stage is known as:
options: Adoption of Agenda
Matters Arising
Adoption of Minutes of last meeting
Any Other Business
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The "Adoption of Minutes of last meeting" is the formal stage where the record of the previous meeting is read out and adopted by the members [10, 27].

quizzes.scenario: An applicant for a legal officer role includes false information about his class of degree on his CV. He is hired but the firm discovers the lie three months later. What is the potential legal consequence?
options: None, as he has proven his skill
A simple warning
Serious consequences including potential imprisonment
A requirement to retake the bar exam
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: One should never lie on a CV as there can be serious consequences, such as imprisonment and the potential withdrawal of certificates from the institution [25].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is drafting a formal letter and writes the date as "12/04/2024". According to the rules of professional legal writing, this format is:
options: Highly recommended
To be avoided
Mandatory for all SANs
Only for informal letters
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An undated document is worthless, but dates in formal letters should be written out fully (e.g., 27th November 2018) rather than using only numbers like "25/08/2023" [28-30].

quizzes.scenario: A meeting is held to resolve a dispute between partners in a law firm. After lengthy deliberations, the partners reach a final decision on how to share profits. This decision is recorded under:
options: Opening Remarks
Matters Arising
Resolutions
Any Other Business
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Resolutions are the itemized decisions and agreements reached on each issue discussed following the agenda of the meeting [10-12].

quizzes.scenario: Chief Koko, aged 98, is bedridden and unable to visit a law office. He requests his lawyer, Mr. Ade, to come to his residence to take instructions for his Will. Mr. Ade complies. Is Mr. Ade's action professional?
options: Yes, because extreme old age is an exception to the rule against taking instructions at a client's house
No, a lawyer must never take instructions outside the law office
Yes, provided the lawyer charges an extra fee for the visit
No, unless the lawyer obtains permission from the NBA Chairman
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 22 of the Rules of Professional Conduct (RPC) prohibits taking instructions at a client's house but provides exceptions for special circumstances or urgency, such as extreme old age or infirmity [1-4].

quizzes.scenario: During a client interview, Mr. Emeka admits to his lawyer that he committed a fraud two years ago. After the interview, the lawyer shares this information with his colleagues at a bar hangout. Which rule has been breached?
options: Rule 14 RPC
Rule 19 RPC
Rule 15 RPC
Rule 16 RPC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 19(1) of the RPC and Section 192 of the Evidence Act provide that all communications made by a client in the course of professional employment are privileged and must not be revealed without the client's consent [5-7].

quizzes.scenario: ABC Ltd and XYZ Ltd are in a mediation session governed by the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023. They reach an agreement and sign a settlement document. XYZ Ltd later attempts to walk away, claiming the agreement is not binding because it is not a court judgment. What is the legal status of the agreement?
options: It is non-binding until a judge signs it
It is binding only if registered at the Corporate Affairs Commission
It is binding and enforceable as a contract, consent judgment, or consent award
It is non-binding because mediation is always voluntary
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 82 of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023, a settlement agreement resulting from mediation is binding on the parties and enforceable in court as a contract, consent judgment, or consent award [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: A junior lawyer drafts a clause stating: "The document was prepared and signed by the Solicitor." His senior instructs him to use the active voice. Which of the following is the correct active voice version?
options: The Solicitor prepared and signed the document
The document had been prepared and signed by the Solicitor
Preparation and signing of the document was done by the Solicitor
By the Solicitor, the document was prepared and signed
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In legal drafting, the active voice (subject-verb-object) is preferred over the passive voice to ensure clarity and intelligibility [10, 11].

quizzes.scenario: In an examination-in-chief, Mr. Musa, the plaintiff's counsel, asks his witness: "Is it not true that you saw the defendant break the window with a stone?" The defense counsel objects. Is the objection valid?
options: No, because it is a relevant fact
Yes, because it is a leading question which is generally not allowed in examination-in-chief
No, because the witness is under oath
Yes, because only closed questions are allowed
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 221(1) and (2) of the Evidence Act 2011 provide that leading questions (those suggesting an answer) shall not be asked in examination-in-chief except with the court's permission or on introductory/undisputed matters [12-14].

quizzes.scenario: A legal practitioner is conducting research on a novel point of Nigerian law. He relies solely on a textbook by a famous professor and a legal dictionary. Which category of sources has he utilized?
options: Primary sources
Secondary sources
Tertiary sources
Finding tools
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Secondary sources include textbooks and dictionaries which explain the law from an author's perspective and are not binding on courts, unlike primary sources like statutes and case law [15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: A matter was concluded ten years ago. The client returns to the firm and demands his original title deeds. The lawyer informs him that the file was destroyed three years ago. What is the proper duration for keeping closed files?
options: Files must be kept forever
Files must be kept for exactly 5 years
There is no statutory period, but it is suggested to keep them for at least a year longer than the limitation period
Files must be destroyed immediately after judgment
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: There is no statutory requirement for the length of time to keep files, but it is suggested they be kept for at least a year longer than the limitation period for the particular action [17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: A contract between Party A and Party B contains a mandatory "Scott v Avery" arbitration clause. Party A sues Party B in the High Court. Party B enters appearance and files a Statement of Defense before applying for a stay of proceedings pending arbitration. Will the stay be granted?
options: Yes, because the clause is mandatory
No, because by filing a Statement of Defense, Party B has taken a step in the proceedings and waived the right to arbitration
Yes, provided Party B pays the required fine
No, because arbitration is unconstitutional
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 5(1) of the Arbitration and Mediation Act, an application for a stay must be made before taking any other step in the proceedings; filing a Statement of Defense is considered a step that waives the right [19-22].

quizzes.scenario: A legislative drafter is preparing a bill for the National Assembly. He starts the enacting formula with: "ENACTED BY THE HOUSE OF ASSEMBLY OF LAGOS STATE as follows:". Is this correct?
options: Yes, it is the standard formula for all Nigerian laws
No, a Federal law must state it is enacted by the National Assembly of the Federal Republic of Nigeria
Yes, provided the President assents to it
No, it must start with "THE GOVERNOR ENACTS"
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The enacting formula must correctly disclose the authority that enacts the legislation. For Federal laws, it is the National Assembly; for State laws, it is the House of Assembly of that specific state [23, 24].

quizzes.scenario: In a settlement negotiation letter, a lawyer includes the phrase "Without Prejudice." The negotiation fails, and the opposing counsel attempts to tender the letter in court as an admission of liability. Is the letter admissible?
options: Yes, all written letters are admissible
No, the "Without Prejudice" tag makes the document inadmissible in evidence against the maker
Yes, but only if the lawyer is called as a witness
No, because only oral evidence is admissible in such cases
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 26 of the Evidence Act, documents marked "Without Prejudice" or made during negotiations are generally not admissible in evidence against the maker [25-27].

quizzes.scenario: During cross-examination, a lawyer asks a female witness: "Is it not true that you were once treated for a sexually transmitted disease?" purely to annoy and embarrass her, with no relevance to the case. What should the court do?
options: Allow the question to test the witness's character
Disallow the question as it is indecent and scandalous
Require the witness to answer under oath
Report the witness for contempt
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 227 and 228 of the Evidence Act 2011, the court may disallow questions that are indecent, scandalous, or intended only to annoy or insult the witness [28].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is consulted on a debt recovery matter. He immediately files a writ of summons in court without discussing the possibility of mediation or negotiation with the client. What is the ethical implication?
options: The lawyer is being diligent
The lawyer is guilty of professional misconduct
The lawyer is entitled to choose the best strategy
This is acceptable if the debt is large
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 15(3)(d) of the RPC makes it a professional duty for a lawyer to inform his client of the option of alternative dispute resolution (ADR) before resorting to litigation; failure to do so is misconduct [29-31].

quizzes.scenario: At the end of a transaction, a lawyer draws two parallel lines across the face of the file and writes "CLOSED" in upper case letters. Where should this file be kept?
options: It should be returned to the High Court
It should be kept in the firm's active cabinet
It should be kept in the cabinet or a separate room meant for closed files, called an archive
It must be burned immediately
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: When a file is closed using the parallel lines and "CLOSED" marking, it is placed in a file case and stored in an archive for easy future identification and retrieval [32-34].

quizzes.scenario: During negotiation, a lawyer realizes that if no agreement is reached, his client will likely lose everything in a court trial that will take years. This "last resort" scenario is known as:
options: BATNA
WATNA
Bottom Line
Opening Offer
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: WATNA stands for "Worst Alternative To Negotiated Agreement," representing the worst course of action or outcome a party may face if negotiation fails [35-37].

quizzes.scenario: A witness called by Mr. Gomez starts testifying in a manner that is clearly biased against Mr. Gomez and supports the opponent. Mr. Gomez wants to cross-examine his own witness. What must he do?
options: He can do so automatically
He must apply to the court to declare the witness "hostile"
He must withdraw from the case
He must wait until re-examination
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 230 of the Evidence Act, a party calling a witness cannot discredit them unless the court declares the witness "hostile," meaning they bear hostile animus and are unwilling to tell the truth [38, 39].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is drafting a tenancy agreement. He writes: "The premises shall be vacated by the tenant on the expiration of the term." How can this be improved using the active voice?
options: The tenant shall vacate the premises on the expiration of the term
On the expiration of the term, the premises shall have been vacated by the tenant
Vacating of the premises shall be done by the tenant
The tenant is expected to have vacated the premises
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Using the active voice (subject-verb-object) makes the statement clearer and more direct, as the tenant (subject) is the one performing the action (vacate) [10, 11].

quizzes.scenario: In a mediation session, a party tells the mediator a secret during a private caucus session and tells the mediator not to share it. The mediator later tells the other party to help them understand the situation. Is this permitted?
options: Yes, mediators have full discretion
No, when a party gives information subject to a condition of confidentiality, the mediator must not disclose it
Yes, if it leads to a settlement
No, unless the lawyer of the other party is present
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 75 of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023, if a party gives information to a mediator subject to a specific condition that it be kept confidential, it may not be disclosed to any other party [40].

quizzes.scenario: After an intensive cross-examination where the witness's memory was shaken, the lawyer who called the witness wants to ask questions in re-examination to introduce a new favorable fact that was forgotten during examination-in-chief. Is this allowed?
options: Yes, re-examination is for adding new facts
No, the aim of re-examination is solely to clear ambiguities arising from cross-examination
Yes, provided the other lawyer agrees
No, re-examination is not allowed in Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 214(3) of the Evidence Act, the aim of re-examination is to clear ambiguities arising during cross-examination, not to supply new or omitted facts [41, 42].

quizzes.scenario: A secretary drafts minutes for a board meeting but omits the venue and the specific time the meeting started. Which part of the minutes format is defective?
options: The Attendance
The Resolutions
The Heading
The Adjournment
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Heading of the minutes must properly state the nature of the meeting, the venue/place, the date, and the time [43, 44].

quizzes.scenario: To secure a job at a top-tier law firm, a lawyer includes a fake "First Class" grade on his CV. What are the potential consequences mentioned in the sources?
options: Only a verbal warning
No consequences if he is a good lawyer
Imprisonment and withdrawal of his certificate by his institution
He will be required to pay a fine to the NBA
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Lying on a CV or job application is dishonest and can lead to serious consequences, including imprisonment and the revocation of certificates by the relevant institution [45].

quizzes.scenario: Chief Emeka, a witness in a civil trial, traveled from Enugu to Lagos to testify for the claimant. After his testimony, the claimant's lawyer, Mr. Ade, paid him a sum to cover his flight tickets and the income he lost while away from his business.
options: This is professional misconduct as it amounts to bribing a witness.
Mr. Ade is allowed to pay reasonable expenses and compensation for loss of time.
Only the court can authorize payments to witnesses.
Mr. Ade can only pay for the flight tickets but not for lost income.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Rule 25(2) of the Rules of Professional Conduct (RPC), a lawyer shall not enter into a bargain with a witness on a fee as a pre-condition for testimony, but this does not preclude the payment of reasonable expenses for the purpose of giving evidence. [1, 2]

quizzes.scenario: During a criminal trial for theft, Mr. Okon, the defense counsel, is conducting the examination-in-chief of the defendant. He asks, "You were at home watching television at 8:00 PM on the night of the incident, weren't you?" The prosecution objects.
options: The objection will be overruled as leading questions are allowed in criminal trials.
The objection will be sustained because leading questions are generally not allowed in examination-in-chief.
The objection will be overruled because it relates to a matter already in the public domain.
The objection will be sustained because the witness is a party to the case.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 221(1) and (2) of the Evidence Act, leading questions are generally prohibited in examination-in-chief except with the permission of the court, as they suggest the answer the questioner expects to receive. [3, 4]

quizzes.scenario: In a high-profile corruption case, the lead prosecutor, Mrs. Bello, discovers a document in the case file that suggests the defendant was out of the country when the alleged crime occurred. She decides not to mention it to ensure a conviction.
options: Mrs. Bello is acting correctly to protect the interests of the State.
Mrs. Bello is guilty of professional misconduct for suppressing facts that could establish innocence.
Mrs. Bello should only disclose the document if requested by the defense.
Mrs. Bello should wait until the final address to mention the document.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 37(6) of the RPC provides that the primary duty of a lawyer engaged in public prosecution is not to convict but to see that justice is done, and the suppression of facts capable of establishing the innocence of the accused is unethical. [5, 6]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Tunde is assigned as a defense counsel in a murder trial. On the day of the hearing, he receives a more lucrative brief for a corporate merger and decides to seek an adjournment of the murder trial to attend the corporate meeting.
options: Mr. Tunde is entitled to prioritize the corporate brief as it pays more.
Mr. Tunde should prioritize the murder trial as it involves a person's life.
Mr. Tunde can delegate the murder trial to a junior associate without informing the court.
Mr. Tunde should seek the court's leave based on personal convenience.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In the case of Udofia v. State, it was held that once counsel accepts instructions in a murder case, he is expected to give it priority over all other engagements because it involves a man on trial for his life. [7]

quizzes.scenario: During the cross-examination of an opposing witness, Mr. James asks the witness questions about his previous criminal convictions, which were not mentioned during the examination-in-chief. The opposing counsel objects.
options: The objection will be sustained because questions must be limited to facts in issue.
The objection will be overruled as cross-examination has a wide latitude including testing credibility.
The objection will be sustained as counsel cannot introduce new facts in cross-examination.
The objection will be overruled only if the witness is an expert.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 215(2) and Section 223 of the Evidence Act provide that cross-examination is not restricted to facts in issue or evidence given in chief; it can be used to test accuracy, veracity, or to shake the witness's credit by injuring his character. [8-10]

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer, Mr. Chidi, is representing himself in a civil suit for breach of contract. He appears in court fully robed in his wig and gown and sits at the bar to conduct his case.
options: Mr. Chidi is acting correctly as he is a legal practitioner.
Mr. Chidi should sit at the bar but is disallowed from wearing his robes while conducting his own case.
Mr. Chidi must sit in the gallery like any other litigant.
Mr. Chidi can only robe if he is a Senior Advocate of Nigeria.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: While a legal practitioner can conduct his own case, Rule 45(2)(b) and Rule 36(1)(f) of the RPC provide that a lawyer shall not wear his robe when conducting his own case as a party to a legal proceeding. [11-13]

quizzes.scenario: During a trial, Mr. Yusuf, the defense counsel, notices that the trial judge is about to deliver a ruling based on an old law that has been recently repealed. Mr. Yusuf knows the current law would be unfavorable to his client.
options: Mr. Yusuf is duty-bound to keep silent to protect his client.
Mr. Yusuf has a duty of candour to inform the court of the current position of the law.
Mr. Yusuf should only inform the court if the prosecution raises the issue.
Mr. Yusuf should wait for the judgment and use it as a ground for appeal.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 32(1) of the RPC and the duty of candour require a lawyer to be candid and fair with the court, which includes the duty not to knowingly suppress material facts or fail to cite relevant law, even if it is against his case. [14, 15]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Obi is the defense counsel for a man accused of armed robbery. Before the trial begins, the client confesses to Mr. Obi that he actually committed the crime but wants to plead "not guilty" and have Mr. Obi argue that he was elsewhere.
options: Mr. Obi must immediately withdraw from the case.
Mr. Obi can continue to represent the client but cannot present evidence of an alibi he knows to be false.
Mr. Obi should inform the police of the confession.
Mr. Obi must plead "guilty" on behalf of his client regardless of instructions.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A lawyer may continue to represent a client who has confessed guilt, but under Rule 15(3)(f) and (g) of the RPC, he shall not knowingly use perjured or false evidence or make a false statement of fact to the court. [16-18]

quizzes.scenario: After a witness is cross-examined, the lawyer who called him, Ms. Amina, wants to ask questions regarding a completely new topic that was forgotten during the examination-in-chief.
options: Ms. Amina can ask the questions as a matter of right.
Re-examination is limited to clearing ambiguities arising from cross-examination and not for new facts.
Ms. Amina can only ask new questions if the witness is a hostile witness.
Re-examination allows for a full repeat of the examination-in-chief.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 214(3) of the Evidence Act provides that the purpose of re-examination is to clear ambiguities arising from cross-examination, and not to supply omitted or new facts. [19, 20]

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer, Mr. Segun, is stuck in traffic and realizes he will be 45 minutes late for his trial. He has the phone number of the opposing counsel and the court registrar.
options: Mr. Segun should do nothing and apologize when he arrives.
Mr. Segun should contact the court and the opposing counsel to request that the matter be stood down.
Mr. Segun should turn back and send a junior associate to ask for an adjournment.
Mr. Segun must file a formal motion for lateness.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: While not explicitly in the RPC, advocacy etiquette suggests that if counsel is unavoidably delayed, he should inform the court and the opposite counsel, requesting the case be stood down. [21, 22]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Dapo is conducting a trial before a Magistrate in Lagos. He addresses the Magistrate as "Your Worship." The Magistrate corrects him.
options: Mr. Dapo is correct; "Your Worship" is the standard for all Magistrates.
Magistrates in Lagos State are addressed as "Your Honour" by law.
Mr. Dapo should have used "My Lord."
Magistrates should be addressed as "Your Excellency."
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: While "Your Worship" is common for Magistrates elsewhere, Section 352 of the ACJL and Section 8 of the Magistrates' Courts Law of Lagos State specify that Magistrates in Lagos are addressed as "Your Honour." [23, 24]

quizzes.scenario: In a civil case where the defendant led evidence, the trial has concluded, and it is time for final addresses. Who should address the court first?
options: The Plaintiff always addresses the court first.
The Defendant addresses the court first because he led evidence.
The court decides the order of address.
Both parties must address the court simultaneously in writing.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to the general rules of trial procedure mentioned in the sources, if the defendant led evidence, he shall address the court first, followed by the plaintiff. [25]

quizzes.scenario: During cross-examination, Mr. Kunle asks a female witness, "Is it not true that you are a woman of easy virtue who sleeps around for money?" The question has no relevance to the facts of the case.
options: The question is allowed to test the witness's character.
The question is unprofessional as it is insulting and scandalous without reasonable cause.
Mr. Kunle can ask anything he wants during cross-examination.
The witness must answer the question if she is under oath.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 25(4) of the RPC and Section 228 of the Evidence Act forbid lawyers from using abusive or unfair language or asking questions only to insult or degrade a witness. [2, 26, 27]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Bassey lost a case in the High Court. He meets his client outside the courtroom and says, "The judge was obviously bribed by the other side; that's why we lost." He then tells this to a journalist from a national daily.
options: Mr. Bassey is exercising his right to freedom of speech.
Mr. Bassey is guilty of professional misconduct for scandalizing the court.
Mr. Bassey is correct to inform the public of judicial corruption.
Mr. Bassey should only say this if he has evidence.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 31 of the RPC and the laws on contempt forbid scandalizing the court or making frivolous allegations of partiality against a judge, especially to the press. [28-31]

quizzes.scenario: Ms. Funmi, a lawyer with 2 years post-call experience, is handling a case in the High Court. She discovers that she failed to ask a witness a crucial question during the trial, leading to her client losing the case. The client sues her for negligence.
options: Ms. Funmi is liable in negligence for her handling of the case in court.
Ms. Funmi is immune from liability for damages attributable to her negligence while conducting proceedings in court.
Ms. Funmi is only liable if she was paid a high fee.
The client can only sue the firm, not Funmi personally.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 9(3) of the Legal Practitioners Act (LPA) exempts a lawyer from liability for negligence arising from the conduct of proceedings before a court or tribunal. [32-34]

quizzes.scenario: A Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN), Mr. Akande, is appearing in a civil matter at the High Court. He appears alone without a junior counsel. The opposing counsel, who is not a SAN, objects.
options: The objection will be overruled; a SAN can always appear alone.
The objection will be sustained; a SAN must appear with a junior or another SAN in civil cases.
A SAN can only appear with a junior if the claim is above 50 million Naira.
Mr. Akande can appear alone if it is a criminal case.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Rule 2(1) of the Senior Advocates of Nigeria (Privileges and Functions) Rules 1990, a SAN shall not appear as counsel in any civil case before any superior court of record except with a junior or another SAN. [35-37]

quizzes.scenario: During a criminal trial, the prosecutor, Mr. Bala, realizes he does not have enough evidence to support the charge against the defendant but proceeds anyway to satisfy the complainant.
options: Mr. Bala is doing his duty to his client (the State).
Mr. Bala is acting unprofessionally; a prosecutor should not institute a charge not supported by probable evidence.
Mr. Bala should leave it to the judge to decide if the evidence is sufficient.
Mr. Bala is correct because the defendant is probably guilty anyway.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 37(5) of the RPC provides that a public prosecutor shall not institute or cause to be instituted a criminal charge if he knows it is not supported by probable evidence. [38]

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer, Mr. Eze, is invited to a TV talk show to discuss a case he is currently handling which is pending judgment. He speaks extensively on why his client's version of the facts is the only logical one.
options: Mr. Eze is acting within his rights to defend his client's reputation.
Mr. Eze is in breach of the rule against trial publicity (sub judice).
Mr. Eze can speak as long as he doesn't mention the judge's name.
Mr. Eze is helping the court by explaining the case to the public.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 33 of the RPC forbids a lawyer involved in a pending or anticipated suit from making any extra-judicial statement likely to prejudice or interfere with a fair trial (the sub judice rule). [28, 39-41]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Ladi is conducting a trial. While the judge is addressing him, Mr. Ladi remains seated because he is feeling tired from standing during the examination-in-chief.
options: Mr. Ladi is acting correctly to preserve his energy.
Mr. Ladi is in breach of court decorum; a lawyer should rise when addressing or being addressed by the judge.
Mr. Ladi can sit if he asks for the judge's permission first.
Court decorum only requires standing during the opening statement.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 36(c) of the RPC provides that a lawyer should rise when addressing or being addressed by the judge. [23, 42]

quizzes.scenario: Two days before a witness is to testify, Mr. Sani, the counsel, meets with him and goes over the sequence of events and the questions he will be asked in court to refresh his memory.
options: This is professional misconduct known as witness coaching.
This is a permitted pre-trial briefing to refresh memory but counsel must not coach the witness.
Counsel should never meet witnesses outside of the courtroom.
Refreshing memory is only allowed with the permission of the court.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Etiquette of the bar and advocacy practice permit a lawyer to refresh a witness's memory before the hearing, provided he does not coach the witness as to the version of events. [4, 43]

quizzes.scenario: Chief Obafemi engages a lawyer, Mr. Segun, to file a breach of contract suit against a construction firm. Mr. Segun immediately files a writ of summons without mentioning the possibility of mediation to his client. What is the ethical implication of Mr. Segun's action?
options: He has acted professionally to ensure a speedy trial
He has committed professional misconduct for failing to inform his client of ADR options
He is not required to mention ADR unless the court orders it
He has only committed a minor discourtesy
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Rule 15(3)(d) of the Rules of Professional Conduct 2023, a lawyer shall not fail or neglect to inform his client of the option of alternative dispute resolution mechanisms before resorting to or continuing litigation on behalf of his client [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: A multinational oil company invites a local contractor to resolve a payment dispute through mediation under the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023. After 45 days, the contractor has not responded to the written invitation. How should the oil company proceed?
options: They must wait for 90 days before taking further action
They may treat the silence as a rejection of the invitation to mediate
They must apply to the High Court to compel the contractor to attend
They must send a second invitation by registered post
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Section 70(2) of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023, where an invitation to mediate is sent and no acceptance is received within 30 days, the party who sent the invitation may treat it as a rejection [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: During a private mediation session regarding a family land dispute, Mr. Okoro admits to the mediator that he forged a signature on a deed of assignment. The mediation fails, and the matter goes to court. The opposing counsel calls the mediator to testify about this admission. Is this testimony admissible?
options: Yes, because admissions against interest are always admissible
No, because all information relating to mediation proceedings shall be kept confidential
Yes, if the court determines it is in the interest of justice
Yes, because the mediator is a competent witness
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 76 of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023, all information relating to mediation proceedings shall be kept confidential, and Section 77(1) specifically prohibits the introduction of admissions made by a party during the course of proceedings as evidence in judicial proceedings [5-7].

quizzes.scenario: Two business partners agree in their partnership deed that "all disputes shall be referred to arbitration." A dispute arises, and one partner files a suit in the High Court. The other partner wants to stop the court proceedings. What must they do?
options: File a statement of defence first and then apply for a stay
Apply for a stay of proceedings before taking any other step in the proceedings
Wait for the judge to refer the matter to ADR suo motu
File an appeal against the court's jurisdiction
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: By Section 5(1) of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023, an application for stay of proceedings must be made to the court after appearance but before taking any other step in the proceedings, such as filing pleadings [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: A contract for the supply of luxury cars is found to be illegal because it violates import restrictions. However, the contract contains a valid arbitration clause. The supplier argues that since the main contract is void, the arbitration clause is also dead. Is this correct?
options: Yes, the clause dies with the contract
No, because an arbitration agreement is an independent agreement under the doctrine of separability
No, but only if the court grants leave to enforce it
Yes, because a void contract cannot produce a valid obligation
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Doctrine of Separability as provided in Section 14(2) of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023, an arbitration clause is treated as an independent contract that remains valid even if the underlying contract is declared a nullity [10, 11].

quizzes.scenario: A mediator facilitates a settlement between a landlord and a tenant. Both parties sign the written agreement. A week later, the landlord changes his mind and refuses to honor the terms, claiming it is not a court judgment. What is the status of the signed agreement?
options: It is a mere "gentleman's agreement" with no legal force
It is binding and enforceable as a contract and can be made a consent judgment
It is only binding if it was witnessed by a High Court Registrar
It is voidable at the option of either party within 30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 82 of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023 provides that a settlement agreement resulting from mediation is binding on the parties and enforceable in court as a contract or consent judgment [12, 13].

quizzes.scenario: A tech firm and a software developer have a dispute. They agree to arbitrate but do not specify the number of arbitrators in their agreement. How many arbitrators will hear the case under Nigerian law?
options: A panel of three arbitrators
A sole arbitrator
Five arbitrators to ensure a majority vote
The Chief Judge of the Federal High Court will decide
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 6 of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023 provides that where there is no agreement as to the number of arbitrators, the arbitral tribunal shall consist of a sole arbitrator [14, 15].

quizzes.scenario: An employee is negotiating a severance package with his former employer. The employee knows that if negotiation fails, he has a 90% chance of winning a lawsuit for wrongful termination which would pay double the current offer. This realization represents which concept?
options: WATNA (Worst Alternative To Negotiated Agreement)
BATNA (Best Alternative To Negotiated Agreement)
Bottom Line
Positional Strategy
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: BATNA (Best Alternative To Negotiated Agreement) is a party's best course of action for satisfying their interest if negotiation fails, such as successful litigation [16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: During a negotiation for the sale of a ship, the buyer's lawyer suddenly states, "I have negotiated everything else, but I do not have the power to agree to this final price without checking with my board of directors." Which tactic is being deployed?
options: Leapfrogging
Lack of Authority (Refer Upstairs)
Take it or Leave it
Nibble
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The "Lack of Authority" or "Refer Upstairs" tactic involves a negotiator participating until the commitment stage and then claiming they must consult a higher authority before the agreement becomes binding [18, 19].

quizzes.scenario: A claimant in Lagos State files a suit for debt recovery. During the Case Management Conference (CMC), the judge notices the matter is purely commercial and involves long-standing business partners. What is the judge's responsibility regarding ADR?
options: The judge must proceed to trial to ensure a binding precedent
The judge should make referrals to the Lagos Multi-Door Court House for amicable settlement
The judge must dismiss the case and ask them to go to LMDC first
The judge has no power to suggest ADR once a suit is filed
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Order 27 Rule 2 of the High Court of Lagos State (Civil Procedure) Rules 2019, one of the agendas for the judge at the CMC is making referrals to the Lagos Multi-Door Court House to promote amicable settlement [20, 21].

quizzes.scenario: Parties in a mediation proceeding agree in writing to use Zoom for all sessions because they are based in different countries. The mediator insists on a physical meeting. Can the mediator overrule the parties' agreement on electronic sessions?
options: Yes, the mediator controls the process entirely
No, because parties may agree that mediation be conducted by electronic means under the Act
Yes, if the mediator believes physical presence is necessary for empathy
Only if the court approves the electronic sessions
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 73(5) of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023 provides that parties may agree that all or any mediation sessions be conducted by electronic means or video conference [22, 23].

quizzes.scenario: An arbitrator is appointed to resolve a dispute between two neighbors over a shared driveway. During the preliminary meeting, the neighbor discovers the arbitrator is the claimant's first cousin. What should the neighbor do?
options: Nothing, as the appointment is already final
Challenge the appointment within 14 days of becoming aware of the arbitrator's relationship
Sue the arbitrator for fraud in the High Court
Walk out of the proceeding and ignore the award
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 8 and 9 of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023 allow a party to challenge an arbitrator where circumstances exist showing justifiable doubts as to their impartiality or independence, typically within 14 days of awareness [24, 25].

quizzes.scenario: A bank and a debtor conclude a mediation and reach an agreement in writing. The bank later seeks to enforce this settlement agreement in court. What must the bank supply to the court to rely on the agreement?
options: A video recording of the mediation session
The signed settlement agreement and evidence that it emanated from mediation
A letter from the NBA Chairman approving the settlement
A fresh writ of summons to prove the debt again
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 83(1) of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023 requires a party relying on a settlement agreement to supply the signed agreement and evidence that it resulted from mediation, such as the mediator's signature or attestation [26, 27].

quizzes.scenario: A claimant is suing for damages arising from a car accident. The statutory limitation period is 6 years. The claimant enters into a mediation agreement with the defendant 5 years and 11 months after the accident. Does the time continue to run?
options: Yes, mediation does not affect the limitation period
No, the limitation period regarding the claim is suspended while mediation proceedings commence
Yes, unless a court order stays the time
No, but only if the mediation results in a settlement
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 71(1) of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023 provides that where a matter is the subject of mediation, the limitation period regarding the claim is suspended while mediation proceedings commence [3, 28].

quizzes.scenario: A mediator suggests a specific settlement figure to parties in a commercial dispute. The parties disagree with the figure. Can the mediator force them to accept it because it is "fair"?
options: Yes, the mediator's decision is final and binding
No, the mediator does not have the authority to impose a solution on the parties
Yes, if the parties had initially agreed to "facilitative mediation"
Only if the suggestion is endorsed by a judge
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The definition of mediation in Section 91 of the AMA 2023 and the role described in Section 73(6) clarify that the mediator does not have the authority to impose upon the parties a solution to the dispute [29, 30].

quizzes.scenario: An arbitral tribunal in Abuja makes an award in a domestic commercial dispute. The losing party is unhappy and wants to challenge it. Within what timeframe must they apply to the court to set aside the award?
options: Within 14 days from the date of the award
Within three months from the date of the award
Within one year from the date of the award
Anytime before the award is enforced
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 307 of the sources (citing Old Act) and generally under arbitral practice, a party may apply to set aside an award within three months from the date the award was made [31].

quizzes.scenario: A settlement agreement is reached between a French company and a Nigerian firm through mediation in Lagos. The Nigerian firm wants to ensure the agreement is enforceable internationally under the Singapore Convention. Is this possible?
options: No, international agreements can only be enforced by the UN
Yes, provided Nigeria is a party to the Singapore Convention and the relationship is commercial
Only if the mediation was conducted in French
No, the Singapore Convention does not apply to Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 87 of the Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023 provides that the Singapore Convention shall apply to the enforcement of international settlement agreements resulting from mediation, provided the State is a party and the relationship is commercial [32, 33].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer represents a client in a negotiation for the lease of an office complex. The parties reach an agreement. What is the final role the lawyer must perform according to the negotiation process?
options: Act as a witness to the signatures
Draft and prepare the terms of settlement in the proper written form
File a motion for judgment in the High Court immediately
Serve as the stakeholder for the first month's rent
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A vital role of a legal practitioner at the conclusion of negotiation is acting as a draftsman to ensure the agreement is reduced into writing in proper form [34, 35].

quizzes.scenario: A dispute arises from a smart contract executed on a digital ledger. The parties want to use an ADR method that specifically deals with codes and blockchain technology. What is this called?
options: Online Mediation
Blockchain Arbitration
Digital Conciliation
Smart Litigation
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Blockchain Arbitration is a form of Online Dispute Resolution (ODR) developed specifically for disputes arising from smart contracts written in codes [36, 37].

quizzes.scenario: Two parties are in a heated dispute. They meet with a neutral third party who listens to both sides and then prepares a formal document suggesting "Terms of Settlement" which he asks them to sign. What process is this?
options: Arbitration
Conciliation
Litigation
Puffs
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Conciliation involves a neutral third party who suggests solutions and presents terms of settlement for the parties to adopt or reject [38-40].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Tunde recently opened his private law office in Ikeja. He plans to notify the local branch of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) of his office address three months later, once he has fully settled in. Is this timing compliant with professional ethics?
options: Yes, settlement is a valid reason for delay
No, he must notify the NBA within 30 days of commencement of practice
No, he must notify the NBA before he even opens the office
Yes, provided he pays his practicing fees for the year
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 13(1) of the RPC 2023 provides that a legal practitioner shall, not later than 30 days after the commencement of such practice, deliver to the Branch of the NBA in the jurisdiction a Notice of his law office location. [1-3]

quizzes.scenario: Bolanle practices alone as a legal practitioner but uses the name "Bolanle, Ade & Co." on her letterhead to create an impression of a large, established firm. Is this naming style permissible under Nigerian law?
options: Yes, it is a common marketing strategy
No, a sole practitioner cannot use a name suggesting a partnership
Yes, provided she has at least two junior lawyers as employees
No, unless she is a Senior Advocate of Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 5(4) of the RPC 2023 prohibits a lawyer practicing alone from holding himself out to the public as if he is in partnership by using a name like "and Co" which suggests a partnership. [4-6]

quizzes.scenario: Chief Jude, a veteran lawyer, and Mr. Hassan, a renowned Chartered Accountant, decide to form a multi-disciplinary partnership to provide "one-stop" legal and financial services. Is this arrangement ethically sound?
options: Yes, it promotes efficiency in professional service
No, a lawyer must not enter into a partnership with a non-lawyer for legal practice
Yes, if Chief Jude owns at least 51% of the firm
No, unless the General Council of the Bar grants a waiver
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 5(1) of the RPC 2023 prohibits a legal practitioner from entering into a partnership with a non-lawyer for the purpose of practicing the legal profession. [5, 7-9]

quizzes.scenario: Dr. Amadi is a full-time Senior Lecturer in the Faculty of Law at a Federal University. He decides to open a private law firm in the city to increase his income. Based on the Code of Conduct for Public Officers, can he run this firm?
options: Yes, law lecturers are exempt from all restrictions
No, a public officer shall not manage or run any private business except farming
Yes, provided it does not interfere with his lecture hours
No, unless he practices as a solicitor only
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 2(b) of Part 1 of the 5th Schedule to the 1999 Constitution provides that a public officer shall not, except where not employed on a full-time basis, engage or participate in the management or running of any private business, profession, or trade except farming. [10-12]

quizzes.scenario: A high-profile politician requests that his lawyer, Mr. Silas, come to his private residence every Saturday to give legal advice and take instructions on pending litigation to ensure maximum privacy. What is the ethical position for Mr. Silas?
options: He must comply because the client is a high-profile figure
He should refuse as a lawyer should not take instructions at a client's house except in special circumstances
He must comply because Saturday is not a working day
He can only comply if the client pays an "out-of-office" premium fee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 22 of the RPC 2023 provides that a lawyer shall not call at a client's house or place of business for the purpose of giving advice or taking instructions except in special circumstances or for some other urgent reason. [13-16]

quizzes.scenario: Following the conclusion of a long land dispute, Chief Kola requests his lawyer, Mrs. Funmi, to return the original Certificate of Occupancy he gave her at the beginning of the brief. Mrs. Funmi insists on keeping the original for her records. Who is correct?
options: Mrs. Funmi, as it is part of her case file
Chief Kola, as the original document belongs to the client
Mrs Funmi, as she has a permanent lien on all originals
Both, they should keep the document in joint custody
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The case file and documents are generally the property of the client, and original copies of documents given to the lawyer should be returned to the client at the conclusion of the matter. [17-19]

quizzes.scenario: A law firm, "Okoro & Partners," has successfully closed a client's file. The firm is now deciding how long they must retain the file before destruction. What is the recommended period?
options: One year after the judgment
Indefinitely
At least a year longer than the limitation period for that particular action
Exactly 10 years for all cases
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: It has been suggested that the length of time for keeping closed files should be at least a year longer than the limitation period for the particular type of action or subject matter. [20-22]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Akpan, an aspirant for the rank of Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN), currently employs three junior lawyers in his firm. According to the LPPC Guidelines, does his office meet the personnel requirement for the rank?
options: Yes, three juniors are sufficient
No, he must have at least five full-time junior legal practitioners
No, he must have at least ten juniors
Yes, as long as he has at least one secretary and a librarian
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Second Filter Requirements for Advocates in the 2022 Guidelines for the Conferment of SAN, an applicant's office must have at least five full-time junior legal practitioners in salaried employment. [23-25]

quizzes.scenario: Three lawyers contribute capital to rent a large office in Abuja. They share the reception and library but maintain independent practices, separate clients, and do not share profits. What type of law office unit is this?
options: Partnership
Sole Proprietorship
Associateship
Sole Practitionership
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An Associateship is a unit of practice where two or more lawyers pool resources to share office facilities and staff costs, but each maintains a separate practice and independent clients without sharing profits. [26, 27]

quizzes.scenario: The partners of "Excel Law Firm" are setting their annual goals. They ensure that the achievement of their financial target will directly facilitate the upgrade of their law library. Which criterion of goal setting (CRAMS) does this reflect?
options: Specific
Attainable
Complementary
Related to time
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Goals are said to be Complementary when they are formulated in such a way that the achievement of one goal brings about or facilitates the achievement of others. [28, 29]

quizzes.scenario: A newly established firm is deciding on its filing system. They choose to keep all files in a single, central room managed by a dedicated file clerk to ensure better control and security. What is this policy called?
options: Decentralized filing
Alphabetical filing
Centralized filing
Non-alphabetical filing
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A centralized filing policy involves keeping all firm records in one location under the control of specific staff, rather than keeping them in individual lawyers' offices. [30]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Bello, a partner in "Bello & Co," has just been elevated to the Bench as a High Court Judge. He wants his name to remain as the lead name in the firm's title to maintain its reputation. Is this permissible?
options: Yes, provided he does not participate in active practice
No, the name of a serving judicial officer must not appear in the firm's name
Yes, if the other partners agree in writing
No, unless he is the founding partner
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 5(3) of the RPC 2023 provides that a legal practitioner who is elevated to the Bench as a judge shall not continue to have his name as part of a partnership name. [4-7]

quizzes.scenario: After 30 years of practice, the senior partner of "Uche & Sons" passes away. The surviving partners wish to continue using the firm's name. Under what condition is this allowed?
options: It is never allowed; the firm must change its name immediately
It is allowed provided it does not lead to deception or imposition
It is only allowed for six months after the death
It is allowed only if the deceased partner was a SAN
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 5(2) of the RPC 2023 provides that the name of a deceased or former partner may be retained in the firm name, provided it does not lead to misrepresentation or deception. [5, 6, 8]

quizzes.scenario: A firm is preparing its Business Plan to secure a startup loan from a bank. Which of the following professionals is traditionally recommended to prepare the financial projections for this plan?
options: A Senior Advocate of Nigeria
An Estate Agent
An Accountant
A Litigation Clerk
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A business plan containing financial projections for a proposed law firm is normally prepared by an Accountant for the owner. [31, 32]

quizzes.scenario: "Standard Chambers" uses an ICT package that automatically sends reminders to all counsel about upcoming court dates and filing deadlines. Which function of Law Office Management does this ICT usage primarily support?
options: Online Library
Financial Transaction
Case Management
Incorporation Services
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Using ICT for Case Management includes the use of automated diary systems and automated updates of pending matters and status to support law office administration. [33-36]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Efosa, a senior lawyer, believes that his personal memory is sufficient for tracking his court dates. However, after missing a crucial hearing, his partners insist on a formal system. According to management experts, what is the most effective personal reminder system?
options: A wall calendar
Relying on the litigation clerk
The use of Diaries
A collection of sticky notes
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The most effective personal reminder system for a legal practitioner is the use of Diaries both for personal and office use. [37, 38]

quizzes.scenario: A law firm is conducting an induction program for three new junior associates. What is the primary purpose of this induction?
options: To test their legal knowledge
To inform them about the culture, history, and administrative procedures of the firm
To assign them their first 50 cases
To collect their practicing fee receipts
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The purpose of staff induction is to integrate new staff into the working system and inform them about the history of the firm, its culture, and administrative procedures. [39, 40]

quizzes.scenario: While evaluating the firm's performance, the partners of "Prime Law Firm" hire an external auditor to review their income, professional fees realized, and overhead costs. This activity is part of which management function?
options: Coordinating
Planning
Evaluating
Organizing
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Evaluating is a management function that involves the analysis of legal work done, fees realized, and overheads, often achieved through periodic auditing to assess financial performance. [41-43]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Gani requires funds to buy office furniture and a car for his new law firm. What type of capital is this?
options: Working capital
Equity capital
Start-up capital
Nominal capital
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Start-up capital is required for the provision of fixed facilities needed by the firm before it can operate, such as premises, furniture, and vehicles. [44, 45]

quizzes.scenario: A firm's Office Manual contains a detailed section on what happens if a staff member is consistently late to work. Which of the following would be an appropriate procedure to include in this manual for such a breach?
options: Summary dismissal without hearing
Verbal warning followed by a query
Immediate salary cut
Public reprimand at the court premises
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A law office establishes disciplinary procedures in its office manual for staff misconduct, which typically include verbal warnings, queries, and formal written warnings. [46, 47]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Emeka, a solicitor, just finished a three-year litigation for ABC Ltd. He wants to officially retire the file to the archive. What is the proper physical procedure to mark the file as concluded?
options: Write "END" in red ink across the file
Draw two parallel lines across the face with "CLOSED" in upper case between them
Stamp the file with a "COMPLETED" seal and date it
Place the file in a black box labeled "ARCHIVE"
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Once a matter or transaction is concluded, the standard procedure is to draw two parallel lines across the face of the file and write the word CLOSED in upper case letters between them [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: Chief Bola has a general retainer with Lex Chambers. The firm just concluded a specific debt recovery matter for him, but continues to handle his other corporate affairs. Which file(s) should be closed?
options: Both the personal file and the debt recovery file
Only the personal file
Only the specific case file for the debt recovery matter
Neither file until the retainer is terminated
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A client's personal file generally remains open until the retainer is terminated, even if a specific case or brief handled for that client has been concluded and closed [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: After a successful divorce petition, Mrs. Aisha demands her entire case file from her lawyer, Mr. Oke. Mr. Oke refuses, claiming the file is firm property because he did the work. Who is correct?
options: Mr. Oke, because he created the documents
Mrs. Aisha, because the file is the client's property
Mr. Oke, provided he keeps the file for 7 years
Mrs. Aisha, but only if she pays a retrieval fee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The file is the client's property and should generally be returned to him at the conclusion of a matter [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: A firm is closing a land transaction file. They have the original Title Deed and several photocopies. What is the ethically recommended practice regarding these documents?
options: Return only the photocopies to the client
Return the original documents to the client and keep photocopies
Keep both originals and photocopies in the archive
Destroy the originals and return photocopies
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Original copies of documents given to the lawyer should be returned to the client, while it is good practice for the lawyer to make and retain photocopies before returning the originals [7].

quizzes.scenario: A firm is organizing its archive. They have a closed file for a simple contract where the limitation period for an action is 6 years. What is the suggested minimum period to keep this file before destruction?
options: 6 years
5 years
7 years
10 years
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: While not statutorily prescribed, it is suggested that files be kept for at least a year longer than the limitation period for that particular type of action or subject matter [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Musa has 500 closed files from 1995 and needs to create space in his small office. What is the correct ethical procedure before he destroys them?
options: Burn them immediately to protect confidentiality
Sell them to a paper recycling plant
Provide reasonable notice to the clients to collect them
Shred them without notice if 20 years have passed
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Files may be destroyed after a reasonable length of time only after reasonable notice has been given to the client to collect them [5, 6, 9].

quizzes.scenario: When placing a closed file on a shelf in the archive, what information should be written on the spine to facilitate easy future retrieval?
options: The date the file was closed
The total professional fees paid
The suit number and name of parties or subject matter
The name of the junior counsel who handled it
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: For easy future identification and retrieval, a closed case file should have the suit number and name of parties or the subject matter written on its spine [1, 10].

quizzes.scenario: A transaction is finished, but the client still owes 20% of the professional fees. What should the lawyer include with the letter informing the client that the file is closed?
options: A threat to report the client to the NBA
A bill of charges for outstanding fees
A notice that the file has been shredded
A request for a new retainer
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: At the conclusion of a matter, the solicitor writes a letter to the client informing them of the closing of the file, which is usually accompanied by a bill of charges for any outstanding fees [5, 11].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Z terminates his brief with Firm A and hires Firm B. According to the Rules of Professional Conduct, which of the following must Firm A hand over to the client?
options: The lawyer's personal research notes
All letters written by the client to the lawyer
All letters written by the lawyer to third parties at the client's direction
Only the original instruction letter
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Upon a change of counsel, the former lawyer must hand over all letters (and copies) written to other persons at the direction of the client, as well as drafts and copies made in the course of business [12, 13].

quizzes.scenario: A law firm is running out of physical space and decides to digitize all old paper files. Is this ethically permissible under Nigerian practice guidelines before destroying physical copies?
options: No, legal files must be kept in hard copy indefinitely
Yes, the firm may elect to store the content electronically before destruction
Only if the Chief Registrar of the Supreme Court approves
Only if the file is more than 20 years old
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Before destroying a file after the retention period, a firm may elect to store the content electronically [6].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is closing a file for a criminal matter where the client was acquitted. Which physical action is part of the standard procedure before placing the file in a case?
options: Stapling the file shut for security
Tying the file up with ribbons
Placing the file in a red folder
Removing all internal correspondence
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Once a file is marked "CLOSED" and identified, it is tied up with ribbons and placed in a file case [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: Client Y moves to another firm but owes Lawyer X a significant balance of fees. Lawyer X refuses to release the documents in the case file until paid. Is this legally permissible?
options: No, it is a breach of the duty to serve the client
Yes, the lawyer is entitled to a lien on the client's papers for unpaid fees
No, files must be surrendered immediately upon demand
Yes, but only if the court grants an injunction
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: When a client changes counsel, the old lawyer is entitled to a lien on the papers or documents of the client in respect of unpaid fees [13-15].

quizzes.scenario: Why is it professionally necessary for a legal practitioner to open a formal file immediately upon taking a brief?
options: To justify charging an "opening fee"
To comply with the Companies and Allied Matters Act
To avoid losing important information or material relating to the matter
To ensure the file is ready for the NBA's annual inspection
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A file must be opened once a lawyer takes a brief so that the lawyer does not lose important information or material relating to that specific matter [3].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer finishes a brief but fails to send a formal closing letter. Five years later, the client sues for the return of original documents. What was the lawyer's primary administrative error?
options: Not charging the client for storage
Failure to formally close the file and return the property upon conclusion
Not shredding the documents after three years
Not asking for a "termination fee"
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Once a case or transaction is concluded, the lawyer should close the file and inform the client, typically returning original documents at that stage [3, 5].

quizzes.scenario: A law firm decides to destroy all files that have been closed for exactly seven years to free up space. Does this duration align with Nigerian practice suggestions?
options: No, files must be kept for at least 15 years
Yes, closed files may be retained for a maximum period of seven years
No, files can only be destroyed with a court order
Yes, but only if the matter was a criminal felony
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: It has been suggested that closed files may be retained for a maximum period of seven years, often modeled on when a matter becomes statute-barred plus one year [6].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer is returning a concluded file to a client. What is a specific administrative merit of this action for the law firm?
options: It allows the firm to charge a "surrender fee"
The law firm is saved the responsibility and cost of preserving the file
It automatically triggers a new retainership
It prevents the client from hiring a different lawyer later
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Returning the file is beneficial because the law firm is saved the responsibility and cost of preserving the file in its own archive [6].

quizzes.scenario: Which of the following should NOT be destroyed when a lawyer eventually decides to dispose of a very old closed case file?
options: Internal office memos regarding the case
Original and important documents
Photocopies of previous court rulings
Drafts of letters to opposing counsel
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: When destroying case files, original and important documents are not to be destroyed [9].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer concludes a matter and stores the file in a separate room dedicated to inactive, closed matters. What is the proper term for this room?
options: The Registry
The Vault
The Archive
The Library
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A separate room (if any) meant for closed files is properly called an archive [1, 10].

quizzes.scenario: In the event of a change of counsel, the former lawyer is ethically entitled to keep which of the following records?
options: All drafts and copies made in the course of business
All letters written by the client to the lawyer
All original certificates provided by the client
Copies of all letters written to third parties
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Upon a change of counsel, while many documents must be handed over, the old lawyer is entitled to keep all letters written by the client to the lawyer [13, 14].

quizzes.scenario: What is the significance of the "two parallel lines" and the word "CLOSED" in upper case on a file?
options: It is a signal for the accounts department to delete the client
It is the standard physical procedure for closing a case or transaction file
It is a requirement for SAN application purposes
It indicates that the firm's lien has been satisfied
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: This specific physical marking is the prescribed act for closing a case or transaction file once it has been concluded or brought to an end [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: A Lagos-based law firm, "Legal Aces," recently transitioned from physical files to an electronic file management system to save office space. However, during a court hearing, the Lead Counsel discovered that a critical document was missing because it was not properly scanned. Which benefit of ICT did the firm aim to achieve, and what was the corresponding challenge they faced?
options: Improved research and data transmission
Space-saving and reduction of physical files; challenge of lack of requisite IT personnel/skills
Enhanced confidentiality and privacy
Elimination of all human errors in litigation
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The firm aimed for the benefit of providing more office space due to fewer files [1], but the challenge was the lack of requisite IT skills or personnel to ensure all documents were correctly digitized [2].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Ada uses Microsoft Excel to track all client payments, professional fees, and office expenditures. A client disputes the calculation of a success-based fee in a property matter. Barrister Ada presents a printed spreadsheet showing the automated calculations. Which ICT usage is Barrister Ada employing?
options: Case Management
Online Library Usage
Safe keeping of data and financial transactions
Electronic Communication
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Barrister Ada is using ICT for safe keeping of data and financial transactions, specifically using spreadsheets for fee calculation and income/expenditure analysis [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: A junior associate at "Quick Justice Chambers" accidentally opened a phishing email, resulting in a ransomware attack that encrypted all client folders. The firm had no offline backups. Which solution to ICT challenges should the firm have implemented?
options: Use of genuine IT equipment only
Regular power supply via an inverter
Use of system back-up and effective anti-virus software
Proper legal framework for ICT products
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: To mitigate risks like system damage by viruses or hackers, law firms are advised to use system backups and effective anti-virus software [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: Chief Okafor, a Senior Advocate of Nigeria, maintains an expensive firm website. The "About Us" section states that the firm has a "100% success rate in criminal appeals" and is "the most sophisticated firm in West Africa." Which ethical rule regarding ICT usage has been breached?
options: Rule 13 on Notification of Legal Practice
Rule 39 on Advertising and Soliciting
Rule 19 on Client Confidentiality
Rule 7 on Engaging in Business
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: While a law firm may hoist a website, Rule 39(2)(d) of the RPC prohibits any statement about the quality of the lawyer's work, size, or success rate of his practice [7-9].

quizzes.scenario: During a complex commercial trial, the presiding Judge uses a device that automatically converts his handwritten notes into typed text for the final judgment. What is this ICT tool called?
options: Optical Disk
Modem
Note-Taker
Peachtree Software
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A Note-Taker is a tool used in effective case management that aids judges by converting handwritten notes into typed text [10].

quizzes.scenario: A firm in a rural part of Ebonyi State frequently experiences total blackouts, preventing them from accessing their "Law Pavilion" electronic database during urgent research. Which solution is most appropriate for this firm to sustain its ICT infrastructure?
options: Hiring more IT staff
Providing alternative power supply like a generator or inverter
Switching back to manual paper catalogues
Filing a complaint with the NBA branch
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Regular power supply, including the provision of alternative power like a generator or inverter, is a primary solution to the challenge of erratic power supply in law office management [5, 11].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Kunle needs to find a 1985 Supreme Court decision to support his motion. Instead of going to a physical library, he uses his tablet to access an online database that stores scholarly materials and law reports. What is this facility technically called?
options: Automated sound recording system
Virtual or E-library
Management Information System
Spreadsheet package
