quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Anton Piller injunction is an ex-parte application to inspect premises and seize infringing items or evidence of piracy.

quizzes.scenario:
Mr. John piracy dispute. Appropriate forum for copyright infringement:

The appropriate court where the application you have identified above (Anton Piller injunction for book piracy) should be brought is:
options: The Federal High Court
The High Court of Lagos State
The High Court of the Federal Capital Territory
The National Industrial Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 251(1)(q) of the 1999 Constitution, the Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction over matters relating to copyrights, patents, designs and trademarks.

quizzes.scenario:
INTERPLEADER APPLICATIONS

Commercial Trust Bank Plc has been notified that one of its biggest customers Chief Akin Tanko has died. The Bank got the information via two letters from two different lawyers representing the eldest son of each of Chief Tanko's wives. None of the letters cited a letter of administration or will but each demanded that the Bank pay the credit balance in the deceased's account to their client. The Bank has consulted you as an external solicitor for advice.

What will you advise the Bank to do?
options: Share the money in equal half and deposit in the account of the rival claimants.
Apply to the court to order the claimants to take action between themselves to determine who is entitled to the money in the account via Interpleader.
Pay the money to the government account
Deposit the money with the Central Bank of Nigeria.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The bank should file an interpleader application, which allows a neutral stakeholder facing rival claims to request the court to decide the ownership of the property.

quizzes.scenario:
Commercial Trust Bank Plc interpleader action:

One of the following is not an essential fact that the Bank will state in the affidavit in support of the interpleader application:
options: That the bank has not concluded with the rival claimants
That the bank is willing to pay the money to any person that the court directs
That the bank has a special interest in the outcome of the case
That the bank has no interest in the money other than charges for its services.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The applicant in an interpleader must depose that they claim no interest in the subject-matter in dispute other than for charges or costs. Order 47, Rule 2(2), Lagos.

quizzes.scenario:
Commercial Trust Bank Plc interpleader action:

The procedure the bank will adopt is called:
options: Sheriff interpleader
Court interpleader
Bank interpleader
Stakeholder interpleader
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Stakeholder interpleader is the procedure where a person holding property (the stakeholder) is sued or expects to be sued by two or more rival claimants.

quizzes.scenario:
Commercial Trust Bank Plc interpleader action:

Assuming the two eldest children file processes in court, what order can the court make to prevent the bank from paying the money to any of the claimants until the case is determined?
options: Certiorari
Mandamus
Interlocutory injunction
Interim injunction
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Interlocutory injunction lasts till the determination of the suit, maintaining the status quo.

quizzes.scenario:
DEFAULT AND SUMMARY JUDGMENT PROCEDURE

Babatunde Oyemo sued your client, Dodo Osadunde at the FCT High Court for the liquidated sum of N400,000,000 representing his share of the profit from a joint venture allegedly entered by your client and Mr. Babatunde. Unfortunately, your client was in New York when substituted service was effected on him. He came back to Nigeria two months later to discover that he had been sued. Two days after, he was served with an application for default judgment which is slated for hearing on the 20th of May, 2020. He immediately briefed you to represent him.

The step you will take to ensure that the case is heard on its merit and not in default is:
options: Apply for the application for default judgment to be set aside
Seek leave to file statement of defence out of time
Seek leave of court to appear out of time
Seek leave to file memorandum of appearance, statement of defence and necessary documents out of time
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: To defend the suit on the merit, the defendant must apply to file memorandum of appearance, statement of defence, and other accompanying documents out of time, supported by a motion for extension of time.

quizzes.scenario:
Babatunde Oyemo joint venture debt. Dodo Osadunde's defence:

The mode by which the application you seek above (extension of time) will be brought is:
options: Motion on notice
Originating motion
Motion ex parte
Petition
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: It is necessary that the other party be put on notice when seeking leave to enter appearance out of time. Hence, a motion on notice is required.

quizzes.scenario:
Babatunde Oyemo joint venture debt. Setting aside default judgment:

Assuming judgment had already been given against your client in default of appearance/pleading, and you would like to set it aside, the application you would bring would be by:
options: Motion on notice
Originating motion
Motion ex parte
Petition
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An application to set aside a default judgment is brought by way of motion on notice before the same court.

quizzes.scenario:
Babatunde Oyemo joint venture debt. Setting aside conditions:

The following are conditions to be fulfilled before a default judgment can be set aside by the granting judge except:
options: Evidence of payment of penalty / default fees
Application to set aside must be brought within a reasonable time
Undertaking as to damages
A good defence on the merit to the claim
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An undertaking as to damages is a condition for the grant of interim or interlocutory injunctions, not for setting aside a default judgment. Order 10, Rule 11, Abuja.

quizzes.scenario:
PLEADINGS

Rasheed Ijaodola commenced an action against Yusuf Ali at the High Court of Lagos State for a declaration of title to land and claim of N10,000,000 (Ten Million Naira) as unliquidated damages caused by the trespass committed on the land which he claims has caused a depreciation on the value of the land. He believed that the defendant does not have any defence to the claim because he is armed with all the evidence. The originating process was served on the defendant on the 1st of March, 2020 by Barrister Sergio.

Within how many days after receipt of the originating process is the defendant expected to file his statement of defence?
options: 21 days
42 days
14 days
7 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A defendant in Lagos is expected to file statement of defence within 42 days of receiving an originating process. Order 17, Rule 1(2), Lagos.

quizzes.scenario:
Rasheed Ijaodola land trespass case in Lagos:

If the claimant decides to reply to the statement of defence, within how many days after the receipt of the statement of defence must he file the reply?
options: 14 days
7 days
21 days
42 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Order 17, Rule 1(4) of the Lagos Rules, the claimant has 14 days after service of the statement of defence to file a reply.

quizzes.scenario:
Rasheed Ijaodola land trespass case in Lagos:

Assuming the defendant fails to file his statement of defence within the prescribed time period by the Rules of Court, pleadings shall be deemed closed:
options: After the 42 day prescribed period
After the 21 days prescribed period
After the 14 days prescribed period
After the 7 days prescribed period
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Order 17, Rule 18, Lagos. If a party fails to file a pleading within the time limit, pleadings are deemed closed upon expiration of that time.

quizzes.scenario:
Rasheed Ijaodola land trespass case. Counterclaim heading:

Assuming the action brought by the claimant is one for the recovery of debt from the defendant in the sum of N100,000; in addition to the statement of defence filed by the defendant, he counterclaims that the claimant owes him the amount of N70,000. As the counsel to the claimant, which of the following is the appropriate heading of the document to be filed?
options: Reply to counter claim and defence to statement of defence
Response to statement of defence and reply to counter claim
Reply to statement of defence and defence to counter claim
Response to counter claim and reply to statement of defence
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The claimant is expected to reply to the statement of defence and provide a defence to the counterclaim, compiled under a single heading: 'Reply to Statement of Defence and Defence to Counter-claim'.

quizzes.scenario:
Rasheed Ijaodola land trespass case. Conflicting claims:

In the originating process filed by Barrister Sergio on behalf of his client, the claim of N10,000,000 as damages was endorsed on it, however, he omitted this claim in the statement of claim. At the trial, what is the effect of the omission?
options: It is still deemed to be properly claimed as it is contained in the originating process
It will lead to striking out of the action
The effect is based on the discretion of the court
It is deemed abandoned
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The principle is that any claim on the writ which is omitted in the statement of claim is deemed abandoned. Elf Nigeria Ltd v Sillo. The statement of claim supersedes the writ in the event of conflict.

quizzes.scenario:
Rasheed Ijaodola land trespass case. Statement of claim anatomy:

All but one of the following are essential parts of the statement of claim filed by Rasheed Ijaodola:
options: Matters of inducement (introductory part)
The main body of facts
Reliefs/Prayers
None of the above
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The parts of a statement of claim are: introductory facts (matters of inducement), main body (substantive facts), and reliefs sought. All are essential, hence None of the above.

quizzes.scenario:
Rasheed Ijaodola land trespass case. Set-off doctrine:

All of the following are incorrect about set-off except:
options: It can be used as a shield as well as a sword
It can only be used where there are monetary claims
It is used specifically for monetary claims and declaratory reliefs
It is available for both a claimant and a defendant in a suit
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Set off is only applicable for monetary claims. It is a shield and not a sword, available only to the defendant in an action.

quizzes.scenario:
PRE-TRIAL ISSUES AND PRE-TRIAL PROCEEDINGS

In a case instituted by George Gregor at the High Court of the FCT against McCarthy over a large expanse of land at the Maitama District, Abuja, parties have exchanged pleadings, joined issues and the case has been set for pre-trial conference. You are an extern in the office of Mr. Goodness, counsel to the claimant.

Within how many days after the close of pleadings by the parties is the claimant expected to apply for issuance of pre-trial conference notice in Abuja?
options: 15 days
7 days
14 days
5 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In Abuja, the claimant must apply for the pre-trial conference notice within 14 days of close of pleadings. Order 27, Rule 1, Abuja.

quizzes.scenario:
George Gregor v McCarthy property dispute. Pre-trial notice defaults:

If the claimant fails to apply for the pre-trial conference notice within the prescribed period of 14 days, which of the following options is open to the defendant?
options: Apply for the issuance of the pre-trial conference notice
Apply for the dismissal of the case
Apply for the case to be set for trial
Apply for issuance of the pre-trial notice OR apply for dismissal of the case
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: If the claimant defaults, the defendant may apply for the pre-trial notice or apply to dismiss the suit for want of prosecution.

quizzes.scenario:
George Gregor v McCarthy property dispute. Forms:

The pre-trial notice and pre-trial information sheet issued to your principal was in form............ of the Abuja Rules:
options: Form 18 and 19
Form 19 and 20
Form 17 and 18
Form 20 and 21
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Abuja High Court Rules, pre-trial conference processes are in Forms 18 and 19.

quizzes.scenario:
George Gregor v McCarthy property dispute. Absence at conference:

On the date fixed for the pre-trial conference, neither the defendant nor his counsel was present for the proceeding. Which of the following represents the proper order the Court would give in the circumstance?
options: Dismiss the action
Set the action for trial immediately
Strike out the action
Enter judgment against the defendant or make a default order as the judge deems fit
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: If the defendant fails to attend, the court may enter judgment/make default order against him. Order 27, Rule 4, Abuja.

quizzes.scenario:
George Gregor v McCarthy property dispute. Claimant absence:

If it was the claimant and his legal practitioner that were absent at the pre-trial conference, the order the Court would give is:
options: Enter judgment against the claimant
Set the action for trial immediately
Dismiss the action
Strike out the action
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: If the claimant fails to attend the pre-trial conference, the court shall dismiss the action. Order 27, Rule 4, Abuja.

quizzes.scenario:
George Gregor v McCarthy property dispute. Setting aside default:

Assuming judgment was given against a defendant for failure to participate in the pre-trial conference and he wishes to set it aside, within how many days after the judgment must the application to set aside be brought before the Court?
options: 14 days
3 days
7 days
10 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An application to set aside any default order/judgment given during the pre-trial conference must be brought within 7 days. Order 27, Rule 4, Abuja.

quizzes.scenario:
You are counsel to Mrs Beatrice Coke in an action she instituted against Soshine Brewery Ltd at the High Court of the FCT for breach of contract of supply and claiming the sum of N5,000,000 as money received for a consideration which had failed. She seeks to rely on the written contract of supply dated Feb. 20, 2020 and the payment receipt dated March 20, 2020. The original copies of these documents were lost.

The process you will draft and serve on the defendant for the discovery of the documents in this case is:
options: Witness summons requesting him to come to court with the documents
Letter requesting him to make discovery on oath / Notice to admit
Notice to produce / Discovery of documents
Subpoena summoning him to come to court with the documents
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The proper process is an application or notice for discovery of documents under Order 28, Rule 8(1), Abuja.

quizzes.scenario:
EVIDENCE

Which of the following is true of the evidence of a child who has not attained the age of 14 years?
options: The child can only give an unsworn evidence
The child can give both sworn and unsworn evidence
The child can only give sworn evidence
The child is capable of giving sworn evidence but can decide not to on ground of religion.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Sec. 209(1), Evidence Act (E.A), 2011. A child below 14 years of age is generally deemed incompetent to give sworn evidence unless they understand the nature of an oath, but can give unsworn evidence if they possess sufficient intelligence.

quizzes.scenario: By the provision of the Evidence Act, before receiving their testimonies in court, caution is required to be given to_____
options: All witnesses
Only witnesses subpoenaed to come to court
Hostile witnesses
Eye witnesses
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: See Sec. 206. Cautioned is required to be given to any witness summoned to give oral evidence.

quizzes.scenario: Which of the following is not a form of primary evidence?
options: Documents executed in several parts
Copy of a document made by a uniform process, like printing
Photocopy of a public document presented in court for inspection
Copies of documents made using carbon paper
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Photocopy of any document is secondary evidence. Sec. 87, E.A.

quizzes.scenario:
In a defamation suit brought by Dr. Gbajabiamila Olowokere against Senator Ahmed Kabiru, where the former claims that the latter has defamed him by making a public statement published in all the national newspapers that he is a quack doctor. The defendant relies on the defence of justification alleging that the claimant possesses a forged certificate.

To sustain his claim, the standard of proof required of the claimant is:
options: Proof beyond reasonable doubt
Balance of probability
Strict proof
Proof to the satisfaction of the Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In civil cases, the standard of proof is balance of probabilities or preponderance of evidence. Sec. 134, E.A.

quizzes.scenario:
Dr. Gbajabiamila v Senator Ahmed Kabiru. Special damages proof:

Assuming Dr. Gbajabiamila claims the sum of N5 million as special damages from Senator Ahmed, to be entitled, the special damages claimed must be:
options: Proved beyond reasonable doubt
Proved on the preponderance of evidence
Proved to the satisfaction of the court
Strictly proved and specially pleaded
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Special damages must be specially pleaded and strictly proved by the party claiming them.

quizzes.scenario:
Dr. Gbajabiamila v Senator Ahmed Kabiru. Secondary evidence of public document:

When the defendant challenges Dr. Gbajabiamila to tender the document of his qualification as a Medical Practitioner in Nigeria, the claimant claims that the document has been destroyed by fire and seeks to tender a secondary evidence of it. What form of secondary evidence of the above document will be admissible?
options: Any secondary evidence of the content of the document is admissible
Only the written statement of the Provost of the College where the certificate was obtained is admissible
Only the certified true copy (CTC) of the document is admissible
Only the photocopy of the document is admissible
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Since the document in question is a public document, the only secondary evidence of a public document that is admissible is the certified true copy. Sec. 89 and 90, E.A.

quizzes.scenario: Which of the following is not a public document under the Evidence Act?
options: Primary school leaving certificate issued by public authority
Certificate of title issued by the Land Registry
Call to Bar certificate
Unregistered deed of assignment between two private entities
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An unregistered deed of assignment between private entities is a private document, not a public document.

quizzes.scenario:
TRIAL, EXAMINATION OF WITNESSES

During a brief hearing in a civil matter before the High court of FCT Abuja, the following conversation ensued:
Counsel: You are Hakeem Tunde of No 12 Asokoro Close, Abuja, a civil servant, am I correct?
Witness: Yes My Lord
Counsel: What do you want to do with your written statement on oath?
Witness: You are my lawyer; you know the best, do whatever you think is right with it.

What stage of examination of witnesses is shown from the scenario?
options: Cross examination
Evidence of good character
Re-examination
Examination-in-chief
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: This represents the introductory phase of Examination-in-chief where the witness is identified and prompted to adopt their written statement on oath.

quizzes.scenario:
Hakeem Tunde civil trial conversation. Question analysis:

What type of question was the first question asked by the counsel in the above scenario ('You are Hakeem Tunde of No 12 Asokoro Close, Abuja, a civil servant, am I correct?')?
options: Leading question
Closed question
Open question
Direct question
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A leading question is one which suggests the answer that the interrogator wishes to receive. The counsel's question is highly leading.

quizzes.scenario:
Hakeem Tunde civil trial conversation. Lack of objection:

The most probable reason why the opposing counsel did not raise objection to the first (leading) question is that:
options: There is no problem with the question at all
The question relates to introductory matters
He is a new wig and didn't want embarrassment
None of the above
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Leading questions are permitted during examination-in-chief in respect of introductory/undisputed matters to save court time.

quizzes.scenario:
Hakeem Tunde civil trial conversation. Written statement on oath adoption:

What was the proper response when the witness was asked what he wants to do with his written statement on oath?
options: My Lord I want to tender the statement in evidence
My Lord please accept the statement since it is on oath
My Lord I adopt the statement on Oath as my oral evidence in this case
None of the above
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A witness must adopt his statement on oath before the Court can treat such as evidence. The adoption must be unequivocal.

quizzes.scenario:
Hakeem Tunde civil trial conversation. Witness adoption follow-up:

If you are the opposing Counsel, what will you do when the witness adopted his written statement on oath?
options: Apply for a short adjournment
Apply for the case to be stood down
Urge the court to declare the witness a hostile witness
Cross-examine the witness based on his written statement on oath and case files
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Once a witness adopts their statement on oath, their evidence-in-chief is complete, and they are presented for cross-examination by the adverse party.

quizzes.scenario:
CLOSING ADDRESS AND JUDGMENT

In a defamation suit brought by Dr. Gbajabiamila Olowokere against Senator Ahmed Kabiru at the High Court of Lagos State, the trial has concluded after both parties led evidence.

Based on the Rules of court, after the close of the trial, who is to file written address first?
options: Dr. Gbajabiamila Olowokere (The Claimant)
Senator Ahmed Kabiru (The Defendant)
Both parties concurrently
It depends on the discretion of the court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under High Court Rules, the party who opens the case (usually the claimant) has the right to address last. Thus, the defendant files their written address first. Order 35, Lagos.

quizzes.scenario:
Dr. Gbajabiamila v Senator Ahmed Kabiru. Final address timeframe in Lagos:

The written address must be filed by the defendant within how many days of the close of evidence?
options: 21 days
14 days
7 days
30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In Lagos, the party who addresses first (defendant) must file within 21 days of close of evidence. Order 35 Rule 2.

quizzes.scenario:
Dr. Gbajabiamila v Senator Ahmed Kabiru. Written address page limit:

By the Rules of Court in Lagos, the written address of the parties shall not exceed how many pages?
options: 30 pages
21 pages
20 pages
40 pages
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A written address shall not exceed 30 pages unless leave of court is obtained. Order 35, Lagos.

quizzes.scenario:
Dr. Gbajabiamila v Senator Ahmed Kabiru. Right of reply:

In this case, who will be entitled to a right of reply on point of law?
options: The court
Both parties
Dr. Gbajabiamila Olowokere (The Claimant)
Senator Ahmed Kabiru (The Defendant)
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The party who addresses first (defendant) is entitled to a reply on points of law only, after receiving the other party's written address.

quizzes.scenario:
ENFORCEMENT OF JUDGMENT AND INTERLOCUTORY APPLICATIONS PENDING APPEAL

In a suit for negligence brought by Dr. Lukeman against Chief Holloway, Justice Caleb Akanni of the High Court of the Federal Capital Territory entered judgment in the sum of N20,000,000 (Twenty Million Naira only) as general and special damages in favour of the claimant.

By the provision of the law, how many days must lapse after the delivery of the judgment before the claimant can commence the process of executing it?
options: 3 days
5 days
7 days
14 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Order 4, Rule 1(2) of the Judgment Enforcement Rules (JER). A judgment debtor has 3 days from the date of judgment to comply before execution processes can be initiated.

quizzes.scenario:
Dr. Lukeman v Chief Holloway. Execution against movables:

Assuming the claimant wants to enforce the judgment by seizing and selling the defendant's two Toyota Camry cars, which of the following will be appropriate?
options: Garnishee proceedings
Writ of fieri facias
Judgment summons
Writ of sequestration
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Writ of fieri facias (fifa) is the appropriate mode of enforcement of judgment where the movable properties of the judgment debtor are targeted as means of settling the judgment sum.

quizzes.scenario:
Dr. Lukeman v Chief Holloway. FiFa forms:

The form that will be filled by the claimant in achieving his goal of issuing a Writ of FiFa under the Sheriffs and Civil Process Act (SCPA) is:
options: FORM 56 of the Schedule to the FCT High Court Rules
FORM 3 of the first schedule to the SCPA
FORM 3 of the schedule to the FCT High Court Rules
FORM 56 of the first schedule to the SCPA
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Form 3 of the First Schedule to the Sheriffs and Civil Process Act is the praecipe for writ of fieri facias.

quizzes.scenario:
Dr. Lukeman v Chief Holloway. Attachment of immovable property:

If the claimant as the Judgment creditor wants to attach the immovable property of the Judgment debtor in satisfaction of the judgment sum, application for leave to do so must be made to:
options: The Magistrate Court
Magistrate or High Court
The High Court
The Registrar of the High Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Order 4, Rule 13, JER. An application to attach and sell immovable property must be made to a Judge of the High Court. Magistrates' court cannot issue writ of sequestration or writ of attachment on immovable property.

quizzes.scenario:
CIVIL APPEALS

The High Court of Lagos State upheld the Judgment of the Magistrate Court in a landlord-tenancy relationship between Chief Eshugbayi and Elemesho in favour of the defendant, Elemesho. The judgment of the High Court was delivered on the 1st of May, 2020. Feeling aggrieved, Chief Eshugbayi has instructed you as his counsel to appeal the decision to the Court of Appeal.

The appeal from the decision of the High Court sitting in its appellate jurisdiction to the Court of Appeal must be filed within:
options: 14 days
3 months
90 days
30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An appeal from a decision of the High Court sitting in its appellate capacity to the Court of Appeal must be brought within 3 months for a final judgment. Section 25(2)(a), Court of Appeal Act.

quizzes.scenario:
Chief Eshugbayi v Elemesho. Need for leave of court:

Which of the following is true of the appeal to the Court of Appeal in this case (appealing High Court's decision sitting as an appellate court)?
options: It must be with the leave of the Court of Appeal or the High Court
It is as of right and no leave is required
It must be with the leave of the Supreme Court
None of the above
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 242 of the Constitution, appeals from decisions of the High Court sitting in its appellate capacity to the Court of Appeal are not as of right; they require the leave of either the High Court or the Court of Appeal.

quizzes.scenario:
Musa Sadau instituted an action for malicious prosecution against Sergeant Ogba at the High Court of Kano State. Judgment was delivered on the 3rd of January, 2020 against Sergeant Ogba who now seeks to appeal the judgment 4 months after. The grounds upon which he wants to appeal are on mixed law and fact.

The application you will file to be able to appeal will be filed at:
options: The High Court of Kano State
The Court of Appeal
Either the High Court or the Court of Appeal
Registry of the High Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: It will be filed at the Court of Appeal because the time within which to appeal (3 months for final judgment) has lapsed and he would need to seek extension of time, which only the Court of Appeal can grant. Sec. 25(4), Court of Appeal Act.

quizzes.scenario:
Musa Sadau v Sergeant Ogba. Trinity prayer:

Which of the following represents the appropriate prayer you will ask in the application you have filed above (seeking extension of time and leave to appeal after time has elapsed on grounds of mixed law and fact)?
options: An order of extension of time to seek leave to appeal only
An order granting leave to appeal only
An order of extension of time granting leave to appeal and an order of extension of time within which to file Notice of Appeal
An order of extension of time within which to seek leave to appeal, an order granting leave to appeal and an order of extension of time within which to file Notice of Appeal
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The 'trinity prayer' is required when an applicant seeks to appeal on grounds requiring leave after the statutory appeal window has closed: extension of time to apply for leave, leave to appeal, and extension of time to file notice of appeal.

quizzes.scenario:
RECOVERY OF PREMISES

Miss Bolanle is a tenant in the property belonging to Mr. Peter Azi situate at No. 22, Gwarinpa Estate, Abuja. It is a yearly tenancy, which commenced on 1st January, 2017. Miss Bolanle has been in default of payment of rent and Mr. Peter Azi has approached you to recover possession of the said premises. The annual rent is N7,000,000 (Seven Million Naira Only).

One of the following must be served on Miss Bolanle in order to determine her tenancy:
options: A 6-month notice to quit which must expire on 31st December (eve of the anniversary)
A 6-month notice which must expire on 1st January
A 6-month notice to expire at the end of January
A 7 days notice of owners intention to recover possession
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Sec 8 and 9 of the Recovery of Premises Act (Abuja). For a yearly tenancy, a 6-month quit notice must be served, and it must expire on the eve of the anniversary of the tenancy.

quizzes.scenario:
Miss Bolanle tenancy recovery. Next step:

Upon service of the quit notice to determine Bolanle's tenancy, what other document are you required to serve on her if she remains in possession?
options: Another notice to quit
7 days Owner's intention to apply to court to recover possession (7 days notice)
Writ of summons
Plaint as in Form E
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: After the quit notice expires, if the tenant fails to deliver possession, the landlord must serve a 7 days notice of owner's intention to apply to court to recover possession. Form B of the Recovery of Premises Act.

quizzes.scenario:
Miss Bolanle tenancy recovery. Forum in Abuja:

Assuming it has become necessary to go to Court to recover possession from Miss Bolanle in Abuja, in which of the following courts will you institute the action?
options: District Court
Magistrate Court
Customary Court, Abuja
High Court of the Federal Capital Territory, Abuja
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The annual rental value is N7,000,000, which exceeds the N5,000,000 monetary jurisdiction of the District Court in Abuja. Hence, the High Court of the FCT has jurisdiction.

quizzes.scenario:
Miss Bolanle tenancy recovery. Forum in Lagos:

If the property was in Lagos, and the rent is N7,000,000, in which court would you have instituted the action?
options: High Court of Lagos State
Magistrate Court of Lagos State
Customary Court of Lagos State
Small Claims Court of Lagos State
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: This is because the annual rental value of the property (N7,000,000) is within the monetary jurisdiction of the Magistrates' Court in Lagos State, which is N10,000,000.

quizzes.scenario:
ELECTION PETITION

Indaboski Bahosa contested the recently held election to the Delta West Senatorial District under the platform of Nigerians Progression Party and lost. The election was characterised by widespread malpractice. He wants to challenge the outcome of the election.

Before which of the under listed Electoral tribunals would you file the petition?
options: National Assembly Election Tribunal
Legislative Houses Election Tribunal
National and State Houses of Assembly Election Petition Tribunal
Government and National Assembly Election Tribunal
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 285(1) of the 1999 Constitution, the tribunal with jurisdiction over National Assembly (Senate and House of Representatives) and State House of Assembly elections is the National and State Houses of Assembly Election Petition Tribunal.

quizzes.scenario:
Indaboski Bahosa Senate election petition. Tribunal composition:

The appropriate composition of the Tribunal you have identified above is:
options: The President and two other members
The Chairman and two other members
The Chairman and one member
The President and one member
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The National and State Houses of Assembly Election Petition Tribunal is composed of a Chairman and two other members. Sixth Schedule, 1999 CFRN.

quizzes.scenario:
Indaboski Bahosa Senate election petition. Filing timeframe:

The period of time within which you are to file the petition is:
options: Within 14 days from the day of declaration of result
Within 7 days after the day of declaration of result
Within 21 days from the day of declaration of result / 21 days after the declaration
Within 21 days after the day of declaration of result
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An election petition must be filed within 21 days after the date of the declaration of the election result. Sec. 285(5), CFRN.

quizzes.scenario:
Indaboski Bahosa Senate election petition. Judgment timeframe:

The judgment of the Tribunal must be delivered within what time frame?
options: 180 days of declaration of election result
90 days of declaration of election result
120 days of filing election petition
180 days of filing election petition
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An election tribunal shall deliver its judgment in writing within 180 days from the date of filing the petition. Sec. 285(6), CFRN.

quizzes.scenario:
MATRIMONIAL PROCEEDINGS

Otunba J. F. Banjo and Miss Wuraola Jakande met at their NYSC camp and had been cohabiting together for more than 10 years, until 2015 when Otunba decided to marry one Sisi Alawiye at the Marriage Registry, Ibadan.

Only one of the following is true of the marriage between Otunba J. F. Banjo and Sisi Alawiye:
options: The marriage is invalid because of the existing cohabitation between Otunba and Miss Wuraola Jakande
The marriage is void as it was not celebrated in a licensed place of worship
The marriage is voidable at the option of Miss Wuraola
The marriage is valid
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The marriage is valid. Cohabitation, even for 10 years, does not create a statutory marriage in Nigeria. Therefore, Otunba is single and free to contract a valid statutory marriage with Sisi Alawiye. (Option D)

quizzes.scenario:
Otunba J. F. Banjo v Sisi Alawiye. Paternity issue:

Assuming the marriage was celebrated in a licensed place of worship and all other formalities were complied with. But two months after the wedding, Otunba J. F. Banjo discovered Sisi Alawiye was already 4 months pregnant, and she confessed that it was for her ex-boyfriend, Fiwajomi. The marriage from this fact is best described as:
options: Illegal marriage
Voidable marriage
Void marriage
Special status marriage
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 5(1)(d) of the Matrimonial Causes Act (MCA), a marriage is voidable if, at the time of the marriage, the respondent is pregnant by a person other than the petitioner.

quizzes.scenario:
Otunba J. F. Banjo v Sisi Alawiye. Remedy:

If Otunba J. F. Banjo intends to file a petition against Sisi Alawiye regarding the pregnancy, the most probable matrimonial relief he will be seeking is:
options: Dissolution of marriage
Nullity of a void marriage
Declaration of the marriage to be illegal
Nullity of a voidable marriage
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Since the marriage is voidable, the appropriate decree to seek is a decree of nullity of a voidable marriage.

quizzes.scenario:
FUNDAMENTAL RIGHTS ENFORCEMENT

Dare Dude while traveling from Ibadan to Lagos in a commercial car was stopped by the Special Anti Robbery Squad (SARS) and alleged to be a fraudster as he was operating on his PC. His attempt to explain was met with a resounding round of slaps. He was taken into custody and has been in detention for more than 10 days without any access to his family members. His sister wants to bring an application for the enforcement of his fundamental rights.

The appropriate court with jurisdiction here is:
options: National Industrial Court
Oyo State High Court / High Court of Lagos State
Federal High Court / State High Court concurrently
Supreme Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In fundamental rights enforcements, both the Federal High Court and the States High Courts have concurrent jurisdiction. FUTMINNA v Olutayo.

quizzes.scenario:
Dare Dude fundamental rights enforcement action:

The action can be commenced through:
options: Writ of summons
Petition
Originating summons / Originating motion
Any of the above
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Fundamental Rights (Enforcement Procedure) Rules 2009, an application for the enforcement of fundamental rights may be commenced by any originating process, but in practice, an originating motion or originating summons is used.

quizzes.scenario:
Dare Dude fundamental rights enforcement action:

The following except one are documents to accompany the originating process (originating motion):
options: Statement of facts setting out the names, description, grounds and reliefs
Affidavits in support
Acknowledgement of petition
Written address in support of the application
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The required accompanying documents under the FREP Rules 2009 are: (1) Statement setting out the name/description, grounds, and reliefs; (2) Affidavit in support; and (3) Written address. Acknowledgement of petition is not required.
